/TDAG/RCSA_DOCGA.desc: Assigns documents to document groups for REACH compliance in SAP EHS Management.

/TDAG/RCSA_EPCHA.desc: Defines endpoint chapters for REACH compliance in SAP EHS Management.

/TDAG/RCSA_EPDOC.desc: Stores endpoint guidance documents for REACH compliance in SAP EHS Management.

/TDAG/RCSA_EPREC.desc: Stores endpoint data records for REACH compliance in SAP EHS Management.

/TDAG/RCSA_IUCSU.desc: Substance data used to synchronize with IUCLID for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_LEOA.desc: An obsolete table that stored letter of access records for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_LGENT.desc: Defines legal entities for REACH compliance in SAP EHS Management.

/TDAG/RCSA_LITER.desc: An obsolete table that stored literature references for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_LOA.desc: Stores letters of access for REACH compliance in SAP EHS Management.

/TDAG/RCSA_MILES.desc: Stores project milestones for REACH compliance in SAP EHS Management.

/TDAG/RCSA_OLHDR.desc: Header data for object lists used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_OLHDT.desc: Language-dependent text describing object list headers used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_OLPOS.desc: Item-level data for object lists used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_STDEP.desc: An obsolete table that assigned studies to endpoints for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_STUDY.desc: An obsolete table that stored study records for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSA_TSTPR.desc: An obsolete table that stored testing proposals for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_ALTYP.desc: An obsolete table that defined alternative types for REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_ALTYP.lore: An obsolete table — SAP's own description says so — that used to define alternative types for REACH compliance. Still referenced by three other tables, so it hasn't been fully cleaned up even though it's retired.

/TDAG/RCSC_ALTYT.desc: An obsolete text table for the alternative types used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_CMPN.desc: Names of campaign types used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_CMPT.desc: Defines campaign types used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_DOCGA.desc: Assigns documents to document groups in REACH compliance customizing.

/TDAG/RCSC_DOCGN.desc: Text descriptions for document groups used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_DOCGP.desc: Defines document groups used in REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_DOCGP.lore: Document groups for REACH compliance — the grouping layer over CfP documents. It's referenced by /TDAG/RCSC_EMLC, tying document grouping into how compliance emails get configured downstream.

/TDAG/RCSC_DOKAR.desc: Defines document types relevant to REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_EMLA.desc: Assigns partner roles to email configurations in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_EMLC.desc: Defines communication settings used in REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_EMLC.lore: The communication settings hub for REACH compliance — it ties together document groups, email groups, and templates (three separate points_to) under one configuration id. A central table for how REACH correspondence goes out.

/TDAG/RCSC_EMLCN.desc: Text descriptions for the communication definitions used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_EMLGA.desc: Assigns emails to email groups in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_EMLGN.desc: Text descriptions for email groups used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_EMLGP.desc: Defines email groups used in REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_EMLGP.lore: Email groups for REACH compliance — who receives which category of compliance correspondence. /TDAG/RCSC_EMLC references it directly when assembling its communication settings.

/TDAG/RCSC_ENDPN.desc: Defines endpoints used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_ENDPT.desc: Text descriptions for the endpoints defined in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_ENV.desc: General environment parameters for SAP EHS Management REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_ENVNA.desc: Language-dependent descriptions for the environment parameters used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_INPCK.desc: Defines input check rules used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_INTPL.desc: Defines incoming templates used in REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_INTPL.lore: Incoming import templates (IMPTYP) for REACH compliance data — how the system reads a supplier's substance declaration file. Points out to five other tables (check reasons, declarations, document types, a viewer, and its own template table), so it's the entry point of an import pipeline rather than the data itself.

/TDAG/RCSC_IUCIN.desc: Defines IUCLID instances connected to REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_LEGSL.desc: Defines legislation records used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_LEGST.desc: Text descriptions for the legislation records used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_MILED.desc: Defines project milestones used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_MILET.desc: Text descriptions for the project milestones defined in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_NROBJ.desc: Defines number ranges used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_OLGRA.desc: Assigns object list categories to object list groups in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_OLGRP.desc: Defines object list groups used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_OLGRT.desc: Text descriptions for the object list groups used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_ORGUN.desc: Defines organizational units used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_ORGUT.desc: Text descriptions for the organizational units used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_OUTLL.desc: Assigns languages and countries to outgoing templates in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_OUTPL.desc: Defines outgoing templates used in REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_OUTPL.lore: Outgoing REACH templates (EXPTYP) — the mirror of RCSC_INTPL, defining how compliance data gets formatted on the way out rather than the way in. Pulls in normalization rules and a viewer, so it's the export half of the import/export pipeline.

/TDAG/RCSC_PHMAP.desc: Maps phrases to technical phrase keys in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_PHTID.desc: Defines technical phrase keys used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_PNAV.desc: An obsolete table that stored portal navigation configuration for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_REGTY.desc: Defines registration type settings used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_RQMNT.desc: Defines legal requirements used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_SLORG.desc: Assigns organizational units to authorization groups in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_SROLE.desc: Defines stakeholder roles used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_SROLT.desc: Text descriptions for the stakeholder roles used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_SSREP.desc: Defines spreadsheet reports used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_SSRET.desc: Text descriptions for the spreadsheet reports used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_SSRTH.desc: Threshold and setting values for spreadsheet reports in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_TEMPL.desc: Defines templates used in REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_TEMPL.lore: REACH compliance templates, one row per TEMPLID. Referenced by both an inbound and an outbound processing table, so the same template list serves correspondence going in both directions.

/TDAG/RCSC_TEMPN.desc: Text descriptions for the templates defined in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_TOBND.desc: Defines tonnage bands used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_TOBNT.desc: Text descriptions for the tonnage bands used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_TPLG.desc: Defines template groups used in REACH compliance.
/TDAG/RCSC_TPLG.lore: Template groups (TPLGRP) for REACH compliance — a grouping layer over the individual import/export templates, referenced by requirement and email-category tables. Organizational config more than working data.

/TDAG/RCSC_TPLGA.desc: Assigns templates to template groups in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_TPLGN.desc: Text descriptions for the template groups used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_TPLL.desc: Assigns languages and countries to templates in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSC_TPLN.desc: Text descriptions for the templates used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSL_BGR.desc: Parameters controlling background processing of import/export reports in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSW_EPCHD.desc: Defines endpoint chapters used in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSW_EPCHT.desc: Text descriptions for the endpoint chapters defined in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSW_MSG.desc: Stores system messages for REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSW_PAWM.desc: Check table linking phrase sets to characteristics for populating table TCG66 in REACH compliance.

/TDAG/RCSW_PHSET.desc: Defines phrase sets used in REACH compliance.

A133.desc: Condition records for domestic mixed tax determination in Materials Management.
A133.lore: A condition access table for mixed tax determination in MM, keyed on country, plant and material. The condition type comes from T685, the material from MARA, and DATBI makes validity part of the key rather than an attribute.

A144.desc: Basic data for sales deals in SD billing and pricing.
A144.lore: A pricing condition table for sales deals: application, condition type and sales organisation narrowed down through three product hierarchy levels to a material, with a validity end date closing the key. T685 defines the condition type, TVKO the sales org.

A145.desc: Sales deal condition records keyed by customer and material in SD billing.
A145.lore: Sales deal condition records cut by customer and material — one of the numbered A-tables that hold access-specific pricing keys. Condition type resolves through T685, the customer through KNA1, the material through MARA, all bounded by a validity end date.

A146.desc: Condition records for customer hierarchy-based pricing in SD billing.
A146.lore: One of the numbered pricing condition tables, this one keyed down the customer hierarchy — sales area, office, group, then payer, sold-to and ship-to. The condition type comes from T685; the partner keys resolve against KNA1. Long keys, narrow purpose.

A147.desc: Sales deal condition records keyed by customer hierarchy in SD billing.
A147.lore: One of the numbered condition tables behind SD billing, keyed right down the customer hierarchy plus the sales deal (KNUMA_AG) and a validity end date (DATBI). Condition type resolves through T685, the sales org levels through TVKO and TVTW.

A148.desc: Condition records for product hierarchy-based pricing in SD billing.
A148.lore: Pricing condition records for SD billing keyed on all three product hierarchy levels plus material, within a sales organisation and distribution channel. T685 defines what the condition type does; A148 only holds the records that match on hierarchy.

A149.desc: Condition records for customer-dependent data determination in SD billing.
A149.lore: Condition access for customer-dependent determination in billing, keyed on sales organization and distribution channel plus the payer, sold-to and ship-to trio. The condition type behind KSCHL is defined in T685.

A160.desc: Condition records for plant-specific purchasing info record variants.
A160.lore: Condition access for purchasing info record variants at plant level, keyed by vendor, purchasing organisation, plant, info record category, material and a variant condition on top. The usual supporting cast attaches: LFA1, T024E, T001W and T685.

A161.desc: Condition records for purchasing info record variants.
A161.lore: Purchasing condition records keyed right down to the variant condition, alongside vendor (LFA1), purchasing organization (T024E) and material. Same A-table shape as its sales-side equivalents: condition type from T685, validity date closing out the key.

A399.desc: Country-specific withholding tax code condition records in SD billing.
A399.lore: About as short as a condition table gets: application, condition type and country, valid to a date. Country resolves via T005, condition type via T685. Withholding tax rates that vary only by where you are, nothing else.

ACCTCR.desc: Currency amounts from compressed FI/CO documents, one row per document currency.
ACCTCR.lore: The currency side of compressed FI/CO documents: for each reference object (AWTYP/AWREF/AWORG) and line item, one row per currency type and currency key. An amounts-by-currency companion with no configuration of its own.

ACCTHD.desc: Header data from compressed FI/CO documents.
ACCTHD.lore: Header rows for compressed FI/CO documents, keyed solely by the originating object — reference type, reference number and reference organisation. It tells you where a compressed document came from rather than what it posted.

ACCTIT.desc: Line item data from compressed FI/CO documents.
ACCTIT.lore: Line items recovered from FI/CO documents that were compressed on the way in. Keyed by the originating object rather than company code and document number, which is what makes it useful when the summarized document no longer carries the detail.

AGKO.desc: Stores cleared account records in General Ledger accounting.
AGKO.lore: A cleared-items register in the general ledger, keyed by company code, document, fiscal year, account type and the account that was cleared. It records that clearing happened rather than the amounts; the document itself sits in BKPF.

AMPL.desc: Approved manufacturer parts list (AMPL) used in purchasing, one row per approved part.
AMPL.lore: The approved-manufacturer-parts list: which manufacturer part purchasing is allowed to buy against a given internal material. Ties the manufacturer from LFA1 to the internal material in MARA, optionally narrowed to a plant via T001W.

AMPL_REJECTION.desc: Records rejected approved manufacturer parts list (AMPL) entries.
AMPL_REJECTION.lore: The rejection side of the approved manufacturer parts list — manufacturer part plus AMPL number, recorded as not approved. A small negative list with no configuration around it; it exists to say no.

ANEA.desc: Fixed asset line items recording proportional, partial-period values, one row per asset, fiscal year, and line number.
ANEA.lore: Proportional value line items in Asset Accounting — the partial-period amounts for an asset, split by company code, asset and sub-number, fiscal year and line number, with the depreciation area (AFABE) sitting in the key as well.

ANEK.desc: Header data for asset posting documents, one row per company code, asset, fiscal year, and line number.
ANEK.lore: Header level of asset posting documents, one row per company code, asset main and sub-number, fiscal year and line number. Note that the asset, not the accounting document, drives the key here.

ANLP.desc: Periodic values for fixed assets, such as planned depreciation, one row per company code, asset, and fiscal year period.
ANLP.lore: Periodic fixed asset values such as planned depreciation, one row per company code, asset, fiscal year period and depreciation area. The periodic layer of asset accounting rather than the asset master itself.

ATAB.lore: A Basis-layer table carrying no description and no key fields in the catalogue. Nothing available here supports a claim about what it holds beyond its home in the technical stack.

AVIK.desc: Header data for payment advices in Financial Accounting.
AVIK.lore: The header half of a payment advice — company code, account type, account and advice ID. Regulation and version settings resolve through T053A and T053V, currency through TCURC; the detail lines sit underneath it rather than here.

AVIP.desc: Line item data for payment advices in Financial Accounting.
AVIP.lore: The item level of payment advices, keyed by company code, account type, account and advice number, then a running item. Reason codes come from T053R, currencies from TCURC, tax codes from T007A — the advice's detail lines rather than its header.

AVIR.desc: Subitem-level data for payment advices in Financial Accounting.
AVIR.lore: The subitem level of a payment advice: below the advice identified by account type, account and AVSID sit the individual advice positions. Company code resolves via T001, reason codes via T053R.

AVIT.desc: User-defined text linked to payment advice headers in Financial Accounting.
AVIT.lore: Free-form user text hung off payment advice headers, keyed by company code, account type, account, advice ID and text number. Commentary attached to the advice, not the advice's own data.

BFIT_A.desc: G/L account items for subsequent business area/profit center adjustment postings in Financial Accounting.
BFIT_A.lore: G/L items produced by the subsequent business area and profit center adjustment run. Keyed on the original document and line with an extra adjustment counter, so the correction sits beside the posting rather than inside it.

BFIT_A0.desc: Zero-balance postings for business area subsequent adjustment in Financial Accounting.

BFOD_A.desc: Customer line items for subsequent business area/profit center adjustment postings in Financial Accounting.
BFOD_A.lore: Customer line items produced by subsequent business area and profit center adjustment postings. Keyed to the original document (company code, document, year, line) with several further sequence numbers layered on for the adjustment itself.

BFOD_AB.desc: Valuation differences in open customer items for subsequent business area/profit center adjustment in Financial Accounting.

BFOK_A.desc: Vendor line items for subsequent business area/profit center adjustment postings in Financial Accounting.
BFOK_A.lore: Vendor-side line items created when business area or profit center postings are adjusted after the fact. Keyed like an ordinary document line but with extra sequence fields for the adjustment; vendor via LFA1, company code via T001.

BFOK_AB.desc: Valuation differences in open vendor items for subsequent business area/profit center adjustment in Financial Accounting.
BFOK_AB.lore: Valuation differences on open vendor items, kept so business area and profit center can be adjusted afterwards. The key runs down to line, open item and adjustment sequence within a document; T001 supplies the company code and TCURC the currency.

BFO_A_RA.desc: Stores versions used to reset cleared items in Financial Accounting.
BFO_A_RA.lore: Holds the versions needed to reset cleared items, keyed down to the clearing document, line and open item. An undo record: without it, putting items back to open would be guesswork.

BKDF.desc: Supplementary header data for recurring entry documents in Financial Accounting.
BKDF.lore: Supplementary header for recurring entry documents — the extra fields the ordinary document header has no room for, keyed by company code, document number and fiscal year. Hangs off T001 for the company code and T054 for the recurring entry control.

BKORM.desc: Stores requests for accounting correspondence in Financial Accounting.
BKORM.lore: The queue of correspondence requests in FI: who asked for what, against which document, and when. User, date and time sit in the key alongside the document, so it reads more like a work log than like master data.

BKORR.desc: An additional table of accounting correspondence requests in Financial Accounting.
BKORR.lore: Correspondence requests in accounting, keyed by event, company code, account type and account before the document. Described as an additional table, so it sits alongside the main correspondence request storage rather than replacing it.

BP001.desc: Financial Services industry-specific organizational attributes for a business partner, one row per partner.
BP001.lore: Financial Services-specific organizational attributes hung off a business partner, one row per client and partner. An industry extension of the partner record rather than the partner master itself.

BPBK.desc: Stores document header data for controlling objects in Funds Management.
BPBK.lore: The header for a Funds Management posting document — same header-then-lines pattern as BKPF and BSEG, just scoped to FM controlling objects. BPEG, BPEJ, and BPEP hang off it as the line-item detail.

BPEG.desc: Stores overall line item values for controlling objects in Funds Management.
BPEG.lore: Funds Management line item values for controlling objects, keyed by document number and line. BPBK holds the matching document header via BELNR, making BPEG the detail half of that pair; TWAER resolves through TCURC.

BPEJ.desc: Stores annual line item values for controlling objects in Funds Management.
BPEJ.lore: Annual line item values for controlling objects in Funds Management, keyed by nothing more than document number and line. BPBK supplies the document header via BELNR; TCURC validates the currency.

BPEP.desc: Stores period line item values for controlling objects in Funds Management.
BPEP.lore: Period-sliced values for controlling objects in Funds Management: one row per document, line item and period block. BPBK holds the document header it belongs to, TCURC the transaction currency — this is the period breakdown, not the posting itself.

BPGE.desc: Stores total value totals records for controlling objects in Funds Management.
BPGE.lore: Funds Management overall-value totals per object and commitment item, with fund, version, value type and transaction currency in the key. TCURC supplies the currency; everything else in the key is FM's own coding block.

BPID001.desc: Additional identification numbers assigned to a business partner, one row per partner, ID type, and ID number.
BPID001.lore: Identification numbers hung off a business partner — one row per partner, ID type and ID number. Pure extension data: the partner exists elsewhere, this just carries the extra numbers somebody needs to quote.

BPIJ.desc: Index of annual budget objects in Funds Management.
BPIJ.lore: An index over annual budget objects in Funds Management, keyed by CO object, POSIT, value type, fiscal year and fund. A lookup layer rather than the budget values themselves; currency validates against TCURC.

BPJA.desc: Stores annual totals records for controlling objects in Funds Management.
BPJA.lore: Annual totals for controlling objects in Funds Management — one row per ledger, object, item, value type, year, fund, version and more. The key is long because the totals are sliced that finely; TWAER points at TCURC.

BPPE.desc: Stores period totals records for controlling objects in Funds Management.
BPPE.lore: Period totals for controlling objects in Funds Management. The wide key covers ledger, object, commitment item, fund, value type, version and period block — every reporting dimension is part of the key rather than an attribute.

BSBW.desc: Stores valuation field data for accounting documents in Financial Accounting.
BSBW.lore: Valuation data attached to accounting document line items, adding currency type, valuation area and method on top of the usual company code, document, year and item key. No declared relationships: a side-table hung off the document rather than part of it.

BSIA.desc: An outdated archive index for FI documents, superseded by ARCH_IDX.
BSIA.lore: A legacy archive index for FI documents, keyed by nothing more than company code, document and year. Its description marks it as outdated and superseded by a newer archive index — present in the dictionary, not somewhere to look.

BSIM.desc: Secondary index of accounting documents by material in Inventory Management/Invoice Verification.
BSIM.lore: A secondary index that lets you get from a material to its accounting documents, keyed by material and valuation area before the document number and item. MARA supplies the material, T003 the document type. Read path only — the postings themselves live elsewhere.

BSIP.desc: Index used to validate vendors for duplicate document detection in Financial Accounting.
BSIP.lore: The duplicate-detection index on the vendor side: company code, vendor, currency, document date, reference and amount, with the document number trailing behind. Exists so an incoming document can be matched against what is already posted.

BSIW.desc: Index of customer bills of exchange used in Accounts Receivable.
BSIW.lore: Index over customer bills of exchange in Accounts Receivable, keyed by company code, G/L account and assignment down to the document line it points at. An access path into the documents rather than a store of bill data; KNB1 holds the company code customer view.

BSIX.desc: An additional index table for customer bills of exchange used in Accounts Receivable.
BSIX.lore: A secondary index over customer bills of exchange, keyed by company code, G/L account and assignment before the document number. Built for finding items by account and assignment rather than by document.

BUT000_TD.desc: Time-dependent shadow copy of general business partner master data, one row per partner and validity end date.
BUT000_TD.lore: A time-sliced shadow of general business partner master data — one row per partner and validity end date, so history accumulates by versioning rather than overwriting. It mirrors the partner record rather than extending it.

BUTADRSEARCH.desc: Search-help data for locating business partners by address, one row per partner and address number.
BUTADRSEARCH.lore: A search helper for business partners: one row per partner and address number, existing so that address-based lookups resolve. Pure index infrastructure, with no relationships modelled out to anything else.

BVOR.desc: Defines intercompany posting procedures in Financial Accounting.
BVOR.lore: Ties the documents of an intercompany posting together under a cross-company transaction number, one row per participating company code and document. The link table that makes two local documents one business event.

CATSCO.desc: Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS) records transferred to Controlling (CO).

CATSDB.desc: Main database table for Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS) entries.

CATSHR.desc: Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS) records transferred to Human Resources (HR).

CATSMM.desc: Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS) records transferred to Materials Management (MM).

CATSPM.desc: Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS) records transferred to Plant Maintenance (PM) and Customer Service (CS).

CATSPS.desc: Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS) records transferred to Project System (PS).

CCIHC_QUALPROT.desc: Defines quality-of-protection settings in SAP EHS Management.

CCIHC_QUALPROTXT.desc: Language-dependent descriptions for the quality-of-protection settings in SAP EHS Management.

CCIHT_IHVA.desc: Value Assignment Data Records for Classific. System.

CCIHT_IPEVA.desc: Inc./Acc. Log - Data for Person Affected (Event).

CCIHT_IPVA.desc: Obsolete -IAL, Person Aff. - Addit. Info. (Class. Sys.).

CCIHT_WAAD.desc: Language- Dependent Description of Accident Location.

CCIHT_WADO.desc: Work Area - Links to Info Objects.

CCIHT_WALD.desc: Language- Dependent Name and Descr. of Work Area.

CDCLS.lore: No description carried in the source, and no key fields or relationships recorded either — all that is known is that it belongs to the Basis layer rather than to any application module.

CHVW_IDX.desc: Index table used to select data objects in a batch where-used search in Inventory Management.
CHVW_IDX.lore: A working index for batch where-used searches — a single object ID column whose only job is to hold the set of data objects a search has selected. Scaffolding for the run, not inventory data.

CKLMLCR.lore: A Material Ledger table sitting between CO and FI. No description and no key fields are recorded for it here, so there is nothing to say beyond its name and where it lives.

CKMI1.desc: Index linking accounting documents to a material's costing record, one row per costing number, fiscal year, and period.
CKMI1.lore: An index running from accounting documents back to a material's costing record, keyed by costing number, fiscal year and period plus the reference object type, number and organisation. Pure lookup: you traverse it, you don't report from it.

CKMLCR.desc: Material Ledger period totals for values, one row per costing number, fiscal year, period, and currency type.
CKMLCR.lore: Material Ledger period totals on the value side — one row per costing number, fiscal year, period and currency type. The currency type in the key is what lets the same period carry several parallel valuations.

CKMLCRWIP.desc: Material Ledger period totals for work-in-process values, one row per costing number, fiscal year, period, and currency type.
CKMLCRWIP.lore: Material Ledger period totals for work in process, keyed by costing number, year, period and currency type. Aggregated WIP values per costing object per period — the totals layer of the ledger rather than any document behind them.

CKMLKEPH.desc: Material Ledger cost component split by element, one row per costing number, period, cost element, and currency type.
CKMLKEPH.lore: Material Ledger cost component split at element level: for a costing number and period, one row per cost element, category, valuation type and currency type. The itemised side of a costing result rather than a single total.

CKMLLACRWIP.desc: Material Ledger activity type period records for work-in-process values, one row per costing number, period, and currency type.
CKMLLACRWIP.lore: Material Ledger period records for activity types carrying work-in-process values, one row per costing number, period and currency type. The costing number KALNR is the anchor rather than the material.

CKMLLAPPWIP.desc: Material Ledger activity type period records for work-in-process quantities, one row per costing number and period.
CKMLLAPPWIP.lore: Activity type period records carrying work-in-process quantities in the Material Ledger, keyed by costing number and period with a sub-period field for finer breakdowns. Quantities only, on the activity side.

CKMLMV003.desc: Material Ledger process model data for goods receipts and mixing strategies, one row per movement group, fiscal year, and period.
CKMLMV003.lore: Material Ledger process model data for goods receipts and mixing, keyed by movement group, fiscal year and period, with separate costing numbers for the input, balance and output sides. Structural plumbing behind the ledger's cost flows.

CKMLMV004.desc: Material Ledger process model data for material withdrawals, one row per plant, movement group, fiscal year, and period.
CKMLMV004.lore: Withdrawal data for the Material Ledger's process model, keyed by plant, movement group and period, with separate costing numbers for the input material and the process. Working data for the model rather than a reportable result.

CKMLMV004WIP.desc: Material Ledger process model data for work-in-process deductions, one row per plant, movement group, and fiscal year.
CKMLMV004WIP.lore: Process model data for WIP deductions in the Material Ledger, keyed by movement type group, year, period and plant, with separate costing numbers for input material and process. Run-time bookkeeping for the costing model.

CKMLMV011.desc: List of costing objects included in a Material Ledger costing run, one row per run ID and costing number.
CKMLMV011.lore: The worklist of a Material Ledger costing run — which costing objects (KALNR) were pulled into which run (LAUFID). No values and no results, just membership.

CKMLPP.desc: Material Ledger period totals for quantities, one row per costing number, fiscal year, and period.
CKMLPP.lore: The quantity side of Material Ledger period totals, keyed by costing number, fiscal year and period. Where the value records track money, this one keeps the quantities that moved through the period.

CKMLPPWIP.desc: Material Ledger period records for work-in-process quantities, one row per costing number, fiscal year, and period.
CKMLPPWIP.lore: Material Ledger period records for work-in-process quantities, one row per costing number, fiscal year and period. Keyed on the costing object rather than the material number, which is the Material Ledger's usual habit.

CKMLPRKEKO.desc: Header data for a Material Ledger cost component split, one row per costing number, period, and price type.
CKMLPRKEKO.lore: Header of a Material Ledger cost component split: one row per costing number, period (down to sub-period), price type and currency type. The key identifies which split is meant; the individual cost components are not carried here.

CKMLPRKEPH.desc: Material Ledger cost component split elements, one row per costing number, period, cost element, and price type.
CKMLPRKEPH.lore: The cost component split behind Material Ledger values — one row per costing number, period, cost component, price type and currency type. Where a single figure is decomposed into the elements that make it up.

COSP_BAK.lore: No description and no key fields recorded, and the name reads as a backup companion to CO cost totals. Nothing here can be stated with confidence beyond its CO home.

COSS_BAK.lore: No description, key fields or relationships are recorded for this one. It sits in Controlling, and the input offers nothing further to characterise it with.

COVP.lore: Keyed by controlling area, document number and line item — the standard shape of a CO document line. The catalogue carries no description, so read it as CO document line-level data and check the key before assuming anything more.

CRACC.desc: Account assignment data in the payment card master record for Accounts Payable.
CRACC.lore: The account assignment half of the payment card master, keyed by card institution and card number. It carries the defaults a card's postings pick up — company code, G/L account, controlling area, tax jurisdiction, vendor — with a validity date.

CRNUM.desc: Master record data for payment cards in Accounts Payable.
CRNUM.lore: Payment card master records, keyed by card type and card number alone. The thin key belies the breadth of accounting context reachable from it — vendor, G/L account, company code, document type, tax code, currency, controlling area and tax jurisdiction.

CVCBP.desc: Defines valid business process IDs in SAP EHS Management.

DBTABLOG.desc: Log entries recording changes made to database tables, one row per log date, time, and log ID.
DBTABLOG.lore: The table change log: one row per logged change, keyed only by date, time and log ID. Detached from any business object — it records that something logged moved, not what business meaning the movement had.

DBVM.desc: MRP planning file entries flagging materials that need requirements planning, one row per MRP area and material.
DBVM.lore: MRP planning file entries: which materials need planning, flagged per MRP area and low-level code. Keyed by planning file indicator, low-level code, material and MRP area — a worklist for the planning run, not a result of it.

DD06L.desc: ABAP Dictionary metadata describing pool and cluster table structures, one row per SQL table name and version.
DD06L.lore: ABAP Dictionary metadata describing pool and cluster structures, keyed by SQL table name plus AS4LOCAL and AS4VERS. Repository housekeeping about how tables are stored, not application data.

DD06T.desc: Language-dependent text descriptions of SQL tables from the ABAP Dictionary, one row per table, language, and version.
DD06T.lore: Language-dependent descriptions for Dictionary tables, one row per table, language and version. Pure ABAP Dictionary metadata: it describes tables rather than holding business data of its own.

DD16S.desc: ABAP Dictionary metadata for SQL table fields, one row per table, field name, and version.
DD16S.lore: ABAP Dictionary metadata describing the fields of SQL tables, versioned through AS4LOCAL and AS4VERS. Basis plumbing: it describes tables rather than holding anything a business user would recognise.

DGMS2.desc: Appendix table for dangerous goods documents in SAP EHS Management.

DGMSD.desc: Stores dangerous goods document records within the DG (dangerous goods) component of SAP EHS Management.

DGTEXCCNTRIES.desc: Defines country-specific exceptions to dangerous goods regulations in SAP EHS Management.

DGTEXCEPT.desc: Header table for exceptions to dangerous goods regulations in SAP EHS Management.
DGTEXCEPT.lore: Header record for a dangerous goods regulation exception — a case where a shipment is exempt from a rule it would otherwise trigger, versioned so exceptions can change over time. Country lists, texts, and UN numbers for the exception hang off it in DGTEXCCNTRIES, DGTEXCTEXTS, and DGTEXCUNNO.

DGTEXCTEXTS.desc: Texts and documents linked to dangerous goods regulation exceptions in SAP EHS Management.

DGTEXCUNNO.desc: UN numbers linked to dangerous goods regulation exceptions in SAP EHS Management.

DGTMD_LKEY.desc: A dummy table providing a lock object on the logical key for table DGTMD.

DGTPK.desc: Defines packing requirements for dangerous goods in SAP EHS Management.

DOKCLU.lore: A Basis cluster table with no description and no key fields recorded in the catalogue. Beyond its place in the technical layer, there is nothing here to characterise its contents honestly.

DOKTL.desc: Text lines making up SAP documentation content, one row per document, object, language, type, version, and line.
DOKTL.lore: The line-by-line text of SAP documentation objects, keyed by document ID, object, language, type, version and line number. Content storage for help texts, which is why nothing business-shaped points at it.

EBAN_TECH.desc: Purchase requisition data used for external sourcing in Purchasing.
EBAN_TECH.lore: Technical companion data for purchase requisition items used in external sourcing, keyed exactly like a requisition item on requisition number and item. Sits beside the requisition rather than carrying business content of its own.

EBEW.desc: Valuation data for stock tied to a sales order, one row per material, valuation area, sales order, and item.
EBEW.lore: Valuation of sales-order stock: material and valuation area as usual, but with the special stock indicator, sales document and item in the key, so each order's stock carries its own value.

EBEWH.desc: Historical valuation data for sales-order stock, one row per material, sales order, item, fiscal year, and period.
EBEWH.lore: Historical valuation of sales-order stock, keyed by material, valuation area and type, special stock indicator, sales order item, fiscal year and period. The period-by-period record of what that segregated stock was worth.

EBKN.desc: Account assignment data for purchase requisition items in Purchasing.
EBKN.lore: Account assignment lines for purchase requisition items — the cost center, order, WBS element, asset or G/L account a requisition line charges, one row per ZEBKN counter. EBAN holds the requisition item itself.

EBKPF.desc: Financial accounting document header data for documents received from external systems.
EBKPF.lore: Document header for financial accounting documents that arrived from external systems, keyed by an external ledger and system pairing alongside document number and year. It leans on T001, T003 and TCURC exactly as a natively created header would.

EBSEG.desc: Document segment data for financial accounting documents from external systems.
EBSEG.lore: Line-item segments for FI documents originating in external systems, keyed by the external book, document and year. Its related tables read like a full coding block: G/L account, asset, order, material, plant, customer and country.

EBUB.desc: Index of stock transport requisitions by material in Purchasing.
EBUB.lore: An index onto stock transport requisitions from the material side, keyed by material and supplying plant before the requisition and item. EBAN holds the requisition, T001W the plant; this is the lookup path.

EHFNDD_LISU_RT.desc: Destination for listed substances transferred out of EHFNDD_LISU_ROOT during conversion.

EHQMT_BASIC_DATA.desc: Basic data for inspection plans in the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_CHARTYPE.desc: Defines characteristic types in the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_CHARTYPET.desc: Text descriptions for the characteristic types in the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_CODE.desc: Assigns phrases to codes in the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_CODE2.desc: Assigns characteristic values to codes in the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_GRP.desc: Assigns phrase groups to code groups in the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_IP_MEMORY.desc: Records inspection plans created via the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_MAIN1.desc: Substance value assignment types used to create inspection plans in the EHS-QM integration.

EHQMT_MAIN2.desc: Characteristics used to create inspection plans in the EHS-QM integration.

EHSBT_APPL_SCOPE.desc: Defines general scope-of-application settings in SAP EHS Management.

EHSWAT000.desc: MD (BDT): Master Table for the Waste Management Master Data.

EHS_DGTM2_APPEND_03.desc: Structure append adding fields to the dangerous goods table for enhancement package 3 (EhP3).

EHS_DGTM2_APPEND_05.desc: Structure append adding fields to the dangerous goods master table for enhancement package 5 (EhP5).

EHS_TDG83.desc: Defines packaging categories in SAP EHS Management for dangerous goods.

EIPA.desc: Order price history linked to purchasing info records.
EIPA.lore: Order price history attached to a purchasing info record — what was paid on which purchase order item. Info record via EINA, the order via EKKO and EKPO, plant via T001W, purchasing organization via T024E.

EKAB.desc: Documents release actions for purchasing documents.
EKAB.lore: Release documentation for purchasing documents — which contract item (KONNR and KTPNR, reaching EKKO and EKPO) a given purchase order item released against. The link between an outline agreement and the orders drawing on it.

EKAN.desc: Vendor address data for purchasing documents.
EKAN.lore: Vendor address data recorded on a purchasing document, keyed by EBELN alone. It hangs off the EKKO header as a document-level address, with the country checked against T005.

EKBEH.desc: Stores purchase order history records that have been removed.
EKBEH.lore: Purchase order history entries that have been removed from the live history, keyed identically to the history record itself right down to the reference document and item. A holding area, still wired to MARA, T001W, T156, T163B and TCURC.

EKBE_MA.desc: Purchasing document history at the account assignment level.
EKBE_MA.lore: Purchasing document history broken out per account assignment — the account assignment number in the key is what separates it from history kept at plain item level. Each row points on to a follow-on document, year and line.

EKBE_SC.desc: Component consumption history for subcontracting purchase orders.
EKBE_SC.lore: Purchase order history for subcontracting components — which components were consumed against which PO item, keyed by order, item, document year and line. EKKO and EKPO give the order, MARA the component, T001W the plant.

EKBZH.desc: Delivery cost history per purchasing document.
EKBZH.lore: Delivery cost history for a purchasing document, keyed down to the condition step and counter as well as the follow-on accounting document. The delivery-cost strand of purchase order history; header and item via EKKO and EKPO.

EKBZ_MA.desc: Delivery cost data at the account assignment level for purchasing documents.
EKBZ_MA.lore: Delivery costs broken out to account assignment level, keyed by purchase order item, condition step and counter, transaction event, the follow-on document line, and the account assignment sequence ZEKKN.

EKCC.desc: Index table supporting the CCP process in Purchasing.
EKCC.lore: An index for the CCP process in Purchasing, pairing a schedule line with its counterpart through a mirrored EBELN/EBELP/ETENR key. Pure navigation — the substance stays in EKKO, EKPO and EKET.

EKCPA.desc: Account assignment data for purchasing contract commitment plans.
EKCPA.lore: Account assignment for commitment plan items on purchasing contracts — contract document, item, commitment plan item and account assignment sequence number. A narrow extension of the contract item, with no declared neighbours to lean on.

EKEH.desc: Documents release actions for scheduling agreements.
EKEH.lore: Release documentation for scheduling agreements: which release, of which type, covered which schedule line of which agreement item. The record of what was actually called off, kept separately from the agreement itself.

EKEK.desc: Header-level data for releases against scheduling agreements.
EKEK.lore: The header of a scheduling agreement release: order, item, release type and release number. One row per release issued against a schedule line set, with the detail carried elsewhere.

EKETH.desc: History of scheduling agreement delivery schedules.
EKETH.lore: Historical delivery schedule lines for scheduling agreements, keyed by document, item and schedule line number. Requisition link via EBAN, header and item via EKKO and EKPO.

EKKI.lore: An index over purchasing pricing conditions, keyed by condition category, vendor and supplying plant down to the purchasing document item and condition record number KNUMV. Access path only — the condition values live elsewhere.

EKKN.desc: Account assignment data for purchasing document items.
EKKN.lore: Account assignment for purchasing document items: the cost center, order, WBS element or controlling area a PO line charges, one row per ZEKKN. EKPO holds the item, EKKO the header, this holds who pays.

EKPB.desc: Customer- or vendor-provided material items in purchasing documents.
EKPB.lore: Material provided by the customer or vendor on a purchasing document: components that appear on the order without being bought, keyed under document and item. It sits beneath EKKO and EKPO and reaches out to MARA, T001W and T001L.

EKPV.desc: Stock-transfer shipping details at purchasing document item level.
EKPV.lore: Shipping detail for a stock transfer at purchase order item level — sales organization, distribution channel, customer and forwarding agent hung off an EKKO/EKPO item. The point where a purchasing document starts behaving like a delivery.

EKRS.desc: Goods movements pending invoicing under the Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS) procedure.
EKRS.lore: Goods movements that have been received but not yet settled under Evaluated Receipt Settlement, keyed by posting date, vendor and material document. A queue: entries sit here until the settlement run turns them into invoices.

EKRSDC.desc: Planned delivery costs pending invoicing under the Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS) procedure.
EKRSDC.lore: Planned delivery costs that Evaluated Receipt Settlement has not yet turned into an invoice, keyed by accounting document, line, condition step and counter, and condition type. Effectively a pending-work list for ERS.

EKUB.desc: Index of stock transport orders by material in Purchasing.
EKUB.lore: Material-driven index of stock transport orders: given a material and a supplying plant from T001W, find the purchase order items in EKKO and EKPO. Purely a secondary access path into purchasing.

ELBK.lore: Header for a vendor evaluation, one row per purchasing organization and vendor — evaluation is scoped to the org, not the company. LFA1 supplies the vendor, T024E the purchasing organization, T147J the weighting key.

ELBM.desc: Material-related item data for vendor evaluations.
ELBM.lore: Material-level detail behind a vendor evaluation, keyed by purchasing organisation, info record and main criterion. It joins the evaluation criteria in T147G to a concrete info record in EINA, and from there through to vendor and material.

ELBP.desc: Defines the main criteria used for vendor evaluation.
ELBP.lore: The main criteria of a vendor evaluation, one row per purchasing organization, vendor and main criterion. The criterion catalogue lives in T147G; this is where an evaluation is actually anchored to a specific vendor.

EQUK.desc: Header data for quota arrangement files in Purchasing.
EQUK.lore: Header for a quota arrangement — material, plant, valid-to date. Establishes that a quota exists for that material at that plant over a period; MARA and T001W supply the two key objects, the split percentages live in the item level.

EQUP.desc: Item data for quota arrangement files in Purchasing.
EQUP.lore: The item level of a quota arrangement in Purchasing, keyed by quota number and position. Vendor via LFA1 and material via MARA; the header carries the arrangement, this carries the individual entries.

EREV.desc: Stores document version data in Purchasing.
EREV.lore: Version data for purchasing documents, keyed by document category, document, item and version number. It records that a document was revised rather than what the revision said.

EST0B.desc: Dangerous Goods Management - Packaging Code Approval.

ESTDH.desc: Report Header Environment, Health and Safety - Product Safety.

ESTRR.desc: Reference Environment, Health and Safety - Product Safety.

EVAL_IV_RES_HD.desc: Header data for vendor evaluation results.
EVAL_IV_RES_HD.lore: Header for vendor evaluation results, keyed by a GUID and criterion rather than by purchasing organization and vendor. Results are addressed by evaluation run, which makes them independent of the master data they scored.

EVAL_IV_RES_ITM.desc: Item-level data for vendor evaluation results.
EVAL_IV_RES_ITM.lore: Item rows of a vendor evaluation result, hung off an evaluation GUID with a line number and criterion. The GUID key means rows are addressed by evaluation object rather than by purchasing organisation, unlike the older criterion tables.

FAGLBSAS.desc: Secondary index of cleared general ledger account line items, one row per company code, account, and clearing document.
FAGLBSAS.lore: A secondary index over cleared G/L line items, keyed company code, account, clearing date and clearing document, with assignment and the document reference trailing. An access path rather than a source of truth.

FAGLBSIS.desc: Secondary index of general ledger account line items, one row per company code, account, and document number.
FAGLBSIS.lore: General ledger open item index by company code, account and document, with clearing date, clearing document and assignment in the key. A read path built for account line item selection rather than a source of postings.

FEBEP.desc: Line items from electronic bank statements in Financial Accounting.
FEBEP.lore: Line items of an electronic bank statement, keyed by the internal statement key KUKEY and the line number ESNUM. Bank country resolves via T005, statement currency via TCURC.

FEBKO.desc: Header records for electronic bank statements in Financial Accounting.
FEBKO.lore: Header records for electronic bank statements, keyed by application, sender, statement ID and an internal key. Company code, chart of accounts, G/L account and currency all resolve through the usual T001, T004, SKB1 and TCURC configuration.

FILCA.desc: Actual journal entries for FI Consolidation (FI-LC), keyed to two object tables.
FILCA.lore: Actual journal entries for FI Consolidation, keyed by client and the GL_SIRID record ID. Company code, business area, subgroup, movement type and currency are the reporting dimensions hanging off that single record number.

FILCP.desc: Planned line items for FI Consolidation (FI-LC) object tables.
FILCP.lore: Planned line items for FI Consolidation, addressed only by client and a summary record ID. The real dimensions — company code, subgroup, movement type, currency, business area — all arrive through T001, T852, T856, TCURC and TGSB rather than the key.

FILCT.desc: Global totals for FI Consolidation (FI-LC), keyed to two object tables.
FILCT.lore: Totals for the older FI Consolidation ledger, carrying the standard ledger/record-type/version/year key plus three object numbers. Company code, subgroup and transaction type resolve through T001, T852 and T856.

FITV_REJECT.desc: Stores rejected travel trips in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FITV_REJECT.lore: Trips that were rejected in Travel Management, keyed by personnel number, trip number and period. A small holding area for what did not get approved.

FMAVCT.desc: Availability control summary data for Funds Management's Budget Control System, one row per fiscal year, object, and currency.
FMAVCT.lore: Availability control summary data for the Budget Control System — the figures Funds Management checks against, held in the classic totals key shape of ledger, record type, version, year, objects and currency.

FMBDT.desc: Budget totals for Funds Management, one row per fiscal year, budget object, and currency.
FMBDT.lore: Budget totals in Funds Management, carrying the ledger, record type, version, year, object, currency and debit/credit key shape typical of totals tables. Totals only — the documents behind them live elsewhere.

FMFCTR.desc: Master record data for funds centers in Funds Management.
FMFCTR.lore: Funds center master data (FICTR) in Funds Management — the budget-holding unit within a financial management area (FIKRS), roughly Funds Management's answer to a cost center. Ties back to company code, country, currency, and business area, inheriting a lot of context from core FI.

FMGLFLEXA.desc: Actual general ledger line items, one row per fiscal year, document number, ledger, company code, and line item.
FMGLFLEXA.lore: Actual G/L line items in the flexible ledger key shape — fiscal year, document number, ledger, company code, line. Ledger-aware from the key up, which is the whole point of the flexible structures.

FMGLFLEXT.desc: Totals for public-sector general ledger accounts, one row per fiscal year and set of account assignment objects.
FMGLFLEXT.lore: Totals for public-sector general ledger accounting: one row per fiscal year against a long chain of account assignment object numbers, with a debit/credit indicator and a maximum period. Wide key, aggregated content — totals, not documents.

FMIFIIT.desc: Stores FI line items relevant to Funds Management.
FMIFIIT.lore: FI line items mirrored into Funds Management, keyed by the FM document number and financial management area rather than the FI document. The funds center resolves via FMFCTR, the company code via T001.

FMIOI.desc: Stores commitment documents in Funds Management.
FMIOI.lore: Commitment documents in Funds Management, keyed by a long reference chain — document, document type, organisational unit, item, schedule line, ledger and year. Fund centres come from FMFCTR, vendors from LFA1: it records that budget is spoken for, not yet spent.

FMIT.desc: Totals table for Funds Management balances.
FMIT.lore: The Funds Management totals table, carrying balances in the standard ledger, record type, version, year and object key shape. Company code via T001, currency via TCURC, business area via TGSB.

FMLC_ERD.desc: Holds exchange rate difference data in S/4HANA; SAP names it as the new table used where T169P was previously read.

FMRBT.lore: The catalogue gives no description; the key is the familiar Funds Management totals shape — ledger, record type, version, year, three object fields, currency, debit/credit indicator and period maximum. Read the key, not the name.

FMREF.desc: Cross-commitment reference table for active availability control in Funds Management.
FMREF.lore: Cross-references one commitment to another for active availability control, carrying a source object, commitment item and fund alongside a P_ prefixed parent set. It is how availability control knows what consumed what.

FMUSFGFACTS1A.desc: Individual actual-posting line items feeding the Funds Management US Federal Government facts data set.
FMUSFGFACTS1A.lore: Individual actual postings feeding the US Federal Government facts data set in Funds Management. Keyed only by client and a summary record ID, making it a collection point for a regulatory extract rather than something navigated by business key.

FMUSFGFACTS1T.desc: Aggregated actual and plan totals for the Funds Management US Federal Government facts data, keyed by ledger, version and fiscal year.
FMUSFGFACTS1T.lore: Aggregated actual and plan totals for US federal FACTS reporting in Funds Management, using the same ledger/record-type/version/year plus object-number key shape as the other summary tables. Purpose-built for one reporting regime.

FMUSFGFACTS2A.desc: Individual actual-posting line items feeding a second Funds Management US Federal Government facts data set.
FMUSFGFACTS2A.lore: Line-level actual postings feeding a second US Federal Government FACTS data set in Funds Management, keyed only by client and a single line item identifier. Thin key, reporting-oriented purpose.

FMUSFGFACTS2T.desc: Aggregated actual and plan totals for a second Funds Management US Federal Government facts data set, keyed by ledger, version and fiscal year.
FMUSFGFACTS2T.lore: Aggregated actual and plan totals for the second US Federal Government facts data set in Funds Management. Same ledger, version and fiscal year key shape as the other FM totals tables, dedicated to federal reporting.

FMZUOB.desc: Allocates Controlling (CO) objects to Funds Management account assignments.
FMZUOB.lore: Bridges Controlling to Funds Management: which CO object and cost element charge to which FM account assignment, per settlement period and year. CSKA validates the cost element, FMFCTR the funds center, TKA01 the controlling area.

FRUN.desc: Stores the run date of a program in Financial Accounting.
FRUN.lore: One row per program ID recording when it last ran. About as small as FI gets: no business data, just a date to compare against on the next run.

FTPT_FLIGHT_TSTK.desc: Stores flight ticket information in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FTPT_FLIGHT_TSTK.lore: Flight ticket data in Travel Management, keyed down through employee, trip, plan, variant and variant version to the individual ticket. FTPT_VARIANT holds the variant it belongs to; TCURC covers the fare currency.

FTPT_ITEM.desc: Stores travel service request item data in Travel Management (FI-TV).

FTPT_PLAN.desc: Stores general travel plan data in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FTPT_PLAN.lore: General travel plan data — personnel number, trip number and plan number. FTPT_VARIANT holds the plan's variants and PTRV_HEAD the trip itself, so this is the connective layer between a person's trip and its planned itinerary.

FTPT_REQUEST.desc: Stores travel plan requests in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FTPT_REQUEST.lore: The request object inside travel planning, keyed by personnel number, trip and plan plus a stack of version fields — plan, variant and request are each versioned separately. Items via FTPT_ITEM, header via FTPT_REQ_HEAD, variant via FTPT_VARIANT.

FTPT_REQ_ACCOUNT.desc: Cost assignment data for travel requests in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FTPT_REQ_ACCOUNT.lore: Cost assignment lines for travel requests, keyed by personnel number, trip number, request version and account line. FTPT_REQ_HEAD holds the request itself; this is the who-pays-for-it detail underneath it.

FTPT_REQ_HEAD.desc: Header and framework data for travel requests in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FTPT_REQ_HEAD.lore: The header for a travel request — keyed down to person (PERNR), trip (REINR), and even a request version, so multiple versions of the same request can coexist. It links to PTRV_HEAD, T005 for country, and TCURC for currency.

FTPT_REQ_REASON.desc: Reason codes for travel requests in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FTPT_REQ_REASON.lore: Reason lines attached to a travel request, keyed by employee, trip number, request version and line — so a request can carry several reasons. FTPT_REQ_HEAD holds the request itself.

FTPT_VARIANT.desc: Stores travel plan variants in Travel Management (FI-TV).
FTPT_VARIANT.lore: Alternate versions of a travel plan — same trip, different variant — so a traveler can compare options before booking. Keyed all the way down to PERNR, REINR, PLANNR and a variant number, and tied to FTPT_PLAN and TCURC for currency.

GLPCP.desc: Planned line items for Profit Center Accounting, one row per client and condition/summary record ID.
GLPCP.lore: Planned line items for Profit Center Accounting, addressed by client and a summary record ID. Same minimal-key pattern as the other plan line item tables: content is reached through the record ID, never through business dimensions.

GLPCT.desc: Totals table for Profit Center Accounting, one row per ledger, fiscal year, and account assignment object.
GLPCT.lore: The Profit Center Accounting totals table, wearing the standard ledger/record-type/version/year plus object-number key shared by the classic totals tables. Balances rolled up per account assignment object rather than per document.

GLT3.desc: Prepares summarized data for FI consolidation.
GLT3.lore: Summarized data prepared for FI consolidation, keyed by ledger, record type, version, company code, year and account, with movement type, trading partner and business area alongside. Movement types resolve via T856. A totals staging layer, not documents.

GSBLCA.desc: Records deviating adjustment entries for business area consolidation.
GSBLCA.lore: Holds the adjustment entries that deviate when business areas are consolidated, keyed like an ordinary document line: company code, document, year, item. Correction data rather than the original postings.

HRP1001.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1001 records, which capture relationships between objects.

HRP1002.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1002 records.

HRP1003.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1003 records.

HRP1004.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1004 records.

HRP1005.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1005 records for planned compensation.

HRP1006.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1006 records.

HRP1007.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1007 records.

HRP1008.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1008 records.

HRP1009.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1009 records.

HRP1010.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1010 records.

HRP1011.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1011 records.

HRP1013.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1013 records.

HRP1014.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1014 records.

HRP1015.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1015 records.

HRP1016.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1016 records.

HRP1018.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1018 records for cost distribution.

HRP1019.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1019 records for required positions.

HRP1020.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1020 records.

HRP1021.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1021 records.

HRP1023.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1023 records.

HRP1024.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1024 records.

HRP1025.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1025 records.

HRP1026.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1026 records.

HRP1027.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1027 records.

HRP1028.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1028 records.

HRP1029.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1029 records.

HRP1030.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1030 records.

HRP1031.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1031 records.

HRP1032.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1032 records.

HRP1033.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1033 records.

HRP1034.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1034 records.

HRP1035.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1035 records.

HRP1036.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1036 records.

HRP1037.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1037 records.

HRP1038.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1038 records.

HRP1039.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1039 records.

HRP1040.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1040 records.

HRP1041.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1041 records.

HRP1042.desc: Stores HR Organizational Management Infotype 1042 records.

HRPADD3.desc: Additional data table linked to HR infotype PADD3.
