HRPADUZ.desc: Additional data table for requirements assignment of persons in HR (PADUZ).

HRPADXN.desc: Additional data table linked to HR infotype PADXN.

HRPS_T012K.desc: Provides decoupled HR access to house bank and account data from table T012K.

HRPVAC.desc: List of vacant positions in HR.

HSMC_BDA.desc: Activation settings for extended hazardous substance checks in EHS.

IKPF.desc: Header data for physical inventory documents in Inventory Management.
IKPF.lore: Physical inventory document headers, keyed by document number and fiscal year, with plant and storage location validating against T001W and T001L. The count document's cover page; the counted items sit below it.

IMEXT.desc: Enhancement fields for goods receipt postings in Inventory Management.
IMEXT.lore: Enhancement fields for goods receipt postings, keyed exactly like a material document line. A shadow table that extends the document rather than an object with any independent existence.

INDX.lore: No description recorded. The key — a region ID and two sort fields under the client — is the generic shape used for stored data clusters rather than business records, so what it holds depends entirely on whoever wrote to it.

ISEG.desc: Line items of physical inventory documents in Inventory Management.
ISEG.lore: The line items of a physical inventory document — one row per count line, keyed by document, year and item. Its related tables cover every special-stock flavour a count might touch: customer, vendor, batch, storage location, WBS element.

IVEXT.desc: Enhancement fields for incoming invoices in Invoice Verification.
IVEXT.lore: Enhancement fields hung off incoming invoice items, keyed by document, year, item and a coding block number. Whatever the standard invoice item does not carry, it carries here.

JVGLFLEXA.desc: General ledger actual line items for Joint Venture Accounting, one row per fiscal year, document number, ledger, and line item.
JVGLFLEXA.lore: The actual line item table of the Joint Venture Accounting ledger, keyed by fiscal year, document number, ledger, company code and line. A flexible-ledger structure serving joint ventures rather than general FI reporting.

JVGLFLEXT.desc: General ledger totals for Joint Venture Accounting, one row per fiscal year and account assignment object.
JVGLFLEXT.lore: General ledger totals for Joint Venture Accounting, keyed by fiscal year plus ten account assignment object fields. That very wide object key is what lets the postings be summarised along whatever dimensions the ledger was set up with.

J_1ADOCCL2.desc: Text descriptions for official document classes in Argentina, one row per class and language.
J_1ADOCCL2.lore: Language-dependent descriptions for Argentine official document classes, one row per class and language. The text side of the document class configuration and nothing more.

J_1ADOCCLS.desc: Official document classes used for billing in Argentina.
J_1ADOCCLS.lore: Official document classes required for billing in Argentina, keyed by the class code alone. Country-specific typing laid over the standard configuration, with T003 and T156 supplying the underlying document and movement keys.

J_1AOFNR3.desc: Exception table for determining deal type, export versus domestic.
J_1AOFNR3.lore: A small exception table for deal-type determination: given a shipping point, country and region, is this export or domestic? The country validates against T005. Config in the truest sense — it exists only to override the normal rule.

J_1ASNRDT1.desc: Rules for determining branch assignment for deliveries.
J_1ASNRDT1.lore: Determination rules mapping a sales organization and shipping point to a branch for deliveries. Two key fields and a TVKO lookup — configuration in its purest form.

J_1ASNRDT2.desc: Rules for determining branch assignment for material movements.
J_1ASNRDT2.lore: Determination rules deciding which branch a material movement is attributed to, keyed by plant and storage location. Small config: plant via T001W, storage location via T001L.

J_1ATXREL.lore: A single-column check table for the tax relevance classification used in billing — the key field carries the table's own name. Config so fine-grained it is essentially a list of permitted values.

J_1ATXRELT.desc: Text descriptions for tax-relevant classifications, one row per classification and language.
J_1ATXRELT.lore: Text descriptions for tax-relevant classifications, one row per classification and language. Descriptions only — whatever the classification actually controls is configured elsewhere.

J_1IMOCUST.desc: India-specific excise duty data attached to a customer master record, one row per customer.
J_1IMOCUST.lore: India-specific excise duty attributes bolted onto the customer master, one row per customer. An extension record in the plainest sense: it adds country-specific fields to a customer that already exists elsewhere.

J_1IMOVEND.desc: India-specific excise duty data attached to a vendor master record, one row per vendor.
J_1IMOVEND.lore: India-specific excise data bolted onto the vendor master, one row per vendor. A country extension that sits beside the standard vendor record rather than inside it, which is why it has no related tables of its own.

J_3GPRLHD.desc: Header data for CEM price list condition records, one row per client and condition record number.
J_3GPRLHD.lore: Header for a CEM price list condition record, keyed by nothing more than client and condition record number. The identity row that the condition detail hangs from.

K003.desc: Sales tax condition records for Germany.
K003.lore: A condition table for German sales tax, keyed by nothing more than condition type and tax code. About as small as a condition table gets, and with no relationships recorded to anything else.

KLPA.desc: Links between customer and vendor master records.
KLPA.lore: Links a customer account to a vendor account and back again, with company code, account type and partner type held on both sides of the key. The plumbing behind treating one party as both.

KMKPF.desc: Header information for FI account assignment models.
KMKPF.lore: Header of an FI account assignment model: the named template used to pre-fill postings, with company code, chart of accounts and currency as its context. The item lines hang beneath the name in KMNAM.

KMZEI.desc: Line item information for FI account assignment models.
KMZEI.lore: Line items of an FI account assignment model — the pre-filled posting lines the model supplies, keyed by model name and item number. Its relationship list reads like a posting line itself: SKA1, T001, T001W, ANLA, AUFK, KNA1, PRPS and MARA.

KNAS.desc: Customer master data holding VAT registration numbers, general section.
KNAS.lore: VAT registration numbers for a customer, one row per customer and country — because a customer can be registered in more than one. Hangs off KNA1, with the country validated against T005.

KNB4.lore: Payment history per customer and company code — how this customer has actually paid. One row per customer-company code pair, with KNA1 and T001 as the two parents.

KNBW.desc: Withholding tax type data in the customer master record.
KNBW.lore: Withholding tax types on the customer master, one row per customer, company code and tax type. Customer via KNA1, company code via T001, the type via T059P and the code via T059Z.

KNC1.desc: Customer master transaction figures, such as balances by fiscal year.
KNC1.lore: Customer transaction figures — balances rolled up per customer, company code and fiscal year. KNA1 holds the general customer master and KNB1 the company code view; this is the money side of the same triple.

KNC3.desc: Special G/L transaction figures held against the customer master.
KNC3.lore: Special G/L transaction figures on the customer side — balances per customer, company code, fiscal year and special G/L indicator. Kept apart from normal transaction figures precisely because the indicator changes what a balance means.

KNKA.desc: Central credit management data in the customer master.
KNKA.lore: Central credit management data in the customer master, one row per customer, sitting beside KNA1 rather than inside it. Central is the operative word: this is the cross-area view, with the currency resolving through TCURC.

KNVD.desc: Customer master sales request form data.
KNVD.lore: Customer master data for sales request forms, keyed all the way down to sales area, document type and language. Output configuration attached to the customer rather than to the document that eventually gets printed.

KNVI.desc: Tax indicator data in the customer master.
KNVI.lore: Tax indicators on the customer master, keyed by customer, country (ALAND) and tax category — the customer's contribution to tax determination. Customer via KNA1, country via T005.

KNVK.desc: Contact persons linked to customer master records.
KNVK.lore: Contact persons attached to master records, keyed by a single partner number. It reaches out to KNA1 for customers and LFA1 for vendors, with T502T and TSAB supplying the marital status and department texts.

KNVL.lore: License data on the customer master, keyed by customer, departure country, tax type and license number. A narrow corner of the customer master rather than part of the general or sales views.

KNVS.lore: Shipping data per customer and shipping point — an organisational slice of the customer master rather than part of its general data. KNA1 holds the customer, and a vendor number in the record resolves through LFA1.

KNZA.desc: Permitted alternative payers for a customer.
KNZA.lore: The permitted set of alternative payers for a customer within a company code. Small and deliberately restrictive: if a payer is not listed here for that company code, it is not allowed.

KOCLU.lore: A cluster table in FI-GL with no description or key fields recorded here. Nothing further can be said about it from what is given.

KOND3.desc: Condition record data used for campaign determination in pricing, one row per client and condition record number.
KOND3.lore: Condition record data used when pricing determines a campaign, keyed only by client and the condition record number KNUMH. An extension hanging off the condition record rather than a condition table in its own right.

KONDN.desc: Condition record data for free-goods pricing determination, one row per client and condition record number.
KONDN.lore: Condition records for free-goods determination, keyed by nothing more than client and condition record number. The header of a free-goods condition — the terms that decide when a giveaway quantity is granted.

KONH.desc: Header data for pricing condition records, one row per client and condition record number.
KONH.lore: The header of a pricing condition record, one row per KNUMH — the number that condition access tables all point at. You find a record through a key combination, then land here for the record itself.

KONHM.desc: Condition record data used for portfolio determination in pricing, one row per client and condition record number.
KONHM.lore: Condition records used for portfolio determination in pricing, one row per condition record number. It rides the standard condition record key while serving a determination purpose rather than carrying a price.

LFAS.desc: Vendor master data holding VAT registration numbers, general section.
LFAS.lore: The vendor-side mirror of the customer VAT registration table: one row per vendor and country, so a vendor registered in several countries gets several rows. Hangs off LFA1, with the country validated against T005.

LFAT.desc: Tax grouping data in the vendor master record.
LFAT.lore: Tax grouping assignments on the vendor master — vendor plus tax group. LFA1 owns the vendor, T007C defines the groups; this is the join between them and nothing more.

LFBW.desc: Withholding tax type data in the vendor master record.
LFBW.lore: The vendor-side counterpart of withholding tax type master data, keyed by vendor, company code and tax type. Vendor via LFA1, recipient type via T059C, exemption reason via T059V, tax type via T059P and code via T059Z.

LFC1.desc: Vendor master transaction figures, such as balances by fiscal year.
LFC1.lore: Vendor transaction figures — balances per vendor, company code and fiscal year. LFA1 carries the general vendor master and LFB1 the company code view; LFC1 is the accumulated amounts against them.

LFC3.desc: Special G/L transaction figures held against the vendor master.
LFC3.lore: The vendor-side counterpart to customer special G/L figures: balances per vendor, company code, fiscal year and special G/L indicator, held separately from ordinary transaction figures.

LFM2.desc: Purchasing-related data in the vendor master record.
LFM2.lore: The purchasing organisation view of the vendor master, split further by vendor sub-range and plant. What gets defaulted onto purchasing documents lives here — purchasing group, confirmation control, currency — while LFA1 carries the general vendor data.

LFZA.desc: Permitted alternative payees for a vendor.
LFZA.lore: Permitted alternative payees for a vendor, per company code. The vendor-side counterpart to the customer payer list — an allow-list constraining who a payment may actually be made out to.

LIPSDG.desc: Appendix table holding dangerous goods data for deliveries.

LIPSDGAP.desc: Appendix table supporting the LIPSDG dangerous goods data table.

MAHNV.desc: Administrative records used by the dunning program.
MAHNV.lore: Administrative records for the dunning program, keyed by run date and run identifier. Run management rather than dunning data: which run, when, and its parameters.

MARCH.desc: Historical plant-level material master data, one row per material, plant, fiscal year, and period.
MARCH.lore: Period-stamped history of plant-level material master data — the material and plant key extended by fiscal year and period, so what the plant view looked like in an earlier period remains readable.

MARCHO1.desc: Additional quantity data appended to the MARCH history table, one row per material, plant, fiscal year, period, and unit of measure.
MARCHO1.lore: An overflow shelf of extra quantity fields appended to the plant-level material history, keyed by material, plant, fiscal year, period and unit of measure. Additional quantities alongside the history record, not a history in its own right.

MARCO1.desc: Additional quantity data at plant level for a material, one row per material, plant, and unit of measure.
MARCO1.lore: Additional quantity data at plant level, one row per material, plant and unit of measure. It exists so quantities can be carried per UoM rather than only in whatever unit the plant record holds.

MARDH.desc: Historical storage-location-level material master data, one row per material, plant, storage location, fiscal year, and period.
MARDH.lore: Period-end snapshots of the storage location view of a material: one row per material, plant, storage location, fiscal year and period. History kept by period rather than by change document, which is a very different kind of audit trail.

MARDHO1.desc: Additional quantity data appended to the MARDH history table, one row per material, plant, storage location, fiscal year, period, and unit of measure.
MARDHO1.lore: Extra quantity data appended to the storage-location history record named in its description, with unit of measure added to the material/plant/storage-location/period key. A satellite of the history table, not a history table itself.

MARDO1.desc: Additional quantity data at storage location level for a material, one row per material, plant, storage location, and unit of measure.
MARDO1.lore: Extra quantity fields for storage location stock, keyed by material, plant and storage location plus a unit of measure. The unit in the key is the giveaway — quantities in alternative units alongside the base storage location record.

MARI.desc: Short document data for material movements.
MARI.lore: Short document data for material movements, keyed exactly like a movement line: material document, year and line. A condensed index sitting alongside the full movement record rather than replacing it.

MATDOCOIL.desc: The oil and gas counterpart to MATDOC. SAP names both as the new material document tables that MM-IM data moves into.

MATERIALID.desc: Mapping of internal material ID numbers to material master records, one row per internal material ID.
MATERIALID.lore: Maps internal material ID numbers to material master records, one row per internal ID. A translation table, useful only when something upstream speaks in internal IDs rather than material numbers.

MBEWH.desc: Historical material valuation data, one row per material, valuation area, valuation type, fiscal year, and period.
MBEWH.lore: Historical material valuation, one row per material, valuation area, valuation type and LFGJA/LFMON period. Strictly the period-by-period record — the current valuation lives with the live data.

MCDX.desc: Consolidation transaction data held in compressed form.
MCDX.lore: Consolidation transaction data held in compressed cluster form, keyed by a region ID and sort fields rather than by any business dimension. You read it through the program that wrote it, not by picking fields off the key.

MCHABO1.lore: No description and no key fields are carried in the source for this one. An MM table listed for completeness — nothing available here supports a claim about what it actually holds.

MCHBH.desc: Historical batch stock quantities, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, fiscal year, and period.
MCHBH.lore: The period history of batch stock at storage location level, adding fiscal year and period to the material, plant, storage location and batch key. What the batch stock was, rather than what it is.

MCHBO1.desc: Additional quantity data at batch level for a material, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, and unit of measure.
MCHBO1.lore: Additional quantity fields for batch stock, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch and unit of measure. An append-style quantities-per-unit extension rather than the batch stock record itself.

MCSD.desc: Stock of a material held for or by a customer, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, and customer.
MCSD.lore: Stock of a material held for or by a customer, keyed down to plant, storage location, batch, special stock indicator and customer. One of the special stock segments, where ownership follows the customer relationship.

MCSDH.desc: Historical customer stock quantities, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, customer, fiscal year, and period.
MCSDH.lore: Historical customer special stock quantities, keyed all the way down to material, plant, storage location, batch, special stock indicator, customer and period. The history counterpart to current customer stock figures.

MCSS.desc: Total customer stock quantities summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, and customer.
MCSS.lore: Aggregated customer special stock by material, plant, special stock indicator and customer. A totals table — quantities summarised at plant level, with the individual movements that produced them living elsewhere.

MCSSH.desc: Historical totals of customer-supplied stock, one row per material, plant, customer, fiscal year, and period.
MCSSH.lore: Period history for customer-supplied stock, one row per material, plant, customer and posting period, with the special stock indicator in the key. The history side of a special stock type rather than a running balance.

MDKP.desc: Header data for an MRP planning document, one row per document type, material, planning plant, and planning scenario.
MDKP.lore: Header of an MRP planning document, keyed by document type, material, planning plant and planning scenario. The scenario in the key means simulative and operative planning results can coexist.

MDRI.lore: No description is recorded. The key runs RQNUM, BAUGR, PLWRK, VERID, RSART and RQITM, which places it among PP records held per item, but the input gives nothing further to go on.

MDTB.desc: Line items of an MRP planning document, one row per document number and item position.
MDTB.lore: Line items of an MRP planning document, keyed by document number and item position. The individual planning elements a planning run wrote out; the header identifies the document they belong to.

MDVM.desc: Entries in the MRP planning file flagging materials that need requirements planning, one row per planning file ID and key.
MDVM.lore: MRP planning file entries flagging materials that need planning, keyed only by planning file ID and key. A worklist rather than a result: it says what to plan, not what planning decided.

MEPO_BADI_EXAMPL.desc: Example BAdI implementation data for customer enhancements in transaction ME21N.
MEPO_BADI_EXAMPL.lore: Sample data for an example BAdI implementation on purchase order enhancements, keyed by document and item. It exists to demonstrate how the enhancement is meant to store data, not to carry anything a business process depends on.

MEREQX.lore: Change parameters for purchase requisitions, keyed by requisition and item. A control record riding alongside the requisition rather than holding any of the requisition's own content.

MEREQ_BADI_EXAMP.desc: Example BAdI implementation data for customer enhancements in transaction ME51N.
MEREQ_BADI_EXAMP.lore: Example data for a BAdI implementation demonstrating customer enhancements on purchase requisitions, keyed like a requisition item. Sample material shipped for developers, not a production data store.

MIGO_BADI_EXAMP2.desc: Example header table for a BAdI implementation in transaction MIGO.
MIGO_BADI_EXAMP2.lore: A sample header table shipped with a BAdI example for MIGO, keyed by material document and year. Demonstration scaffolding rather than anything productive.

MIGO_BADI_EXAMPL.desc: Example table for a BAdI implementation in transaction MIGO.
MIGO_BADI_EXAMPL.lore: A sample table shipped with a BAdI example for MIGO, keyed like a material document line — document, year, item. Demonstration scaffolding rather than anything productive.

MIGO_BADI_HOLD.desc: Holds external data temporarily during a BAdI-based MIGO transaction.
MIGO_BADI_HOLD.lore: Temporary parking for external data during a BAdI-driven goods movement entry, keyed by a GUID. Scratch storage tied to a session, not a record anyone should be reporting on.

MIGO_CUST_ACTION.desc: Controls listbox actions available in the MIGO transaction.
MIGO_CUST_ACTION.lore: Configuration of which listbox actions are offered in goods movement entry, keyed by transaction code and action. User interface control rather than inventory data — it shapes what the screen offers, not what gets posted.

MIGO_CUST_FIELDS.desc: Field selection settings per movement type in MIGO.
MIGO_CUST_FIELDS.lore: Field selection per movement type for the goods movement entry screen — one row per movement type and field name. Screen behaviour config, which is why it is keyed by field name rather than by anything a document would recognise.

MIGO_CUST_REFDOC.desc: Controls the reference-document listbox in the MIGO transaction.
MIGO_CUST_REFDOC.lore: Controls which reference document types appear in the listbox of the goods movement transaction, keyed by transaction, action and reference document. UI configuration for a single screen element.

MIGO_CUST_TCODE.desc: Transactions configured for use within MIGO.
MIGO_CUST_TCODE.lore: Lists which transactions are made available inside MIGO, one row per transaction code. A small piece of configuration governing what the goods movement screen offers.

MIGO_INIT.desc: Control settings for operations performed in the MIGO transaction.
MIGO_INIT.lore: Control settings for MIGO operations, keyed by action and reference document: which operation is being performed against which kind of reference. Configuration for how the transaction behaves, not data it produces.

MIGO_T156.desc: Movement types referenced by the MIGO transaction.
MIGO_T156.lore: The movement types the goods movement entry transaction works with, keyed by BUSTR alone. A thin selection layer sitting over movement type configuration rather than the configuration itself.

MKOL.desc: Special stock of a material held at or by a vendor, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, and vendor.
MKOL.lore: Special stock of a material held at or by a vendor, split by plant, storage location, batch, special stock indicator and vendor. The vendor sitting in the key is precisely what separates these quantities from ordinary own stock.

MKOLH.desc: Historical special stock quantities held at or by a vendor, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, vendor, fiscal year, and period.
MKOLH.lore: Period history for special stock held at or by a vendor: material, plant, storage location, batch, vendor and period all in the key. The special stock indicator is what separates it from ordinary own stock.

MKOLHO1.desc: Additional quantity data appended to the MKOLH history table, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, vendor, fiscal year, and unit of measure.
MKOLHO1.lore: Additional quantity fields appended to the vendor consignment stock history, carrying the full material, plant, storage location, batch, special stock, vendor, year and period key plus a unit of measure. Alternative units for a history record.

MKOLO1.desc: Additional quantity data for vendor special stock, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, vendor, and unit of measure.
MKOLO1.lore: Extra quantity fields for vendor special stock, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch, special stock indicator, vendor and unit of measure. An append-style extension, not the stock record itself.

MKPF_ARIDX.desc: Index table for accessing individual documents archived from table MM_MATBEL.
MKPF_ARIDX.lore: Access index for material documents already archived out of MM_MATBEL, keyed by document number and year. It exists so an individual archived document can still be located by its key.

MLCD.desc: Material Ledger summarization records derived from postings, one row per costing number, period, category, and currency type.
MLCD.lore: Material Ledger summarization records derived from postings, one row per costing number, period, category, process type, valuation and currency type. An aggregated layer sitting above the individual postings.

MLCR.desc: Currency and value data for a Material Ledger document, one row per document number, fiscal year, item, and currency type.
MLCR.lore: The currency and value side of a Material Ledger document line: one row per document, fiscal year, item, period and currency type. Values are multiplied out per currency type, which is why the key runs as deep as it does.

MLCRF.desc: Field-group breakdown of currency amounts for a Material Ledger document, one row per document, period, currency type, and field group.
MLCRF.lore: Currency amounts of a Material Ledger document broken down by field group — one row per document line, period, currency type and field group. Fine-grained storage that trades readability for flexibility in what can be held.

MLCRP.desc: Price-change currency and price data for a Material Ledger document, one row per document, fiscal year, item, period, and currency type.
MLCRP.lore: Currency and price data for a price change in the Material Ledger, keyed by document, year and item, then period and currency type. One row per currency view of the same price change line.

MLDZ4.desc: Reporting data for record type Z4 not yet reported by data medium exchange.
MLDZ4.lore: Record type Z4 reporting data that has not yet gone out via data medium exchange, keyed by company code, document and year. A holding area for regulatory reporting still owed.

MLKEPH.desc: Cost component split by element for a Material Ledger document, one row per document, period, cost element, and currency type.
MLKEPH.lore: Cost component split of a Material Ledger document broken down by element, keyed by document, year, item, period, several cost estimate attributes and currency type. Deep key, narrow purpose: which element carried which slice of the cost.

MLMST.desc: Header data for a Material Ledger costing document, one row per document number and fiscal year.
MLMST.lore: Header of a Material Ledger costing document, one row per document number and fiscal year. Everything below it in the costing document keys off this number and year.

MLPP.desc: Posting period and quantity data for a Material Ledger document, one row per document, fiscal year, item, and period.
MLPP.lore: The quantity and posting-period side of a Material Ledger document line, keyed by document, fiscal year, item and period. Companion to the value-carrying rows — same document, different half of the story, and no currency in the key.

MLPPF.desc: Field-group breakdown of posting period data for a Material Ledger document, one row per document, period, and field group.
MLPPF.lore: The posting-period counterpart to the currency breakdown: Material Ledger document data split by field group per document line and period, without the currency dimension in the key.

MLPRKEKO.desc: Header data for the cost component split of a Material Ledger document, one row per document, period, and price type.
MLPRKEKO.lore: Header of the cost component split attached to a Material Ledger document, keyed by document, item, period, price type and currency type, plus a flag distinguishing the old price. Where the split's totals sit before the components themselves.

MLPRKEPH.desc: Cost component split by component for a Material Ledger document, one row per document, period, cost element, and price type.
MLPRKEPH.lore: Cost component split for a Material Ledger document, broken down by component: document, year and item, then period, cost element, price type and currency type. Document-level detail rather than a period aggregate.

MLREADST.lore: No description in the catalogue — but the key of document number, year, item, posting year and period is the Material Ledger document line shape. Treat it as document-line-level ML data and verify before relying on more.

MLREPORT.lore: No description is recorded for it. The key — document number, year and item, then broken down again by posting year and period — shows document-level data split by period, and that is as far as the input goes.

MLXXV.lore: No description recorded. The key runs document number, fiscal year, item, period and currency type, so whatever it stores is split per period and per currency type against a document line — the shape, at least, is unambiguous.

MMIM_PREDOC_ORG.desc: Organizational data for parked material documents.
MMIM_PREDOC_ORG.lore: Organizational data for parked material documents, keyed only by a GUID. Parked documents sit outside normal document numbering, so they get a technical key rather than a document number and year.

MMIM_REP_CUST.desc: Customizing settings for Inventory Management reporting.
MMIM_REP_CUST.lore: Customizing for Inventory Management reporting, keyed by report, table name and field name. No client field in the key — settings about which fields a report exposes, rather than about any material.

MMIM_REP_PRINT.desc: Print settings for Inventory Management reporting.
MMIM_REP_PRINT.lore: Print settings for Inventory Management reporting, held one row per report. Pure presentation configuration with no bearing on stock figures or the documents behind them.

MMINKON.desc: Worklist of materials with inconsistent stock data.
MMINKON.lore: A worklist of materials whose stock data has been found inconsistent, keyed by valuation area, material, an inconsistency key and a split flag. It records the problems to be worked through, not the stock itself.

MMINKON_UP.desc: Corrections applied to fix technical stock inconsistencies.
MMINKON_UP.lore: Corrections applied to repair technical stock inconsistencies, keyed by year and a running number. A repair log; the interesting question is usually why it has rows at all.

MONI.lore: No description recorded, and no client field in the key — just a region ID and two sort fields. That is the shape of a stored data cluster rather than a business table, but the catalogue offers nothing more definite.

MPNCL1.desc: Controls dynamic calls made while processing sub-items.
MPNCL1.lore: Control table for dynamic calls made while processing sub-items, keyed by sub-item type, application and event. Framework plumbing: it says what fires when, not what any purchasing document contains.

MSCD.desc: Customer stock held at a vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, and customer.
MSCD.lore: Customer stock physically held at a vendor's location: material, plant, batch, special stock indicator, vendor and customer. One of the special stock tables where both a vendor and a customer appear in the same key.

MSCDH.desc: Historical quantities of customer stock held at a vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, customer, fiscal year, and period.
MSCDH.lore: History of customer stock sitting at a vendor's location, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator, vendor and customer, then fiscal year and period. The period-stamped view of that stock relationship.

MSCS.desc: Total customer stock held at a vendor's location, summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, vendor, and customer.
MSCS.lore: Customer stock sitting at a vendor, totalled at plant level per material, plant, special stock indicator, vendor and customer. A summary segment — no batch or storage location in the key.

MSEGO1.desc: Quantity conversion parameters for a material document, one row per material document, fiscal year, and line item.
MSEGO1.lore: Quantity conversion parameters for a material document, keyed identically to the document line. A side table carrying unit conversion detail that the line itself does not hold.

MSEGO2.desc: Material document quantities expressed in an additional unit of measure, one row per material document, line item, and unit.
MSEGO2.lore: Material document quantities restated in an additional unit of measure, one row per document, line item and unit. A satellite of the document line: the same movement, counted a different way, with the base record untouched.

MSFD.desc: Sales-order stock held at a vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, sales order, and item.
MSFD.lore: Sales-order stock sitting at a vendor's location — material, plant, batch, vendor and the sales order item all in the key. Two special-stock ideas at once: it is earmarked for an order and it is not physically with you.

MSFDH.desc: Historical sales-order stock quantities held at a vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, sales order, item, fiscal year, and period.
MSFDH.lore: Period history for sales order stock held at a vendor, with the sales order and item in the key alongside material, plant, batch, vendor, year and period. Historic special stock, several ownership dimensions deep.

MSFS.desc: Total sales-order stock held at a vendor's location, summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, vendor, sales order, and item.
MSFS.lore: Plant-level totals of sales-order stock held at a vendor, keyed by material, plant, special stock indicator, vendor, sales order and item. A summary record; the finer breakdown lives in the batch-level stock tables.

MSID.desc: Stock owned by a vendor and held at another vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, and owner.
MSID.lore: Stock owned by one vendor but physically held at another's location, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator, vendor and owner. Two separate party fields in one key is the whole point of it.

MSIDH.desc: Historical quantities of vendor-owned stock held at another vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, owner, fiscal year, and period.
MSIDH.lore: History of vendor-owned stock sitting at another vendor's location, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator, vendor, owner and period. A niche special stock category that still gets its own period history.

MSIS.desc: Total vendor-owned stock held at another vendor's location, summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, vendor, and owner.
MSIS.lore: Totals of vendor-owned stock sitting at another vendor's location, by material, plant, special stock indicator, vendor and owner. Both parties appear in the key, which is the entire point — possession and ownership diverge here.

MSKA.desc: Stock of a material reserved for a specific sales order, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, sales order, and item.
MSKA.lore: Stock reserved for a specific sales order, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch and the sales order item. Having the order item inside the stock key is exactly what makes this segregated rather than free stock.

MSKAH.desc: Historical quantities of sales-order stock, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, sales order, item, fiscal year, and period.
MSKAH.lore: Historical sales order stock quantities per period, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch, sales order and item plus fiscal year and period. The history counterpart to current sales order stock.

MSKAHO1.desc: Additional quantity data appended to the MSKAH history table, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, sales order, item, fiscal year, and unit of measure.
MSKAHO1.lore: Appended quantity fields for sales-order stock history, carrying the full key — material, plant, storage location, batch, sales order and item, fiscal year and period — plus a unit of measure. Extension data on top of the history record.

MSKAO1.desc: Additional quantity data for sales-order special stock, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, sales order, item, and unit of measure.
MSKAO1.lore: Extra quantity fields for sales-order special stock, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch, sales order item and unit of measure. Supplementary quantities hanging off the sales-order stock record.

MSKU.desc: Special stock of a material held for a customer, one row per material, plant, batch, and customer.
MSKU.lore: Special stock of a material held for a customer, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator and customer. One of the family of special stock tables, each defined by whose stock it is and where it sits.

MSKUH.desc: Historical quantities of special stock held for a customer, one row per material, plant, batch, customer, fiscal year, and period.
MSKUH.lore: Period history of special stock held for a customer, down to batch level, with fiscal year and period closing the key. The historical counterpart to the current customer stock records, kept per period rather than per movement.

MSKUHO1.desc: Additional quantity data appended to the MSKUH history table, one row per material, plant, batch, customer, fiscal year, period, and unit of measure.
MSKUHO1.lore: Extra quantity data appended to the customer special-stock history table named in its description, adding unit of measure to the material/plant/batch/customer/period key. An extension record, not a history record in its own right.

MSKUO1.desc: Additional quantity data for special stock held for a customer, one row per material, plant, batch, customer, and unit of measure.
MSKUO1.lore: Extra quantity fields for customer special stock, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator and customer, with a unit of measure completing the key. Alternative units bolted onto the customer stock record.

MSLB.desc: Special stock of a material held at a vendor, such as consignment stock, one row per material, plant, batch, and vendor.
MSLB.lore: Special stock of a material held at a vendor, consignment being the usual case, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator and vendor. The vendor-location counterpart to ordinary plant stock.

MSLBH.desc: Historical quantities of special stock held at a vendor, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, fiscal year, and period.
MSLBH.lore: Period history of special stock held at a vendor, keyed by material, plant, batch, vendor and the fiscal year and period pair. The historical counterpart to the current balance at that vendor.

MSLBHO1.desc: Additional quantity data appended to the MSLB history table, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, fiscal year, period, and unit of measure.
MSLBHO1.lore: Appended quantity fields for the MSLB history table, adding unit of measure to the existing history key. It exists so quantities can be carried per UoM alongside the standard historical row.

MSLBO1.desc: Additional quantity data for special stock held at a vendor, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, and unit of measure.
MSLBO1.lore: Extra quantity data for special stock at a vendor, carried per unit of measure on top of the material, plant, batch and vendor key. It exists to hold alternative-unit quantities the base stock record has no room for.

MSPR.desc: Stock of a material assigned to a project WBS element, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, and WBS element.
MSPR.lore: Project stock: material assigned to a WBS element rather than to general inventory, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch and WBS element. Same shape as the other special-stock tables, different owner in the key.

MSPRH.desc: Historical quantities of project stock, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, WBS element, fiscal year, and period.
MSPRH.lore: Project stock quantities by period, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch and WBS element with fiscal year and period. Stock assigned to a project, as it stood at each period close.

MSPRHO1.desc: Additional quantity data appended to the MSPRH history table, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, WBS element, fiscal year, and unit of measure.
MSPRHO1.lore: Appended quantity fields for project stock history: material, plant, storage location, batch, WBS element, fiscal year and period, plus a unit of measure. A quantities extension sitting on the history record.

MSPRO1.desc: Additional quantity data for project stock, one row per material, plant, storage location, batch, WBS element, and unit of measure.
MSPRO1.lore: Additional quantity fields for project stock, keyed by material, plant, storage location, batch, WBS element and unit of measure. Overflow quantities attached to project stock rather than a stock record of their own.

MSRD.desc: Project stock held at a vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, and WBS element.
MSRD.lore: Project stock parked at a vendor's location, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator, vendor and WBS element. Project ownership plus an external location is a combination that needs its own table.

MSRDH.desc: Historical quantities of project stock held at a vendor's location, one row per material, plant, batch, vendor, WBS element, fiscal year, and period.
MSRDH.lore: Period history of project stock parked at a vendor's location — material, plant, batch, vendor and WBS element, then fiscal year and period. Two dimensions of control at once: the vendor holds it, the project owns it.

MSRS.desc: Total project stock held at a vendor's location, summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, vendor, and WBS element.
MSRS.lore: Project stock held at a vendor's location, summarized at plant level rather than storage location — material, plant, vendor and WBS element in the key. A totals view of stock that is both project-assigned and externally held.

MSSA.desc: Total sales-order stock summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, sales order, and item.
MSSA.lore: Sales order stock totalled at plant level — material, plant, special stock indicator, sales order and item, with no storage location or batch. The summarized view above the more granular sales order stock records.

MSSAH.desc: Historical totals of sales-order stock, one row per material, plant, sales order, item, fiscal year, and period.
MSSAH.lore: Period-stamped totals of sales-order stock, keyed by material, plant, special stock indicator, sales order and item, plus fiscal year and period. The historical view of order-assigned stock.

MSSL.desc: Total special stock held at a vendor, summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, and vendor.
MSSL.lore: Special stock at a vendor totalled at plant level — material, plant, special stock indicator and vendor, with no batch or storage location in the key. The rolled-up view of what is sitting out there.

MSSQ.desc: Total project stock summarized at plant level, one row per material, plant, and WBS element.
MSSQ.lore: Project stock totalled at plant level, one row per material, plant, special stock indicator and WBS element. The summarized view above the batch- and location-level project stock records.

MSSQH.desc: Historical totals of project stock, one row per material, plant, WBS element, fiscal year, and period.
MSSQH.lore: Period history of project stock totals by material, plant and WBS element. Aggregated at project level, so no batch or vendor appears in the key — this is the summary line of project stock as it moves through periods.

MSTB.lore: Stock currently in transit, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator and valuation type. There is no storage location in the key, which is rather the point: it is between places rather than in one.

MSTBH.desc: Historical quantities of stock in transit, one row per material, plant, batch, valuation type, fiscal year, and period.
MSTBH.lore: Stock in transit by period, keyed by material, plant, batch and valuation type plus fiscal year and period. Goods that were between two places at the close of a period.

MSTE.desc: Stock in transit linked to a Sales and Distribution document.
MSTE.lore: Stock in transit tied to a Sales and Distribution document, keyed by material, plant, batch, special stock indicator, document and item, with valuation type. Goods that have left one place and not yet arrived at the next.

MSTEH.desc: Historical quantities of stock in transit tied to a sales and distribution document, one row per material, plant, sales document, item, fiscal year, and period.
MSTEH.lore: Period history of stock in transit tied to a sales and distribution document, keyed by material, plant, batch, sales document item, valuation type and the fiscal year and period pair.

MSTQ.desc: Stock in transit for a project, one row per material, plant, batch, WBS element, and valuation type.
MSTQ.lore: Project stock in transit, keyed by material, plant, batch, WBS element and valuation type. In-transit quantities held apart from stock that has actually landed somewhere.

MSTQH.desc: Historical quantities of stock in transit for a project, one row per material, plant, batch, WBS element, valuation type, fiscal year, and period.
MSTQH.lore: Period history of project stock in transit, keyed down through batch, WBS element and valuation type. The valuation type in the key is what keeps split-valued materials from having their transit quantities merged.

MWMKPF.desc: Header table for a material movement interface.
MWMKPF.lore: Header table for a material movement interface. Entry date and time sit ahead of the material document number and year in the key — unusual for a document header, and consistent with its role as interface staging rather than the document store.

MWSTOR.lore: Reversal transactions, keyed by transaction code alone. A single-column list, so it is closer to a registry of what counts as a reversal than a document store.

MWXMSEG.desc: Interface table for posting material movement values (ZMSEG).
MWXMSEG.lore: An interface table for handing material movement values (ZMSEG) into posting, keyed exactly like a movement line: document, year, line. Staging structure rather than a system of record.

MWXVM07M.desc: Fields controlling updates in module pool SAPMM07M.
MWXVM07M.lore: Update control fields for the SAPMM07M module pool, keyed like a material document line. Its links to LFA1, T024, T024E, T163 and TCURC show it drags purchasing context along with the movement data.

MWYMBUZ.lore: Line assignment data keyed like a material document item — document number, year, line. The description gives little away; the key is what tells you it hangs off a material document line.

MWZDM07M1.lore: No description recorded, but the key is a material document line and the relationships run to T001, T001L, T004, T009, T001Y, CEPC, MCHA and SKA1. That spread suggests a per-line table gathering assignments made against a goods movement.

MWZDM07M2.lore: No description is carried in the source. The key — document number, year and line — has the shape of an Inventory Management document line, but nothing available here says what the table is actually for.

MWZMBUZ.lore: No description recorded, but the key is the material document one: document number, year and line. Whatever it holds, it holds it per material document item.

MWZMSEG.desc: Interface table for posting material movement values (ZMSEG).
MWZMSEG.lore: Another interface table for posting material movement values (ZMSEG), keyed by material document, year and line. Same shape as its counterpart on the other side of the handover, and equally transient.

MWZVM07M.desc: Fields controlling updates in module pool SAPMM07M.
MWZVM07M.lore: A second update-control structure for the same SAPMM07M module pool, carrying the same document, year and item key and the same purchasing-side links — vendor, purchasing group and organisation, item category and currency.

MYABK.desc: Records database commits made during balance sheet valuation.
MYABK.lore: A record of database commits made during balance sheet valuation, keyed by costing number, program and time. Restart and traceability bookkeeping for the run, not valuation results.

MYCOM.desc: Records database commits made during balance sheet valuation.
MYCOM.lore: A commit log for balance sheet valuation runs, keyed by program, time and commit number. It records that a database commit happened during the run; the valuation figures themselves are not stored here.

MYMFT.lore: The results of a FIFO valuation, keyed by valuation key, material, valuation type, method and a local date. Output of the calculation rather than input to it, with MARA supplying the material.

MYML.lore: LIFO layers at material level, one row per LIFO version, valuation level key, material and fiscal year. TMY05 defines the version and T001Y the valuation level; each year adds a layer rather than replacing one.

MYML1.lore: LIFO layer data at material level, tied to the individual material document that created it — LIFO version, valuation key and material, then document, year and item. The version validates against TMY05.

MYML1Q.desc: Quantities for transaction-related LIFO material layers.
MYML1Q.lore: Quantities for LIFO material layers built transaction by transaction, keyed by LIFO version and key, material, and the material document line that formed the layer. TMY05 defines the version behind MYVER.

MYMLM.lore: Monthly LIFO layers per material, keyed by LIFO version, valuation level key, material, fiscal year and period. TMY05 defines the version, T001Y the key, MARA the material being layered.

MYMP.desc: LIFO period stock data for individual materials.
MYMP.lore: LIFO stock figures per period for individual materials, keyed by LIFO pool key, material, fiscal year and period. T001Y sits behind the pool key and MARA behind the material — the arithmetic of the LIFO layer over time.

MYMP1.desc: Receipt data used for LIFO/FIFO valuation.
MYMP1.lore: Receipt data feeding LIFO/FIFO valuation: which material document line, against which purchase order item (EKKO/EKPO), for which material. T001Y defines the valuation key that heads the whole record.

MYPL.lore: The pool equivalent of material-level LIFO layering — version, valuation level key, pool and fiscal year. TPOOL defines the pool, so this is where materials that are valued together as a group get their annual layer.

MYPLM.lore: Monthly LIFO layers at pool level, keyed by version, MYKEY, pool, fiscal year and period. MYKEY resolves via T001Y, the version via TMY05 and the pool via TPOOL.

NACH.desc: Detailed data for an output record, one row per client and record number.
NACH.lore: Detail data for an output record, keyed by client and condition record number — so an output record is addressed exactly the way a condition record is.

NBNK.lore: Number range assignment for banks, keyed by application. Configuration of the smallest kind: which number range applies, and nothing else.

NKAP.desc: Number range assignments for contact partners.
NKAP.lore: Number range assignment for contact partners, keyed by client alone — one row per client and nothing more. About as small as configuration gets, and correspondingly hard to get wrong.

NKUK.desc: Number range assignments for change documents.
NKUK.lore: Number range assignment for change documents, keyed by nothing more than the number range number. Config so minimal it barely counts as data, but everything numbered depends on something like it.

OBEW.desc: Valuated stock held at a subcontractor, one row per material, valuation area, valuation type, and vendor.
OBEW.lore: Valuated stock provided to a subcontractor, keyed by material, valuation area, valuation type, special stock indicator and vendor. Still yours on the balance sheet, physically somewhere else.

OBEWH.desc: Historical valuation of stock held at a subcontractor, one row per material, valuation area, valuation type, vendor, fiscal year, and period.
OBEWH.lore: Historical valuation of stock parked at a subcontractor, keyed by material, valuation area, valuation type, special stock indicator and vendor, then fiscal year and period. The period-stamped side of subcontractor stock value.

OIUCW_BANK.desc: Bank details used by the IS-Oil Check Write functionality.
OIUCW_BANK.lore: Bank details for the IS-Oil Check Write function, keyed by an organisational level plus bank ID and bank account. Industry-solution banking data kept apart from the standard FI bank configuration.

PA0003.desc: HR master record infotype 0003, holding payroll status data.
PA0003.lore: Infotype 0003, one row per employee (PERNR) — payroll status data: where that employee currently stands in payroll processing. With 456 tables referencing it, this is one of the most load-bearing tables in HR; nobody browses it directly, but nearly everything checks it.

PA0004.desc: HR master record infotype 0004, holding challenge data.

PA0005.desc: HR master record infotype 0005, holding leave entitlement data.

PA0009.desc: HR master record infotype 0009, holding bank details for payments.

PA0010.desc: HR master record infotype 0010, holding capital formation savings data.

PA0011.desc: HR master record infotype 0011, holding external bank transfer data.

PA0012.desc: HR master record infotype 0012, holding fiscal data for Germany.

PA0013.desc: HR infotype holding social insurance data for Germany.

PA0017.desc: HR master record infotype 0017, holding travel privileges data.

PA0019.desc: HR master record infotype 0019, holding date monitoring data.

PA0020.desc: HR master record infotype 0020, holding DUEVO social insurance notification data.

PA0022.desc: HR master record infotype 0022, holding education data.

PA0023.desc: HR master record infotype 0023, holding other/previous employer data.

PA0024.desc: HR master record infotype 0024, holding qualifications data.

PA0025.desc: HR master record infotype 0025, holding appraisal data.

PA0026.desc: HR master record infotype 0026, holding company insurance data.

PA0027.desc: HR master record infotype 0027, holding cost distribution data.

PA0028.desc: HR master record infotype 0028, holding internal medical service data.

PA0029.desc: HR master record infotype 0029, holding workers' compensation data.

PA0030.desc: HR master record infotype 0030, holding powers-of-attorney data.

PA0031.desc: HR master record infotype 0031, holding reference personnel number data.

PA0032.desc: HR master record infotype 0032, holding internal data.

PA0033.desc: HR infotype 0033, holding international statistics exceptions data.

PA0035.desc: HR master record infotype 0035, holding compensation instructions data.

PA0037.desc: HR master record infotype 0037, holding insurance data.

PA0038.desc: HR master record infotype 0038, holding fiscal data for Switzerland.

PA0039.desc: HR master record infotype 0039, holding additional organizational assignment data for Switzerland.

PA0040.desc: HR master record infotype 0040, holding data on objects on loan to the employee.

PA0042.desc: HR master record infotype 0042, holding fiscal data for Austria.

PA0043.desc: HR master record infotype 0043, holding family allowance data for Austria.

PA0044.desc: HR master record infotype 0044, holding social insurance data for Austria.

PA0045.desc: HR master record infotype 0045, holding company loan data.

PA0046.desc: HR master record infotype 0046, holding company pension fund data for Switzerland.

PA0048.desc: HR master record infotype 0048, holding residence status data for Switzerland.

PA0049.desc: HR master record infotype 0049, holding reduced hours and bad weather pay data.

PA0050.desc: HR master record infotype 0050, holding time recording data.

PA0051.desc: HR master record infotype 0051, holding ASB/SPI data.

PA0052.desc: HR master record infotype 0052, holding wage maintenance data.

PA0053.desc: HR master record infotype 0053, holding company pension data.

PA0054.desc: HR master record infotype 0054, holding works council pay data.

PA0055.desc: HR master record infotype 0055, holding previous employer data for Austria.

PA0056.desc: HR master record infotype 0056, holding sick certificate data for Austria.

PA0057.desc: HR master record infotype 0057, holding membership fee data.

PA0058.desc: HR master record infotype 0058, holding commuter rate data for Austria.

PA0059.desc: HR master record infotype 0059, holding social insurance data for the Netherlands.

PA0060.desc: HR master record infotype 0060, holding fiscal data for the Netherlands.

PA0061.desc: HR master record infotype 0061, holding social insurance data for Spain.

PA0062.desc: HR master record infotype 0062, holding fiscal data for Spain.

PA0063.desc: HR master record infotype 0063, holding social fund data for the Netherlands.

PA0064.desc: HR master record infotype 0064, holding social insurance data for France.

PA0065.desc: HR master record infotype 0065, holding tax data for the United Kingdom.

PA0066.desc: HR master record infotype 0066, holding Canadian wage garnishment order data.

PA0067.desc: HR master record infotype 0067, holding Canadian wage garnishment debt data.

PA0068.desc: HR master record infotype 0068, holding Canadian wage garnishment adjustment data.

PA0069.desc: HR master record infotype 0069, holding National Insurance data for the United Kingdom.

PA0596.desc: HR infotype for Philippine payroll recording PhilHealth national health insurance contribution data.

PA0602.desc: HR master record infotype 0602, holding cumulative retirement plan data for an employee.

PA0611.desc: HR master record data for infotype 0611.

PA0612.desc: HR master record data for infotype 0612.

PA0625.desc: HR personnel infotype recording race/complexion data, related to infotype 0002 fields.

PA0628.desc: HR master record data for infotype 0628.

PA0629.desc: HR master record data for infotype 0629.

PA0630.desc: HR master record data for infotype 0630.

PA0631.desc: HR master record data for infotype 0631.

PA0634.desc: HR infotype for Philippine payroll recording an employee's previous employer information.

PA0645.desc: HR infotype data holding general information about a contract termination.

PA0900.desc: HR master record infotype 0900, holding sales-related data for an employee.

PA0901.desc: HR master record infotype 0901, holding purchasing-related data for an employee.

PA2001.desc: HR time management infotype 2001, recording employee absences.

PA2002.desc: HR time management infotype 2002, recording employee attendances.

PA2003.desc: HR time management infotype 2003, recording employee substitutions.

PA2004.desc: HR time management infotype 2004, recording employee availability.

PA2005.desc: HR time management infotype 2005, recording employee overtime.

PA2006.desc: HR time management infotype 2006, recording employee absence quotas.

PA2007.desc: HR time management infotype 2007, recording employee attendance quotas.

PA2010.desc: HR time management infotype 2010, recording employee remuneration information.

PA2012.desc: HR time management infotype 2012, recording time transfer specifications for an employee.

PA2013.desc: HR time management infotype 2013, recording quota corrections for an employee.

PA2500.desc: HR master record data for infotype 2500.

PA2501.desc: HR master record data for infotype 2501.

PA2502.desc: HR master record data for infotype 2502.

PARCT.desc: Log of receipts for Japanese Financial Accounting.
PARCT.lore: A log of issued receipts for Japanese Financial Accounting, keyed by company code, document, line and receipt number. It sits beside the accounting document carrying tax code, special G/L indicator, business area and currency for the receipt itself.

PEKES.desc: Purchasing data on vendor order confirmations for document variants.
PEKES.lore: Vendor order confirmation data recorded against a purchasing document schedule line, keyed by document, item and confirmation sequence. T163E supplies the confirmation category; VBUK and VBUP appear where the confirmation ties to a sales-side document.

PPH_DBVM.desc: The new planning file used by MRP live in S/4HANA, and by classic MRP as well.

PSGLFLEXA.lore: No description and no key fields are carried in the source. An FI table present in the catalogue with nothing recorded about its content or its relationships.

PSGLFLEXT.lore: No description or key fields recorded here. The only firm statement available is that it belongs to FI.

PTRV_HEAD.desc: General trip data for an employee's business trip in Travel Management.
PTRV_HEAD.lore: The header record of a business trip: personnel number, trip number and a header version. Travel planning links back via FTPT_PLAN and FTPT_REQ_HEAD; country grouping via T500L and trip country via T702O.

PTRV_KMSUM.desc: Accumulated mileage and kilometer usage data for business trips in Travel Management.
PTRV_KMSUM.lore: Accumulated mileage per traveller, keyed by personnel number and name, trip country, region, vehicle class and a mileage indicator. Running totals behind distance-based reimbursement; T702O validates the country.

PTRV_PERIO.desc: Period-based data for a business trip in Travel Management.
PTRV_PERIO.lore: Period-based data for a business trip, keyed by employee, trip number, period and a version. Trip data sliced by period instead of held as a single block.

PTRV_PERNRPREFS.desc: Employee-level personalization settings for Travel Management.
PTRV_PERNRPREFS.lore: Per-employee personalisation settings for Travel Management, one row per personnel number. User preferences rather than trip data — it shapes how someone works with travel, not what they travelled on.

PTRV_SHDR.desc: Header-level trip amount data for a business trip in Travel Management.
PTRV_SHDR.lore: Header-level trip amounts for a business trip, keyed by employee, trip number, period and a sequence number. The sequence number is what allows a single trip to carry more than one header record.

PTRV_SREC.desc: Receipt records for a business trip in Travel Management.
PTRV_SREC.lore: Individual receipts on a business trip, keyed by personnel number, trip number, period, receipt number and a sequence counter. The expense-line detail underneath a trip.

QBEW.desc: Valuation data for stock assigned to a project, one row per material, valuation area, valuation type, and WBS element.
QBEW.lore: Valuation of project stock, keyed by material, valuation area, valuation type, special stock indicator and WBS element — so the same material carries a separate value per project.

QBEWH.desc: Historical valuation data for project stock, one row per material, valuation area, valuation type, WBS element, fiscal year, and period.
QBEWH.lore: Historical valuation of project stock, one row per material, valuation area, valuation type, WBS element, fiscal year and period. The period-by-period value of stock earmarked to a project.

QSTRE.desc: Withholding tax reporting data for Spain, used by FI and HR.
QSTRE.lore: Withholding tax reporting data for Spain, keyed by company code, document, line and year. Notably shared between FI and HR, which is unusual company for a document-keyed table.

RBCO.desc: Account assignment data for incoming invoice document items in Invoice Verification.
RBCO.lore: Account assignment lines for incoming invoice items in Invoice Verification, keyed by document, year, item and an account assignment number. That last key field is what allows a single invoice line to be split across several assignments; CSKS and TGSB hang off it.

RBDIFFKO.desc: Pricing condition data used in Logistics Invoice Verification.
RBDIFFKO.lore: Pricing conditions as they appear inside invoice verification, keyed by the invoice document, year and line plus the condition record, type and step. The invoice's own view of pricing, held apart from the purchasing document's.

RBDIFFME.desc: Quantity differences recorded during batch invoice verification.
RBDIFFME.lore: Quantity differences arising during invoice verification, keyed by invoice document, year and item. A per-item record of where the invoiced quantity and the expected quantity failed to agree.

RBDRSEG.desc: Invoice document line items processed in batch invoice verification.
RBDRSEG.lore: Invoice document line items handled by batch invoice verification, keyed by invoice document, year and item. Higher-level purchase order item via EKPO, material via MARA, sub-item type via TMSI1.

RBEX.desc: Persistent header and item-level key figures used in Invoice Verification.
RBEX.lore: Persistent key figures for incoming invoices at header and item level, keyed by invoice document, line, year and an account assignment number. Derived values stored alongside the invoice rather than recalculated.

RBKP.desc: Header data for an incoming invoice document, one row per invoice, in Invoice Verification.
RBKP.lore: Header of an incoming invoice in Invoice Verification, one row per invoice document and year. Vendor from LFA1, company code from T001, currency from TCURC, with the aggregated detail in RBVD and RBVDMAT.

RBKPB.desc: Header data for invoice documents processed via batch invoice verification.
RBKPB.lore: Invoice document headers created through batch invoice verification, keyed by document number and fiscal year like any other invoice header. LFA1 and T005 supply the vendor and country context around it.

RBKP_BLOCKED.desc: Invoices blocked for payment in Logistics Invoice Verification.
RBKP_BLOCKED.lore: Invoices blocked for payment, with the block indicator first in the key and vendor, user, posting date, purchasing group (T024) and due date following. Keyed for finding blocked invoices, which makes it effectively a worklist.

RBMA.desc: Material-related line items on incoming invoice documents in Invoice Verification.
RBMA.lore: The material-related lines of an incoming invoice, keyed by document, year and item, with MARA behind the material. The item detail specific to materials rather than to accounts or general costs.

RBSELBEST.desc: Purchasing document selection data used for invoice receipt in Invoice Verification.
RBSELBEST.lore: Records which purchase order items were selected when an invoice was received, keyed by the invoice document and year plus order and item. Selection data rather than invoice content; order via EKKO and EKPO.

RBSELDELI.desc: Delivery selection data used for incoming invoices in Invoice Verification.
RBSELDELI.lore: Deliveries selected for an incoming invoice, keyed by invoice document, year and delivery number. VBUK provides the delivery header behind VBELN; this only records that the delivery was picked up for invoicing.

RBSELERFB.desc: Service entry sheet selection data used for invoice receipt in Invoice Verification.
RBSELERFB.lore: Which service entry sheets were selected for a given invoice document, keyed by invoice, year and entry sheet number. A link table between invoice receipt and service entry, doing one job.

RBSELFRBR.desc: Bill of lading selection data used for invoice receipt in Invoice Verification.
RBSELFRBR.lore: Bill of lading data used for selection during invoice receipt, keyed by invoice document, fiscal year and the bill of lading number itself. A selection criterion recorded against the invoice, with nothing declared around it.

RBSELLBLN.desc: Bill of lading selection data used for invoice receipt in Invoice Verification.
RBSELLBLN.lore: Bill of lading selection data for invoice receipt, keyed by invoice document and year. A narrow helper table recording the bill of lading selection made for an invoice, with nothing else hanging off it.

RBSELLIFS.desc: Delivery note selection data used for invoice receipt in Invoice Verification.
RBSELLIFS.lore: Delivery notes selected for an incoming invoice, keyed by invoice document, year and delivery note number. Records which delivery notes were pulled in to build the invoice.

RBSELWERK.desc: Plant selection data used for invoice receipt in Invoice Verification.
RBSELWERK.lore: The plant-based equivalent of invoice selection data: invoice document, year and plant, recording what was picked to invoice against. Plant validated via T001W.

RBTX.desc: Tax data for incoming invoice documents in Invoice Verification.
RBTX.lore: Tax lines for incoming invoice documents, keyed by document, year, tax code and line. T007A defines the tax code; RBTX records what the invoice actually carried under it.

RBVD.desc: Aggregated data for invoice documents in Invoice Verification.
RBVD.lore: Aggregated data for invoice documents in Invoice Verification, keyed by invoice, year and a sequence number. It points back to the header in RBKP and out to the purchase order in EKKO, with plant and tax code as context.

RBVDMAT.desc: Material-level invoice aggregation data in Invoice Verification.
RBVDMAT.lore: Invoice values aggregated per material within Invoice Verification, keyed by invoice document, year and a running number. RBKP holds the invoice itself, while MARA and T007A supply the material and tax code behind each aggregated line.

RBVS.desc: Split invoice amount data used in Invoice Verification.
RBVS.lore: Split amounts on an invoice, keyed by document, year and a split key that links through to RBWS. Where a single invoice becomes several separately handled pieces.

RBWS.desc: Withholding tax data recorded for an incoming invoice document.
RBWS.lore: Withholding tax lines on an incoming invoice, keyed by document, year, a split key and withholding tax type. RBVS holds the split the key points at, so several withholding types can attach to the same split.

RBWT.desc: Withholding tax data recorded for an incoming invoice document.
RBWT.lore: Withholding tax lines recorded on an incoming invoice, keyed by document, year and withholding tax type. Tax type via T059P, tax code via T059Z — the document-side mirror of the master data settings.

RCGFILETR.desc: File Transfer Application Server <=> Frontend EHS - Product Safety Structure.

RCGSDDOC.desc: Document information EHS - Product Safety Structure.

RDGPRINT.desc: Structure for Printing DG Data EHS - Environment, Health and Safety.

REGUPW.desc: Withholding tax information per withholding tax type and FI line item within a payment run.
REGUPW.lore: Withholding tax detail inside a payment run — one row per withholding tax type on each FI line item selected, keyed from run date and identifier down to the document line. T059P supplies the type, T059Z the code and T059C the recipient type.

REGUS.desc: Accounts blocked by a payment proposal in the payment program.
REGUS.lore: Accounts blocked by a payment proposal, keyed by account type, company code, account and the payment run's date and identifier. Locking metadata scoped to one specific run.

REGUT.desc: Administration data for TemSe files used by the payment program.
REGUT.lore: Administration records for the TemSe files the payment program produces, keyed by company code, bank country, run date and identification, plus a proposal flag. It tracks the output files of a payment run rather than the payments themselves.

REGUV.desc: Control records for an automatic payment program run.
REGUV.lore: The control record for a payment run, keyed by run date and run identifier. One row per run: the parameters and state of the run itself, not the payments it went on to produce.

RF048.desc: Index and distribution vector data for subsequent business area/profit center adjustment calculations.

RF180.desc: Posting run data for subsequent business area/profit center adjustments.
RF180.lore: Run header data for the program that retroactively corrects a missing business area or profit center on postings — one row per correction run, per company code (BUKRS). Referenced by the BFIT/BFOD/BFOK adjustment tables that hold the actual fix.

RFBLG.lore: Nothing is recorded here beyond its home in FI-GL: no description, no key fields, no relationships. Best treated as unclassified until better information turns up.

RFDT.desc: Accounting data stored using the generic INDX structure.
RFDT.lore: Accounting data parked in the generic INDX-style format, keyed by relation ID, sort field and a continuation counter. The content is whatever the writing program serialised into it; the key tells you nothing about its business meaning.

RFMHN.lore: Nothing is recorded for it beyond the name and its home in FI-GL — no description, no key fields. An honest blank in the catalogue.

RFRR.desc: Accounting data used by the accounts receivable and accounts payable information system.
RFRR.lore: Accounting data for the receivables and payables information system, stored in cluster form under a region ID and two sort fields. Reporting substrate: written by the information system and read back by it, not by anything else.

RKPF.desc: Header data for a goods movement reservation document, one row per reservation.
RKPF.lore: Reservation header, one row per reservation number, with the account assignment spread across its related tables — asset, network, cost center, purchase order item, partner company code and issuing storage location. The items live elsewhere.

RKWA.lore: Consignment withdrawals, keyed by material document, year and line. Its related list reaches across MSEG and MKPF for the movement, LFA1 for the vendor and SKB1 plus T007A for the accounting side — the point where consignment stock becomes a liability.

RPGRI.desc: Text descriptions for groupings of inventory movement types.
RPGRI.lore: Language-dependent texts for groupings of inventory movement types, keyed by language and the grouping code BWAGR. Descriptions only; the grouping itself is defined elsewhere.

RQHD.desc: Header record for a total-requirements record in MRP, one row per requirement number.
RQHD.lore: Header of a total requirements record in MRP, keyed by a single requirement number. It identifies the requirement and holds whatever applies to all of it; the detail sits elsewhere.

RQIT.desc: Item-level detail for a total-requirements record in MRP, one row per requirement number, record type, and item.
RQIT.lore: Item-level detail for total requirements records in MRP, keyed by requirement number, record type and item. The line breakdown sitting under a requirements record.

RSEG.desc: Line item data for incoming invoice documents, one row per item, in Invoice Verification.
RSEG.lore: The item table of incoming invoice documents in Invoice Verification, one row per line keyed by document, fiscal year and item. Its relationships trace the whole chain — EKKO and EKPO for the order, plus MARA, T001, T001W, T007A, T163 and T163K.

S021.desc: Logistics information system statistics on production order items by material, plant and posting period.
S021.lore: An information structure for production order items, aggregated by material, plant and posting period. Carries the standard statistics preamble — source, version and the month/day/week/period columns — ahead of the business dimensions.

S022.desc: Logistics information system statistics on production order operations by work center, plant and posting period.
S022.lore: A logistics information structure over production order operations, keyed by the standard LIS block of source, version and period fields before work centre, material, order, planned order and operation. Aggregated statistics, not operations themselves.

S023.desc: Logistics information system totals records for materials, summarized by plant and MRP controller.
S023.lore: A Logistics Information System totals structure for materials, aggregated by plant and MRP controller. Carries the standard LIS key preamble — source, version and the month/day/week/period stamps — ahead of the characteristics.

S024.desc: Logistics information system totals records for work centers, summarized by plant and production scheduler.
S024.lore: LIS totals for work centers, aggregated by plant, production scheduler and work center across the standard period columns. An information structure — reporting aggregates, not the operational records they summarise.

S025.desc: Logistics information system statistics on run schedules, linking materials, production versions and orders.
S025.lore: An information structure for run schedule statistics, with the standard LIS period key (source, version, month, day, week, posting period) followed by plant, MRP controller, material, production version and order.

S026.desc: Logistics information system statistics on component material usage against production orders.
S026.lore: A Logistics Information System structure on component usage against production orders, carrying the standard LIS period key alongside plant, material, component and order. Statistics aggregated at whatever period granularity was configured.

S028.desc: Logistics information system statistics on reporting point confirmations for materials and production orders.
S028.lore: Statistics on reporting point confirmations, cut down to production version, operation, MRP controller and the production line fields as well as material and plant. Finer-grained than the order-item structures, because it counts confirmations.

S061.desc: Plant maintenance information system data on technical object location and planning, covering functional locations, equipment and planner groups.
S061.lore: Plant maintenance information structure covering technical objects and their planning context — functional location, equipment and assembly, alongside maintenance plant, location, planner group and the usual LIS period key. Statistics over the object landscape.

S062.desc: Plant maintenance information system data classifying technical objects by class, manufacturer and year of construction.
S062.lore: A plant maintenance information structure classifying technical objects by object class, manufacturer and year of construction, behind the usual LIS version and period stamps. Statistics, not master data.
