S063.desc: Plant maintenance statistics analyzing damage causes and codes recorded against equipment and functional locations, grouped by notification type.
S063.lore: Plant maintenance statistics on damage: damage, cause and activity code groups recorded against a functional location or piece of equipment, split by notification type, over the standard statistics period key of month, day, week and posting period.

S064.desc: Plant maintenance statistics on damage analysis for individual pieces of equipment or functional locations.
S064.lore: Plant maintenance damage analysis statistics: the usual LIS period key plus functional location, equipment, and the damage, cause and activity code group/code pairs. Aggregated for analysis rather than for execution.

S065.desc: Plant maintenance information system statistics summarizing technical objects by class, manufacturer and year of construction.
S065.lore: Plant maintenance statistics summarising technical objects by object class, material, manufacturer and year of construction, over the usual LIS period key. Analysis of the object population rather than of individual maintenance transactions.

S066.desc: Open sales order values used for credit management checks, one row per credit control area, customer, and period.
S066.lore: Open sales order values per credit control area, customer and period, held in the standard statistics structure shape. The open-order side of a customer's credit exposure, kept as aggregated figures rather than as documents.

S067.desc: Open delivery and billing document values used for credit management checks, one row per credit control area, customer, and period.
S067.lore: Open delivery and billing values held for credit management checks, keyed by credit control area, customer and the LIS period fields. A running exposure figure per customer rather than the underlying documents.

S070.desc: Plant maintenance statistics on equipment and functional location breakdowns, aggregated by object class.
S070.lore: Plant maintenance statistics on equipment and functional location breakdowns, aggregated by object class with functional location and equipment number in the key. LIS period stamps as usual.

S071.desc: Records associated with updates to pricing conditions in Sales Billing.
S071.lore: A statistics structure capturing updates to pricing conditions in billing, keyed by condition record number and condition item alongside the standard period columns. T009 supplies the fiscal year variant behind those periods.

S076.desc: Statistical data for sales and operations planning within the logistics information system.
S076.lore: An information structure holding sales and operations planning statistics, keyed by the standard LIS period dimensions plus planning and shipping numbers. Statistics, not plans.

S111.desc: Index linking subsequent settlement (rebate) condition records to the billing documents and periods they apply to.
S111.lore: An index tying subsequent settlement condition records to the billing documents and periods they apply against — condition table, key and document/item all sit in the key, with the LIS period fields layered on top for good measure.

S114.desc: Fleet management statistics analyzing vehicle consumption by equipment type, manufacturer and year of construction.
S114.lore: Fleet statistics: vehicle consumption cut by equipment type, manufacturer, model and year of construction. Same statistics key preamble as the other information structures, with the equipment number closing out the key.

S115.desc: Plant maintenance statistics evaluating costs by order type, maintenance activity type and technical object.
S115.lore: Maintenance cost statistics cut by order type, maintenance activity type, functional location and equipment, on the standard LIS period key. Answers what maintenance cost, by what kind of work and on what object.

S116.desc: Plant maintenance statistics analyzing service activity by customer, sales area and technical object.
S116.lore: Plant maintenance service statistics cut by customer, sales area and technical object — sales organization, channel and division alongside planning plant, notification type, functional location and equipment. A wide key for a wide question.

S225.desc: Statistics on goods receipts posted in repetitive manufacturing, linked to material, production version and sales order.
S225.lore: Repetitive manufacturing goods receipt statistics, aggregated by plant, planner, material and production version, and by the sales order item or WBS element the output was made for.

S227.desc: Product cost statistics for repetitive manufacturing, broken down by material, component and activity type.
S227.lore: Product cost statistics for repetitive manufacturing, keyed by the standard LIS period fields plus plant, material, component, activity type, production version, and the sales order or WBS account assignment.

SADLSTRECB.desc: Record body holding the dynamic part of an address list entry.
SADLSTRECB.lore: The variable-length body of an address list entry, chopped into numbered blocks under list, version and block number. The structure exists purely to make a dynamic record storable in fixed-width rows.

SBSE.desc: Stock management level data used for inventory sampling.
SBSE.lore: Stock management level records for an inventory sampling run, keyed by sample number, year and level. Plant, storage location, stock type and currency resolve through T001W, T001L, T064A and TCURC.

SDINVOICE.desc: Worklist entries used by the SDVPRSUPDATE update program in Sales Billing.
SDINVOICE.lore: A worklist for the SDVPRSUPDATE update program, keyed by nothing but the billing document number. Entries queue up here to be processed and then, presumably, stop existing.

SKAS.desc: Keyword list for G/L accounts within a chart of accounts.
SKAS.lore: Search keywords for G/L accounts, keyed by language, chart of accounts, account and keyword. Purely an aid to finding accounts; the account lives in SKA1 and the chart in T004.

SKM1.desc: Sample general ledger account records used as templates.
SKM1.lore: Sample G/L account records used as templates when creating accounts, keyed by chart of accounts and sample account number. SKA1 and SKMT hang off the same MUSTR; the other links are simply the account fields it pre-populates.

SKMT.desc: Names and descriptions for sample G/L accounts.
SKMT.lore: Names and descriptions for sample G/L accounts, one row per language, chart of accounts and sample account. SKM1 holds the sample account; this only labels it.

SKPF.desc: Header data for an inventory sampling procedure, one row per sample.
SKPF.lore: Header of an inventory sampling procedure — one row per sample, keyed by sample number and year. Only currency configuration attaches to it through TCURC; the sampled items themselves live below the header.

SLGH.desc: Elements making up a stock population used in inventory sampling.
SLGH.lore: The individual elements making up a sampling population — one row per position within a stock management level of a sampling run. The level above defines the boundaries; this lists what fell inside them, down to material and batch.

SSCH.desc: Strata definitions used in inventory sampling.
SSCH.lore: Strata used in inventory sampling, keyed by sample number, year and stratum number. The layers a sample is divided into so that counting a subset can stand in for counting everything.

STR_TCG11_A.desc: Append structure adding fields to value assignment type table TCG11 in EHS.

STR_TCG56_A.desc: Append structure adding fields to data origin table TCG56 in EHS.

STR_TCG81_A.desc: Enhancement structure extending the regulatory list table TCG81 in EHS.

STXL.desc: Lines of SAPscript long-text content, one row per text object, name, ID, language, and line.
STXL.lore: The line-level content of SAPscript long texts, keyed by text object, name, ID and language, with RELID marking the storage area and SRTF2 sequencing the lines. Where the actual words end up.

T000CM.desc: Client-specific configuration settings for FI-AR Credit Management.
T000CM.lore: Client-level switches for Credit Management — keyed by MANDT alone, so exactly one row per client. Global on/off configuration rather than anything per customer or credit control area.

T000F.desc: Cross-client configuration settings for Financial Accounting.
T000F.lore: Global Financial Accounting settings held as a single row per client. A settings singleton rather than a list of anything, which is why the key is as short as it is.

T000K.lore: No description recorded. Keyed by client alone, so one row per client, with a fiscal year variant and a currency attached through T009 and TCURC — a global setting for the consolidation side of FI rather than a data table.

T001A.desc: Control settings for additional local currencies by company code.
T001A.lore: Per-company-code control for additional local currencies, one row per company code hanging directly off T001. A small config table with outsized consequences for how amounts end up being stored.

T001B.desc: Allowed posting period ranges by company code and fiscal year variant.
T001B.lore: Permitted posting period ranges, keyed by record type, company code, account type and account upper limit — so periods can be opened for some account types and not others. T010O provides the company code context.

T001CM.desc: Credit control areas permitted for each company code.
T001CM.lore: Which credit control areas a company code is permitted to use, one row per allowed pairing. Company code via T001, credit control area via T014 — a bridge between two organizational units.

T001D.desc: Validation rule assignments for accounting documents by company code.
T001D.lore: Which validation applies to accounting documents in a given company code, keyed by company code and callup event. It points at the validation rather than containing it; T001 is the company code itself.

T001E.desc: Address data specific to each company code.
T001E.lore: Address data per company code, keyed by company code, event and address ID — meaning a company code can present different addresses for different events rather than one fixed address.

T001F.desc: Form selection settings specific to each company code.
T001F.lore: Which form a program uses, decided per company code, program, event and account type. Correspondence configuration fine-grained enough that two company codes can drive the same process to different output.

T001G.desc: Standard texts assigned per company code.
T001G.lore: Standard texts assigned per company code, keyed by program and text ID. Output configuration: which boilerplate a given program reaches for in a given set of books.

T001M.desc: Configuration data for the Z5A foreign trade regulations report in Germany.
T001M.lore: Company code level configuration for the German Z5A foreign trade regulations report. One key field beyond client, T001 as its only parent: country-specific reporting settings and nothing else.

T001N.desc: VAT registration numbers and notifications by company code.
T001N.lore: VAT registration numbers held per company code and country, so one company code can carry several registrations. Company code via T001, country via T005.

T001O.desc: Mapping of company codes across connected systems.
T001O.lore: Cross-system company code mapping, keyed by the global company code. Needed when the same organisational unit answers to different codes in connected systems.

T001P.desc: Definitions of personnel areas and subareas used in HR organizational assignment.
T001P.lore: Personnel subareas (BTRTL) under a personnel area (WERKS) — the finest-grained slice of HR org assignment, feeding into pay scale (T510A) and country grouping (T500L). Every employee's org assignment on PA0001 ultimately resolves down to one of these.

T001Q.desc: Substitution rule assignments for accounting documents by company code.
T001Q.lore: Which substitution applies to accounting documents, per company code and call-up point. Assignment only: it says where a substitution fires, not what the substitution does.

T001R.desc: Rounding rules defined per company code and currency.
T001R.lore: Rounding rules by company code and currency — how amounts behave when those two meet. A small configuration table sitting directly under T001 and TCURC, with no ambition beyond the arithmetic.

T001S.desc: Accounting clerk master records assigned within a company code.
T001S.lore: Accounting clerk codes (BUSAB) assigned within a company code — who's responsible for which customer or vendor accounts. Shows up on KNB1/LFB1 master records and REGUH, so it's how dunning and payment processing know who to flag.

T001T.desc: Descriptive texts specific to each company code.

T001U.desc: Clearing account configuration for cross-company code transactions.
T001U.lore: Clearing account configuration for cross-company-code postings, keyed by both company codes plus an account key. Both halves sit in the key because the arrangement is directional, not symmetric.

T001WT.desc: Company code-specific settings for each withholding tax type.
T001WT.lore: Withholding tax type settings that vary by company code, keyed by company code and withholding tax type. T059P defines the type globally, T059C the recipient categories; this is the local overlay.

T001X.desc: Configuration of external company codes that can receive postings.
T001X.lore: Configuration of external company codes that are allowed to receive postings, keyed by company code alone and checked against T001. A short list rather than a full organizational definition.

T001Y.desc: Valuation level definitions used for LIFO inventory valuation.
T001Y.lore: Valuation levels for LIFO inventory costing (MYKEY) — the grouping a LIFO pool is calculated at. Referenced by the MY-prefixed family (MYML, MYMP, MYPL and their variants), so it's foundational if a company runs LIFO, invisible if it doesn't.

T001Z.desc: Additional configuration parameters for a company code.
T001Z.lore: Additional company code parameters, keyed by company code and parameter type — the extension shelf for settings that did not earn a place in the company code table T001 itself.

T003A.desc: Document types used for postings that include clearing.
T003A.lore: Document types used for postings that include clearing, keyed by account type. A pointer into the document type definitions in T003 rather than a definition in its own right.

T003B.desc: Object types used for early entries in Financial Accounting.
T003B.lore: Object types for early document entry, defined per company code and document type. Configuration layered on top of the standard document type in T003 rather than replacing it — the same type can behave differently by company code.

T003R.desc: Number range assignments for invoice documents in Logistics Invoice Verification.
T003R.lore: Number range assignment for invoice documents in Logistics Invoice Verification, keyed by document category. Config that decides which numbers an invoice document is allowed to take.

T003S.desc: Document types used for storing documents in Logistics Invoice Verification.
T003S.lore: Which document types are used when storing documents in Logistics Invoice Verification, per company code. T003 owns the document types, T001 the company code — this table only says which to use where.

T003T.desc: Text descriptions for document types, one row per type and language.
T003T.lore: Language-dependent names for document types, one row per type and language. The text half of document type configuration, with the type itself defined in T003.

T004G.desc: Text descriptions for field status definition groups.
T004G.lore: Language-dependent names for field status groups, keyed by language, company code and group. Text only; the field status rules themselves belong to the variant configuration that T004V heads.

T004M.desc: Rule definitions governing sample G/L accounts.
T004M.lore: Field-level rules governing sample G/L accounts, one row per rule and field. T004R defines the rule; this is the detail that says which fields it actually reaches.

T004R.desc: Index of rules used for sample G/L accounts.
T004R.lore: An index of the rules used by sample G/L accounts, keyed by rule name alone. T004M and T004S key on the same rule, which makes this the entry point into that small cluster rather than the substance of it.

T004S.desc: Text descriptions for sample account rules.
T004S.lore: Language-dependent descriptions for the sample account rules defined in T004R. A text table and nothing more — the rule itself carries no readable name without it.

T004T.desc: Names and descriptions of charts of accounts.
T004T.lore: Language-dependent names for charts of accounts, keyed by language and chart of accounts. T004 holds the chart itself; this is its text table.

T004V.desc: Definitions of field status variants used to control field status groups in Financial Accounting.
T004V.lore: Definitions of field status variants — the container that groups field status groups in Financial Accounting. The related configuration sits in T004G and T004W; this is just the variant itself.

T004W.desc: Text descriptions for field status variants defined in table T004V.
T004W.lore: Language-dependent names for field status variants, keyed by language and variant. It describes what T004V defines, and nothing beyond that.

T005P.lore: Validation rules for tax keys, keyed by country, region and a formula code that also appears in the withholding tax code tables T059Z and T059REXT. Checking configuration rather than rate configuration.

T005Q.desc: Country-specific keys used for withholding tax calculation.
T005Q.lore: Country-specific withholding tax keys (QLAND) — used where a vendor's tax residence differs from the paying country. Referenced by KNB1, LFB1 and the T059F/T059FB withholding family, so it matters most for cross-border payments.

T005R.desc: Names for country-specific withholding tax keys.
T005R.lore: Language-dependent names for country-specific withholding tax keys. The country comes from T005 and the key from T005Q; this table is nothing but the text layer over a combination the other two already define.

T007A.desc: Definitions of tax keys used for tax code configuration in Financial Accounting.
T007A.lore: Tax code definitions (MWSKZ) within a calculation procedure (KALSM) — the config behind every tax line on a posting. 31 tables depend on it, including BSEG and BSET, so a missing or wrong entry here shows up as a tax error on live documents, not just in customizing.

T007B.desc: Tax processing configuration used in Financial Accounting.
T007B.lore: Tax processing configuration keyed by an account key, which also resolves against T059K. Sits between the tax calculation and the accounts its result eventually lands on.

T007C.lore: Defines tax code groupings by account type and group key. T007D describes them and LFAT assigns them to vendors, so this is the middle definition layer between the two.

T007D.desc: Text descriptions for tax code groups.
T007D.lore: Language-dependent texts for tax code groups, keyed by language, account type and group. The group itself is defined in T007C; this exists only to give it a readable name.

T007F.lore: Grouping definitions for sales and purchases tax, keyed by the grouping UMKRS. T007I and T007J hold the detail under the same key, while T001 links the company codes reporting through it.

T007G.lore: No description is recorded, but the key is unambiguous: country, tax code and account key. A per-country, per-tax-code entry broken down by transaction key, with T007A holding the tax code itself.

T007H.lore: No description recorded. Keyed by country and a grouping code with T005 behind the country, which places it as country-level grouping configuration — but the catalogue supports nothing more specific than that.

T007I.desc: Tax group and calendar year group numbers used for advance tax returns.
T007I.lore: Tax group and calendar year group numbers used for advance tax returns, keyed by the reporting group from T007F, the calendar year and the group number. Reporting-period scaffolding rather than tax rules.

T007J.desc: Advance tax payment data by sales/purchases tax group and calendar year.
T007J.lore: Advance tax payment figures per sales/purchases tax group and calendar year, with T007F defining the group and TCURC the currency. Yearly data held against a tax group rather than a company code.

T007K.desc: Grouping definitions for tax basis balances.
T007K.lore: Grouping rules for tax basis balances, keyed by country, version, tax code, account key and a debit/credit flag. Country via T005, tax code via T007A — reporting-oriented configuration rather than posting logic.

T007L.lore: Grouping definitions for tax balances, keyed by country, version, tax code, account key, a debit/credit indicator and a group number. T005 supplies the country and T007A the tax code.

T007R.desc: External group number assignments for tax amounts.
T007R.lore: Maps internal tax group numbers to external ones per country and version, with T007X on the external side. A translation layer for reporting, not a calculation table.

T007V.desc: Tax codes flagged for transport between systems.
T007V.lore: Tax codes flagged for transport between systems, keyed by transport request alongside the tax code, jurisdiction, date and condition type. A staging list for moving tax configuration around, not the tax configuration itself.

T007X.desc: Text descriptions for tax amount groupings.
T007X.lore: Descriptions for tax amount groupings, by country, language and version. Points back at T007R for the external grouping and T005 for the country — a text table with an unusually wide key.

T008.desc: Blocking reason codes for automatic payment transactions.
T008.lore: Payment block reason codes (ZAHLS) — why a vendor or customer line is being held back from a payment run. Sits on BSEG, BSID, BSIK as well as the KNB1/LFB1 master records, so the same code explains a block whether it's set at document or master data level.

T008T.desc: Names for blocking reasons used in automatic payment transactions.
T008T.lore: Language-dependent names for payment block reasons. T008 defines the blocking keys and their behaviour; this supplies the words shown to whoever wonders why a payment did not go out.

T009C.desc: Names assigned to posting periods within a fiscal year variant.
T009C.lore: Names for the posting periods inside a fiscal year variant, keyed by language, variant, period and year. A cosmetic layer over the variant defined in T009.

T009T.lore: Language-dependent names for fiscal year variants, keyed by language and variant. The variant's actual period definition lives in T009; this only makes it readable.

T010O.desc: Definitions of posting period variants used to control open and closed periods.
T010O.lore: Definitions of posting period variants — the OPVAR that decides which periods stand open. It joins to T010P on the variant and to T001B on company code.

T010P.lore: Descriptions for posting period variants, one row per language and variant. T010O holds the variants themselves — this exists so that the variant appears as a name rather than as a bare code in every screen that references it.

T011.desc: Definitions of financial statement versions used for balance sheet and profit-and-loss reporting.
T011.lore: The definition of a financial statement version — the structure a balance sheet or P&L is reported in. Names come from T011T, the chart of accounts from T004, and T033 shares the same version key.

T011A.desc: Task descriptions used in the FI financial calendar.
T011A.lore: Task definitions for the FI financial calendar, keyed by task identifier. T011B and T011V both reference the same task key, so this is the anchor those two hang scheduling and version detail from.

T011B.desc: Job text descriptions used in the FI financial calendar.
T011B.lore: Language-dependent texts for the jobs listed in the FI financial calendar, keyed by language and job code. The jobs themselves live in T011A, leaving this as pure wording.

T011E.desc: Calendar assignments for the person responsible for an organizational unit.
T011E.lore: Assigns a calendar to the person responsible for an organisational unit, keyed by that person's identifier VEREH. T011F and T011V hang off the same key — this is one facet of that responsibility record.

T011F.desc: Text descriptions for persons responsible for units in the FI financial calendar.
T011F.lore: Language-dependent names for the persons responsible for units in the FI financial calendar. Text only; T011E holds what is being named.

T011T.lore: Names for financial statement versions, per language and version. T011 defines the versions; keeping the text separate means a version can be relabelled without anyone touching the structure it describes.

T011V.desc: Links between FI financial calendar tasks and schedules.
T011V.lore: The join between financial calendar tasks (T011A) and schedules (T011E). It carries nothing but the relationship: which task belongs to which schedule.

T012T.lore: Language-dependent descriptions for house bank accounts, keyed by language, company code, house bank and account identifier. T012 owns the house bank, T012K the account; this names them.

T014N.desc: Credit control areas queued to be newly set up.
T014N.lore: A queue of credit control areas waiting to be newly set up, keyed by the area alone and checked against T014. Transitional configuration rather than the live definition.

T014T.lore: Language-dependent names for credit control areas, keyed by language and area. Text companion to the credit control area defined in T014.

T015L.desc: Ratio definitions referred to as SCB ratios, used in Financial Accounting configuration.
T015L.lore: SCB ratio codes — a slim piece of FI configuration (LZBKZ) referenced from BSEG and the open-item tables BSID/BSIK. The description doesn't spell out what SCB stands for, so treat it as low-visibility config that occasionally surfaces on a posting line rather than something with a bigger story.

T016.desc: Definitions of industry codes used in Financial Accounting master data.
T016.lore: Industry codes (BRSCH) — a classification tag on customer and HR master data (KNA1, PA0023, PA0634). A simple lookup, mostly used for reporting and segmentation rather than driving process logic.

T016T.lore: Industry code descriptions by language, the text half of T016. Pure naming, and the reason industry codes are readable in a UI at all.

T018P.desc: Master data for post office bank branches.
T018P.lore: Master data for post office bank branches, keyed by country and branch. Country-level banking reference data resolved through T005 — narrow in scope, and only relevant where postal banking is part of the payment landscape.

T020R.desc: Program selection assignments linked to transaction codes.
T020R.lore: Program selection assignments tied to transaction codes, keyed by code and report. Navigation config — it says which report an entry point runs, not what that report does.

T021.desc: Field definitions for line item and document line layouts.

T021A.desc: Form assignments for additional line item information.
T021A.lore: Assigns forms for additional line item information, keyed only by application (ANWND). One row per application, so this is coarse-grained output configuration.

T021B.desc: Definitions of line item total variants used in FI line item display.
T021B.lore: Definitions of totals variants for FI line item display, keyed by the variant. T021C and T021V carry the settings under the same SUVAR key, making this the header of a small configuration set.

T021C.desc: Short names for line item summary variants.
T021C.lore: Short names for line item summary variants, one row per language and variant. T021B defines the variant; this makes it presentable.

T021M.desc: Definitions of sort variants used for FI line item display.
T021M.lore: Sort variant definitions for how FI line items get displayed — the config behind those line-item report sort options. It points to DD02L, the ABAP Dictionary table catalog, since a sort variant ultimately references real table fields.

T021N.desc: Descriptions for FI line item sort variants.
T021N.lore: Descriptions for FI line item sort variants, keyed by language plus program, application and variant. T021M defines the variants; the extra key fields here mean the same variant can be described differently per program.

T021R.desc: Field selection settings for special functions in list displays.
T021R.lore: Field selection for special functions in list displays, keyed by event and selection step. Presentation-layer configuration with no business meaning of its own.

T021T.desc: Text descriptions for line layout variants.

T021V.desc: Default value settings for display variants.
T021V.lore: Default settings for display variants, keyed by transaction and account type — which layout applies by default. The variant itself resolves through T021B.

T021Z.desc: Definitions of line layout variants used for FI document line item display.
T021Z.lore: Line layout variants for displaying FI document line items, keyed by list class and variant number. Presentation configuration — it decides which columns a user sees, not what was posted.

T023W.desc: Control settings for determining value-only materials.
T023W.lore: Control settings for determining value-only materials, keyed by location and material group. Configuration deciding which material groups get treated by value rather than by quantity.

T023X.desc: Exceptions to value-only material determination rules.
T023X.lore: Exceptions to value-only material determination, keyed by location and material. A short list of cases where the general rule should not apply, with KNA1 behind the location and MARA behind the material it resolves to.

T024B.desc: Groupings of credit representatives used in credit management.
T024B.lore: Credit representative groups (SBGRP) within a credit control area (KKBER) — who on the credit team owns which customers. Sits alongside T691A's risk categories in the credit management picture, referenced by the same KNKK/VBAK circle of tables.

T024E.desc: Records defining purchasing organizations used in Materials Management purchasing.
T024E.lore: The list of purchasing organisations — the "who is allowed to buy" unit in MM. 31 tables point back at this one, which tells you how much of purchasing hangs off it. Small table, wide blast radius.

T024P.desc: Master data for credit representatives used in credit management.
T024P.lore: Credit representatives, keyed by representative group, credit control area and partner function. The group comes from T024B and the control area from T014 — this is where people get attached to credit management.

T024W.desc: Valid purchasing organization assignments for a plant.
T024W.lore: Which purchasing organizations are allowed to buy for which plants, one row per plant and purchasing organization. T001W and T024E are the two parents; the table exists purely to permit the combination.

T024Z.desc: Records defining purchasing organizations used in Materials Management purchasing.
T024Z.lore: Purchasing organization records in Materials Management, keyed by purchasing organization plus a reference organization that validates against T024E. Structural configuration rather than transactional data.

T026.desc: Definitions of purchasing areas used in Materials Management purchasing.
T026.lore: Definitions of purchasing areas, keyed by the area code EKSGB with T026Z holding the descriptions. Small organisational config sitting alongside the better-known purchasing structures.

T026Z.desc: Links purchasing groups to their purchasing areas.
T026Z.lore: Links purchasing groups to purchasing areas, keyed by purchasing organization and group. Effectively a three-way join between T024, T024E and T026 rather than a data table of its own.

T027A.desc: Shipping instruction codes used in purchasing documents.
T027A.lore: Shipping instruction codes attached to purchasing documents — how the goods should be handled or shipped. EINE (purchasing info record conditions) and MSEG (material document line items) both reference it, so it follows the order through to goods movement.

T027B.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing shipping instructions.
T027B.lore: Language texts for purchasing shipping instructions, keyed by language and instruction key. T027A holds the instructions; splitting text from definition is what lets one code print in several languages without duplication.

T028P.desc: Search string assignments used to interpret bank statement transactions.
T028P.lore: Search string assignments for interpreting bank statement transactions, keyed down to house bank (T012), account (T012K), external transaction and sign. The rules that turn free-text statement lines into something postable.

T028V.desc: Definitions of transaction types used in Financial Accounting configuration.
T028V.lore: Transaction type definitions in FI configuration, keyed by a single transaction type identifier. T028W carries the dependent detail, making this the parent key list.

T028W.lore: Names and descriptions for transaction types, keyed by language and type. A text-only companion to the definitions in T028V, carrying no control settings of its own.

T030B.desc: Definitions of standard posting keys used for automatic postings in Financial Accounting.
T030B.lore: The standard transaction keys used for automatic postings, keyed by KTOSL alone. This is the list of keys themselves; the account determination tables that reference them supply the actual accounts.

T030D.desc: Account determination settings for exchange rate differences on open items.
T030D.lore: Account determination for exchange rate differences on open items, keyed by chart of accounts, G/L account and currency. It decides where the difference lands when a foreign currency item is cleared.

T030E.desc: Account determination settings for exchange rate differences on open items.
T030E.lore: Account determination for exchange rate differences on open items, keyed by chart of accounts, account, currency and currency type. One of the account determination tables that decide where a system-generated posting lands, with T004 and TCURC behind the key.

T030F.desc: Provisioning method settings for doubtful receivables.
T030F.lore: Provisioning methods for doubtful receivables, keyed by the method alone. A very small config table defining how a provision is arrived at rather than holding any amount.

T030G.desc: Standard account determination broken down by business area.
T030G.lore: Standard account determination broken down by business area, keyed by chart of accounts, business area and transaction key, resolving to an account in SKA1. The business area in the key is what distinguishes it from plain account determination.

T030H.desc: Account determination settings for exchange rate differences on open items.
T030H.lore: Account determination for exchange rate differences on open items, keyed by chart of accounts, G/L account, currency and currency type. Accounts via SKA1, chart via T004, currency via TCURC.

T030HB.desc: Account determination settings for exchange rate differences on open items.
T030HB.lore: Account determination for exchange rate differences on open items, keyed by chart of accounts, valuation area, G/L account and currency. T033 defines the valuation area, TCURC the currency, SKA1 the account it resolves to.

T030I.desc: Account determination for intercompany cost flow in the CE ledger.
T030I.lore: Account determination for intercompany cost flow in the CE ledger, mapping an FI chart of accounts and account to a CO chart of accounts and cost element. SKA1 on one side, CSKA on the other.

T030K.desc: Account determination settings used for posting tax amounts.
T030K.lore: Account determination for tax postings, keyed by chart of accounts, transaction key and tax code. Together with the tax code definition in T007A, this is what decides which account a calculated tax amount ends up on.

T030Q.desc: Clearing account assignments for distributed system landscapes.
T030Q.lore: Clearing account assignment across distributed system landscapes, keyed by source system, target chart of accounts and clearing account key. Config for setups where a posting has to find its counterpart somewhere else.

T030R.desc: Rule definitions for standard account determination.
T030R.lore: Rules governing standard account determination, keyed by chart of accounts and transaction key. Sits a level above the account assignments themselves, saying how determination should behave rather than which account results.

T030S.desc: Account determination for exchange rate differences on foreign currency assets.
T030S.lore: Account determination for exchange rate differences on foreign currency assets, keyed by chart of accounts, a valuation key (KDFSL) and currency type. Accounts resolve via SKA1, SKB1 and SKM1; currency type via T033.

T030U.desc: Account determination for balance sheet transfer postings.
T030U.lore: Account determination for balance sheet transfer postings, keyed by chart of accounts, valuation area, transaction key and account, with SKA1 supplying the target account ZIELK. Where a balance gets moved to, per valuation area.

T030V.desc: Account number conversion mappings for distributed system landscapes.
T030V.lore: Account number conversion for distributed landscapes: from a chart of accounts and account in one system to the equivalent in another. Plumbing for a world where the account numbers do not agree.

T031.desc: Records related to bill discount notes in Financial Accounting.
T031.lore: Bill discount note records held per company code, with a key so thin it amounts to one row per company code. T001 supplies the company code and T046 attaches through a bill indicator; everything interesting is in the non-key fields.

T031S.desc: Definitions of status codes for bills of exchange.
T031S.lore: Bill of exchange status codes defined per company code, with descriptions in T031T. Company code sits in the key, so the same status code can be defined differently in different sets of books.

T031T.desc: Names/descriptions for bill of exchange status codes.
T031T.lore: Names for bill of exchange status codes, per language and company code. T031S defines the statuses; this table exists so they read as words.

T033.desc: Definitions of depreciation areas used in Financial Accounting.
T033.lore: Depreciation areas (BWBER) — the parallel valuation views on a fixed asset, such as book vs. tax depreciation or a group-currency view. Every asset value in the system exists once per depreciation area defined here.

T033E.desc: Control data for account determination in asset accounting.
T033E.lore: Control data for account determination in asset accounting, keyed by application. Document type via T003 — a small control record sitting above the account assignment detail.

T033F.desc: Posting rule definitions for account determination.
T033F.lore: Posting rules for account determination, keyed by application and four grouping criteria. T033I is reached via KTOS2 and T041C via the reversal reason — the row itself is thin because the substance lies in what it points at.

T033G.desc: Replacement mappings for account symbols in account determination.
T033G.lore: Replaces one account symbol with another during account determination, keyed by application, chart of accounts, symbol and two modifiers. T033I holds the symbols being replaced.

T033I.desc: Definitions of account symbols used in account determination.
T033I.lore: Account symbols used in account determination — a level of indirection so config tables can say 'post to this symbol' instead of hard-coding a G/L account directly. Three other T033 tables build on it.

T033J.desc: Descriptions for account symbols used in account determination.
T033J.lore: Descriptions for the account symbols used in account determination, split by language and application. T033I defines the symbols — this is what turns an otherwise opaque symbol into something readable in configuration.

T033T.desc: Names/descriptions for FI depreciation areas defined in table T033.
T033T.lore: Descriptions for the FI depreciation areas defined in T033 — one row per language and area. A text table, and the only reason T033 reads intelligibly to a human.

T033U.desc: Account determination for balance sheet transfer postings.
T033U.lore: Account determination for balance sheet transfer postings, keyed by chart of accounts, transaction key, valuation area and source account, with the target account resolving through SKA1. T033 supplies the valuation area.

T041A.desc: Definitions of clearing procedures used in Financial Accounting.
T041A.lore: Definitions of clearing procedures in Financial Accounting, one row per procedure. Document type via T003, with the procedure's further detail in T041B and its texts in T041T.

T041B.desc: Company codes enabled for manual payment processing.
T041B.lore: Which company codes are enabled for manual payment processing, keyed by company code, clearing transaction and paying company code. T041A defines the clearing transaction, T001 the company codes.

T041C.desc: Definitions of reason codes for reverse postings.
T041C.lore: Reason codes explaining why a posting got reversed — the STGRD code you pick when backing out a document. VBRK, the billing header, references it too, so the same reason list covers reversed billing documents.

T041CT.desc: Descriptions for reverse posting reason codes.
T041CT.lore: Descriptions for reverse posting reason codes, one row per language and reason. T041C carries the reason code itself; this is only the label.

T041T.lore: Names for clearing procedures, by language and procedure key. T041A defines the procedures themselves; the split between definition and text is the standard shape across this corner of FI configuration.

T042E.desc: Company code-specific specifications for payment methods.
T042E.lore: Company-code-specific settings for a payment method. The method itself comes from T042Z, the currency from TCURC and the form variant from T021M — this is where a globally defined method is made concrete for one company code.

T042T.desc: Text module assignments for payment forms.
T042T.lore: Assigns text modules to payment forms per company code. A single key field beyond client, which tells you most of what there is to know about its scope.

T042X.desc: Company codes blocked from processing by the automatic payment program.
T042X.lore: Company codes currently blocked from processing by the automatic payment program, keyed by company code, account type and the payment run's date and identifier. A run-scoped lock, not permanent configuration.

T042Z.desc: Definitions of payment methods used by the automatic payment program.
T042Z.lore: Every payment method your payment run can use — cheque, transfer, direct debit — one row each. When F110 says a method isn't allowed, this is usually where the answer is.

T043.desc: Assignment records for accounting clerks in Financial Accounting.
T043.lore: Accounting clerk assignments, keyed by user name. It ties a system user to the clerk identifiers used elsewhere in FI, with T043I and T043T holding the related definitions reached via INVPR and RFPRO.

T043G.desc: Tolerance limit definitions for groups of customers and vendors.
T043G.lore: Tolerance limits set per company code (BUKRS) for a group of customers or vendors — how much of a discrepancy gets waved through before someone has to look at it. Both KNB1 and LFB1 point back to it.

T043GT.desc: Names/descriptions for business partner tolerance groups.
T043GT.lore: Names for business partner tolerance groups, per company code and language. The text side of T043G, which holds the group being described.

T043I.desc: Tolerance group definitions for personnel processing inventory differences.
T043I.lore: Tolerance groups governing how much inventory difference a person may process, keyed by tolerance group and company code. T043 defines the groups; this table is what binds a group to a particular company code.

T043K.desc: Control settings for sending payment notices to customers and vendors.
T043K.lore: Control settings for sending payment notices to customers and vendors, keyed by company code and a grouping key. Determines who gets told what, per company code, rather than affecting the payment itself.

T043T.desc: Definitions of FI tolerance groups for employees.
T043T.lore: FI tolerance group definitions for employees, keyed by tolerance group and company code. T043 defines the group, T001 the company code — what a user is allowed to accept as a difference, per company code.

T044A.desc: Definitions of foreign currency valuation methods.
T044A.lore: Definitions of foreign currency valuation methods, one row per method. Document type via T003, currency type via T033, and the method's detail in T044B.

T044B.lore: Language-dependent names for valuation methods, keyed by language and method. The method's actual rules sit in T044A; this simply makes the code readable.

T044D.desc: Work file data used to extract the accumulated balance audit trail.
T044D.lore: A work file used to extract the accumulated balance audit trail, keyed by account type, company code and account. Intermediate storage for a reporting run rather than a permanent record.

T044E.desc: Foreign currency valuation data for items with special (hedged) cover.
T044E.lore: Foreign currency valuation entries for items with special cover — a hedge recorded against one specific document line, keyed by company code, cover number, account, fiscal year, document and item. Detailed enough to pin cover to a single line.

T044G.lore: Valuation adjustment key definitions, cut by valuation area (T033), country and the overdue/future flags. Where the age of a receivable turns into a valuation rule; KNB1 carries the corresponding key on the customer side.

T044Z.desc: Open item accounts whose reconciliation account has changed.
T044Z.lore: Open item accounts whose reconciliation account has been changed, keyed by account type, company code and the reconciliation account. A record of the change, kept because such changes have consequences for existing open items.

T045B.desc: Expense and bank account assignments with tax codes for bill of exchange presentation.
T045B.lore: Expense and bank account assignments, with tax codes, for bill of exchange presentation — keyed by company code, house bank, account ID, usage and case. House bank via T012, account via T012K, tax code via T007A.

T045D.desc: Available amount data for bill of exchange presentation.
T045D.lore: Available amounts for bill of exchange presentation, keyed by company code, house bank, account ID, usage, term and currency. T012 and T012K supply the bank and account — it governs how much may be presented, not what was.

T045DTA.desc: Check table for DME (data medium exchange) files used in bill of exchange presentation.
T045DTA.lore: Check table for the DME files created when bills of exchange are presented, keyed down to the accounting document line. It ties a document line to the data medium it went out on.

T045E.desc: Records related to presenting bills of exchange to a bank.
T045E.lore: Records for presenting bills of exchange to a bank, keyed by company code, currency and a ranking order. House bank and account come from T012 and T012K, the offsetting account from SKB1, and the country context from T001.

T045F.desc: Bank selection rules for bill of exchange presentation.
T045F.lore: Bank selection for bill of exchange presentation, keyed by company code, bank group, currency, days and a ranking order. The ranking is the interesting part — several banks can qualify and the sequence decides. T012/T012K supply the banks.

T045G.desc: Bank charge amounts for bill of exchange presentation.
T045G.lore: Bank charges for bill of exchange presentation, with a notably long key: company code, house bank and account, usage, currency, amount limit, day limit and validity. Charges that step by amount and term, hence all the thresholds in the key.

T045L.desc: Country-specific charge amounts for bill of exchange presentation.
T045L.lore: Country-specific charge amounts for bill of exchange presentation, keyed down to payment method, currency, amount and day limits plus a validity flag. Country via T005 — fee scales rather than postings.

T045W.desc: Bank subaccount assignments for bill of exchange usage.
T045W.lore: Bank subaccount determination for bill of exchange usage, keyed by chart of accounts, bank account, usage, special G/L indicator and account. It decides which subaccount a bill posting lands on.

T046.desc: Tax rate definitions for bills of exchange.
T046.lore: Tax rate definitions for bills of exchange, keyed by the bill of exchange tax code. T046T supplies the description, T031 shares the same key.

T046R.desc: Text descriptions for exceptions to cashed bills of exchange from vendors.
T046R.lore: Language texts for the exception reasons on cashed vendor bills of exchange, keyed by company code, house bank and reason. T046S defines the reasons and T012 the house bank — the wording is all that lives here.

T046S.desc: Exception records for cashed bills of exchange paid to vendors.
T046S.lore: Exception records for cashed bills of exchange paid to vendors, keyed by company code, house bank (T012) and reason. T046R defines the reasons; this narrows them to a particular bank.

T046T.desc: Names/descriptions for bill of exchange tax codes.
T046T.lore: Names for bill of exchange tax codes, keyed by language and tax code. T046 holds the codes; this is the text table beside it.

T047.desc: Dunning control settings by company code.
T047.lore: Dunning control at company code level, one row per company code. Company code referenced via RBUKM against T001 and T021M via SMHNK; the coarse switchboard sitting above the dunning procedures themselves.

T047A.desc: Dunning control settings at the account level.
T047A.lore: Dunning control at account level, keyed by the dunning procedure MAHNA. KNB5 and LFB5 reference it from the customer and vendor sides, so it is the shared definition both of those views point at.

T048B.desc: Report assignments used for automatic correspondence generation.
T048B.lore: Which report runs for which correspondence event, per company code. A dispatch table for automatic correspondence — it decides what gets called, not what gets printed.

T048I.lore: Properties of correspondence types, defined per company code and event. Configuration describing how correspondence should behave, one step removed from any actual correspondence; T001 supplies the company code.

T048K.desc: Indicator settings for periodic account statements.
T048K.lore: A flag controlling whether a customer or vendor gets periodic account statements — XAUSZ is the on/off switch. KNB1 and LFB1 both carry it, so it's set per business partner per company code.

T048L.desc: Text descriptions for periodic account statement indicators.
T048L.lore: Language-dependent text for the periodic account statement indicators defined in T048K. A pure text table — the indicator itself lives next door and carries no name of its own.

T048V.lore: Correspondence procedure definitions, keyed by procedure identifier. T048W carries the dependent detail — the parent entry for a correspondence flow.

T048W.lore: Names for correspondence procedures, keyed by language and procedure ID. The procedures themselves are defined in T048V; this supplies only the wording.

T049A.desc: Posting configuration for automatic cash application via lockbox.
T049A.lore: Posting configuration for automatic cash application via lockbox, keyed by destination and origin. It pins down company code, house bank and account through T012 and T012K, plus the document type reached via BADEB.

T049B.desc: Control parameters for the automatic cash application (autocash) program.
T049B.lore: Control parameters for the automatic cash application program, keyed by procedure and format. Configuration for how incoming payment data is read before anything is matched.

T049C.desc: Evaluation data for autocash lockbox tapes.
T049C.lore: Evaluation data from autocash lockbox tapes, keyed by creation date, time and lockbox number. Effectively a per-file processing record for incoming payment media, with TCURC covering the currency involved.

T049E.desc: Control data for the Swiss POR payment procedure.
T049E.lore: Control data for the Swiss POR payment procedure, keyed by participant, customer ID and company code. Country-specific plumbing that nonetheless reaches into G/L accounts (SKB1), document types (T003) and sales organization (TVKO).

T049F.lore: Control data for cashed checks, keyed by bank key and bank number, with a G/L account from SKB1 and a document type from T003. Configuration that tells the system how to post a check once the bank confirms it cleared.

T049L.desc: Lockbox definitions at the company's house banks.
T049L.lore: Lockboxes defined at the company's house banks, keyed by company code and lockbox. Customers point at a lockbox via KNB1, the house bank resolves via T012.

T050T.lore: No description in the catalogue, but the key — language, message ID and text number — is the shape of a message text table. Read it as language-dependent texts addressed by message class and number.

T051A.desc: Account balance data used for statutory reporting.
T051A.lore: Account balance data collected for statutory reporting, per company code, G/L account, reporting year and period, and currency. A reporting store standing apart from the live balances.

T052.lore: Payment terms definitions, keyed by terms key plus a day-limit entry so that one key can carry several date-dependent variants. T042Z supplies the payment method, T008 the block indicator, and T052U the term's descriptive text.

T052A.desc: Definitions of known or negotiated payment leave terms.
T052A.lore: Defines negotiated payment terms, one row per URLID, alongside the standard payment terms config. KNB1 references it, so it's attached at the individual customer level within the company code data.

T052R.lore: Rules for issuing invoices, keyed by a rule ID from T052A plus a validity date. The date in the key lets a rule change over time without losing its identity.

T052S.desc: Payment term definitions for holdback/retainage amounts.
T052S.lore: Instalment definitions for payment terms with holdback or retainage, keyed by payment term and instalment number. Splits one term into several dated portions.

T052T.lore: Descriptions for negotiated leave terms, keyed by language and term ID. A text-only sibling of the definitions in T052A, and dependent on them for any actual meaning.

T052U.desc: Custom explanatory texts for payment terms.
T052U.lore: Free explanatory text for payment terms, keyed by language, payment term and a day grouping. The terms themselves live in T052; this is the wording that goes out alongside them.

T053.desc: Text codes assignable to FI line items.
T053.lore: The short text codes assignable to FI line items, one row per language and code. The standard picklist sitting behind an item text field.

T053A.desc: Selection rule definitions for payment advices.
T053A.lore: Selection rules (SREGL) for payment advices — how the system decides which open items a payment advice note should match against. Referenced from AVIK (the advice header) and KNB1, it's config behind the automatic matching, not the advice data itself.

T053B.desc: Text descriptions for payment advice selection rules.
T053B.lore: Language texts for payment advice selection rules, one row per language and rule. T053A carries the rules themselves; this side exists purely so that a rule code reads as something a person recognises.

T053C.desc: Selection sequence definitions for payment advices.
T053C.lore: The selection sequence for payment advices: which rule from T053A applies, and in what order. The sequence number in the key is essentially the whole content of the table.

T053D.desc: Reason codes fixed for clearing according to payment advice.
T053D.lore: Reason codes fixed for clearing according to payment advice, keyed by company code and an advice reason group. T053R holds the reason codes; this decides which ones apply when clearing follows an advice.

T053E.lore: Reason code conversion entries, keyed by company code, a conversion version (VRSDG) and RESTG. Version via T053V, target reason code via T053R — a translation layer sitting beside the reason code definitions.

T053G.lore: Definitions of payment advice types, keyed by the advice type AVSRT alone. Config so small it amounts to the permitted list of advice categories.

T053R.lore: Reason codes (RSTGR) for payment differences, kept per company code (BUKRS). When a customer or vendor pays the wrong amount and someone needs to record why, this is the classification they pick from — referenced straight off BSEG, BSID and BSIK.

T053S.desc: Text descriptions for payment difference classifications.
T053S.lore: Text descriptions for payment difference classifications, per company code and language. T053R holds the reason code; this names it for whoever has to read the report.

T053V.desc: Version definitions for reason code conversion.
T053V.lore: Version codes (VRSDG) for reason code conversion — lets a company redefine what a payment reason code means over time without losing what it used to mean. Referenced by AVIK and the T053E/T053W reason-code tables.

T053W.desc: Names/descriptions for reason code conversion versions.
T053W.lore: Names for reason code conversion versions, one row per language and version. T053V holds the versions — without this, a conversion version would only ever surface as a bare code in configuration and reporting.

T054.desc: Run/schedule plan definitions for recurring documents.
T054.lore: The schedule that drives recurring postings — how often a recurring document template fires. BKDF, the recurring entry document table, references it directly to know when to run.

T054A.desc: Settlement data for recurring entry (posting) plans.
T054A.lore: Settlement data for recurring entry plans, keyed by the plan indicator from T054 plus a date. The date in the key means one row per scheduled point rather than one per plan.

T054T.desc: Descriptions for execution plans of recurring documents.
T054T.lore: Language-dependent descriptions for recurring document execution plans. T054 defines the plans, this names them.

T055.desc: Field assignments within master data field groups.
T055.lore: Which fields belong to which master data field group, keyed by account type, group and field name. The group is defined in T055G; this is the membership list underneath it.

T055G.desc: Definitions of master data field groups.
T055G.lore: Definitions of master data field groups, keyed by account type and group. T055 and T055T carry the fields and texts under the same GRUPP key, leaving this as the group definition itself.

T055T.desc: Names/descriptions for master data field groups.
T055T.lore: Names for master data field groups, one row per account type, group and language. T055G defines the group; this is the text layer over it.

T056.lore: Interest calculation indicators (VZSKZ) used in interest settlement — referenced from customer and vendor master records (KNB1, LFB1) as well as G/L accounts (SKB1, SKM1). One code drives interest calc wherever it's switched on, across both accounts and account types.

T056A.desc: Time-dependent condition records used in interest calculation.
T056A.lore: Time-dependent interest condition records, keyed by interest indicator, currency, validity start and a sequence number. Because validity sits in the key, rate history accumulates rather than overwrites — old rates stay reachable.

T056B.desc: Definitions of transaction types used in interest calculation.
T056B.lore: Transaction type definitions for interest calculation, keyed by interest type. T056L and T056T share the same key, so the definition here is the anchor for both the detail and the text.

T056D.desc: Form name assignments for overdue interest calculations.
T056D.lore: Assigns form names to interest calculation runs, keyed by interest indicator and company code. Purely about what the output looks like, not how the interest is computed.

T056F.lore: Reference interest rate definitions, keyed by rate type, currency and date, so rates are held as a dated series rather than a single value. Currency validates against TCURC.

T056L.desc: Function module assignments for interest transaction types.
T056L.lore: Which function module handles which interest transaction type, keyed by INTTYP. T056B defines the type; this is the hook that says what code runs for it.

T056T.desc: Text descriptions for transaction types used in interest calculation reports.
T056T.lore: Text descriptions for the transaction types used in interest calculation reports, one row per language and type. T056B holds the type being described.

T056U.desc: Control settings for calculating interest on arrears.
T056U.lore: Control settings for calculating interest on arrears, one row per interest indicator, with TCURC behind the currency. The switches governing how interest is worked out, kept separate from the rates themselves.

T056X.lore: Descriptions for the interest indicators defined in T056, per language. A text table, which is also why the interest indicator on a master record means nothing until you join back to it.

T056Z.lore: Interest rate values, keyed by interest indicator, currency and valid-from date. T056 defines the indicator; the date in the key means rates are versioned over time rather than overwritten.

T057.lore: Definitions of subsidy indicators, keyed by the indicator and a validity start date. Small, self-contained configuration with no relationships recorded.

T057T.lore: Language-dependent names for subsidy indicators, keyed by language and indicator. Pure text, with the indicator itself put to work elsewhere in FI.

T058A.desc: Configuration data for Japanese stamp tax.
T058A.lore: Configuration data for Japanese stamp tax, keyed by object type, country, currency and level. Country-specific FI configuration that matters intensely in one place and not at all anywhere else.

T058B.desc: Assignment of number ranges to receipts.
T058B.lore: Assignment of number ranges to receipt groups per company code. Small configuration with three declared neighbours: T001 for the company code, T058T for the group's text, and PARCT reached through a batch field.

T058T.lore: Names for the receipt groups defined in T058B, scoped by company code and language. Note that the key uses CLIENT where most FI tables use the older client field name — a small hint about when it was built.

T059B.desc: Names/descriptions for vendor withholding tax categories.
T059B.lore: Names for vendor withholding tax categories, keyed by language, country and recipient type. T059A holds the categories, T005 the country — the country in the key reflects how national these classifications are.

T059C.desc: Withholding tax class assignments for vendors by withholding tax type.
T059C.lore: Withholding tax classes (QSREC) — the exemption or rate tier a vendor falls into for a given withholding tax type. This is the config that determines how much actually gets withheld, one step past T059P's basic tax type.

T059D.desc: Withholding tax class assignments for vendors by withholding tax type.
T059D.lore: Assigns withholding tax classes to vendors by withholding tax type, keyed by language, country, type and recipient code. Country via T005, recipient code via T059C, tax type via T059P.

T059E.desc: Definitions of income types for withholding tax purposes.
T059E.lore: Income types for withholding tax, defined per country (LAND1) via a link to T005. Before tax can be withheld correctly, the system needs to know what kind of income it's dealing with — this is that classification.

T059F.desc: Formula definitions for calculating withholding tax.
T059F.lore: Formulas for calculating withholding tax, keyed by country, currency, withholding tax code, recipient country and a base amount band. T059Q defines the code; the banding in the key is what makes this a scale rather than a flat rate.

T059FB.desc: Formula definitions for calculating withholding tax under extended functions.
T059FB.lore: Formulas for calculating withholding tax under the extended functions, keyed by country, currency, withholding tax type and code plus validity and base amount. T059P defines the type, T059Z the code.

T059FBH.desc: Header data for withholding tax formula definitions.
T059FBH.lore: Header of a withholding tax formula: country, currency, withholding tax type and code, a second country key and a validity date. The deep key is the whole point — formulas are country- and time-specific, and this pins down which one applies.

T059G.lore: Descriptions for the income types defined in T059E, by country and language. Withholding reporting is country-specific, which is why the country from T005 appears in the key of even the text table.

T059K.desc: Withholding tax code assignments to processing keys.
T059K.lore: Maps withholding tax types to account determination keys per country, with a usage indicator in the key. T059P defines the type, T007B the transaction key: the bridge between the withholding tax concept and the accounts it posts to.

T059L.desc: Regional minimum amount thresholds for US 1099 tax reporting.
T059L.lore: Regional minimum amount thresholds for US 1099 reporting, broken down by country, region and withholding tax type — below these figures nothing is reported. Country via T005, currency via TCURC.

T059M.desc: Definitions of minority indicators used in withholding tax.
T059M.lore: Definitions of minority indicators used in withholding tax, keyed by the indicator MINDK. LFB1 references it from the vendor company code view and T059T holds the descriptions.

T059MINMAX.desc: Minimum and maximum amount thresholds for withholding tax.
T059MINMAX.lore: Minimum and maximum amount thresholds for withholding tax, per country, type, code, validity date and currency. The limits that sit beside the rates rather than inside them.

T059O.desc: Descriptions for official withholding tax keys.
T059O.lore: Official withholding tax keys per country, keyed by country and code. T059OT carries their language texts and T059Z the withholding tax codes that reference them, which makes this the anchor of that small trio.

T059OT.desc: Language-dependent text descriptions for official withholding tax keys.
T059OT.lore: Language-dependent names for the official withholding tax keys held in T059O, per country (T005). The word official is doing work here: these are the codes an authority recognises, not internal shorthand.

T059P.lore: The catalog of withholding tax types, one set per country (LAND1). It sits at the top of a small hierarchy — T059C and T059D, both in its referenced_by list, hang their detail off the WITHT code defined here.

T059Q.lore: No description recorded, but the key of country plus withholding tax code and the reach of its related tables — BSEG, BSID and BSIK all reference the code — mark it as a core withholding tax code definition rather than a peripheral one.

T059R.desc: Definitions of recipient codes used in withholding tax reporting.
T059R.lore: Definitions of recipient codes used in withholding tax reporting, keyed by country, region, a province code (FPRCD) and the recipient code itself. Country via T005, with the related definition in T059A.

T059REXT.desc: Reporting key definitions for extended withholding tax.
T059REXT.lore: Reporting keys for extended withholding tax, keyed by country, withholding tax type, region, a further regional code and recipient type, validated against T005, T005P, T059P and T059C. It steers reporting output rather than the calculation.

T059T.lore: Text descriptions for minority indicators, by language. T059M holds the indicator; this table exists solely so it has a name.

T059U.desc: Text descriptions for withholding tax types.
T059U.lore: Language texts for withholding tax types, scoped by country as well as language. T059P defines the types and T005 the country; this table adds nothing but the wording to a combination defined elsewhere.

T059V.desc: Exemption reason definitions per withholding tax type.
T059V.lore: Reasons a vendor can be exempted from a specific withholding tax type, per country. LFBW — vendor withholding tax data — references it directly, so this is the code that shows up when a vendor legitimately shouldn't have tax withheld.

T059W.desc: Exemption reason definitions per withholding tax type.
T059W.lore: Exemption reasons per withholding tax type, defined against T059V and scoped by country (T005). Language sits in the key, so these are the readable reasons rather than the calculation behind them.

T059Z.desc: Withholding tax code definitions for enhanced/extended functions.
T059Z.lore: Extended withholding tax codes (WT_WITHCD), one layer more granular than T059P's tax type — this is the actual code posted against a vendor line. Five other config tables feed into it (T005, T005P, T059E, T059O, T059P), which tells you extended withholding tax setup isn't a one-table job.

T059ZT.desc: Text descriptions for withholding tax codes.
T059ZT.lore: Text descriptions for withholding tax codes, keyed by language, country, withholding tax type and code. T059Z holds the codes themselves; the full country-type-code key is carried through so the texts land on the right one.

T059ZZINS.desc: Withholding tax code definitions used for interest calculation.
T059ZZINS.lore: Withholding tax code definitions specific to interest calculation, keyed by country, tax type, tax code and a validity date. Country via T005, type via T059P, base code via T059Z.

T060.desc: Rule definitions for the accounts receivable/payable information system.
T060.lore: Rules for the accounts receivable and payable information system, keyed by owner, account type, reference transaction type and version. T060T carries the names, with T060A and T060O defining the transaction type and owner.

T060A.desc: Definitions of evaluation types in the A/R and A/P information system.
T060A.lore: Evaluation type definitions for the A/R and A/P information system — KOART tells you whether an entry is for customers or vendors. It's config sitting under T060, the information system's core table.

T060B.desc: Text descriptions for evaluation types in the A/R and A/P information system.
T060B.lore: Descriptions for evaluation types in the A/R and A/P information system, keyed by owner, account type and evaluation type. T060A and T060O hold the objects being described.

T060K.desc: Definitions of constants used in Financial Accounting.
T060K.lore: Definitions of constants used in Financial Accounting, keyed by constant name. T060L and T060M key on the same constant, so this sits at the head of a three-table set rather than standing on its own.

T060L.lore: Names and descriptions for the FI constants defined in T060K. A text table sitting on top of a config table that most people never open directly, and rarely need to.

T060M.lore: Values for FI constants, keyed by company code, business area, fiscal year, period and constant. T060K defines what each constant is; this table supplies the number for a given period and organisational slice.

T060O.desc: Definitions of evaluation views in the A/R and A/P information system.
T060O.lore: Evaluation view definitions for the A/R and A/P information system — the saved 'lens' (by owner, by account type KOART) that reporting gets run through. Config for how the info system presents receivables and payables, not the transactions themselves.

T060S.desc: Definitions of evaluation views in the A/R and A/P information system.
T060S.lore: Definitions of evaluation views in the A/R and A/P information system, keyed only by owner. Reporting configuration, unusually thin, and with no relationships recorded to anything else.

T060T.desc: Names/descriptions for evaluations in the FI information system.
T060T.lore: Names for FI information system evaluations, keyed by language on top of the same owner, account type, reference type and version key as T060. A text layer over the evaluation definition.

T060U.desc: Descriptions for evaluation views in the A/R and A/P information system.
T060U.lore: Descriptions for evaluation views in the A/R and A/P information system, per owner, account type and language. A sibling of the evaluation type texts, sitting one level up.

T061R.desc: Active external partner product assignments for FI-ARI.
T061R.lore: Active external partner product assignments for the accounts receivable interface, keyed by party and product. A registry of which external products are switched on, with no declared relationships to lean on.

T063D.desc: Screen control settings for views in Inventory Management.
T063D.lore: Screen control for Inventory Management views, keyed by screen, function code and view. Note the absence of a client field — this is cross-client UI configuration rather than business data.

T064A.desc: Definitions of stock types used in physical inventory.
T064A.lore: Stock type codes (BSTAR) used in physical inventory — the state a counted quantity gets flagged as. Referenced by the inventory count tables SBSE and SLGH, it's about the tag, not the quantity itself.

T064B.desc: Stock type definitions and text descriptions for physical inventory.
T064B.lore: Stock types for physical inventory together with their descriptions, keyed by language and stock type. T064A defines the types and ISEG references them on count documents.

T070.desc: Document retention period settings for FI document archiving.
T070.lore: Retention period settings for FI document archiving, keyed by company code and document type. Determines how long a document of that type must stay put before it may be archived.

T071.desc: Account retention period settings for FI document archiving.
T071.lore: Retention periods for FI document archiving at account level, keyed by company code, account type and account. It states how long documents on an account must stay before archiving may touch them.

T074.desc: Definitions of special G/L (general ledger) accounts.
T074.lore: Definitions of special G/L accounts: for a chart of accounts, account type and special G/L indicator, which account (HKONT) pairs with which special G/L account (SKONT). T074U names the indicators.

T074A.desc: Names/descriptions for special G/L transaction types.
T074A.lore: Names for special G/L transaction types, one row per language and type. Nothing is declared against it: the codes themselves are referenced from elsewhere, and this table only makes them readable when they surface.

T074T.lore: Names for the special G/L indicators defined in T074U, per language and account type. Account type is in the key, so the same indicator letter under a different account type is a genuinely different thing.

T074U.desc: Property definitions for special G/L indicators.
T074U.lore: Behavior flags for special G/L indicators (UMSKZ), by account type (KOART) — the setup behind transactions like down payments, bills of exchange, and guarantees. It's what tells the system a posting needs special handling instead of normal open-item logic.

T076B.desc: EDI mapping of invoice names to company codes.
T076B.lore: EDI mapping from an invoice name to a company code, keyed by partner type, account and the name as it arrives. Translation configuration for inbound documents that identify your company in their own words.

T076I.desc: G/L account mappings used in FI-EDI processing.
T076I.lore: G/L account mappings used in FI-EDI processing, keyed by PARART, account, company code and EWLNR. The target account resolves via SKB1 and the company code via T001.

T076K.desc: Additional account assignment mappings used in FI-EDI processing.
T076K.lore: Additional account assignment mapping for FI-EDI, keyed by partner role, account, company code and an assignment key. Its links reach the whole account assignment zoo — cost center, order, project element, profit center, asset, plant and business area.

T076M.desc: EDI mapping between external tax rates and internal tax codes.
T076M.lore: EDI mapping from an externally supplied tax rate to an internal tax code, keyed by partner type, account, tax type, rate and country. Inbound translation, so a foreign rate lands on the right code.

T076S.desc: Program parameter settings for EDI INVOIC (invoice) processing.
T076S.lore: Program parameters for inbound EDI invoice processing, keyed by parameter type, account and company code. Settings applied per account rather than globally, with T001 supplying the company code context.

T077D.lore: Customer account groups (KTOKD) — the classification set at customer creation that controls which fields are required, optional, or hidden, and what number range gets used. Referenced by KNA1 and tables like T077K, T077X and TPAKD that hang further config off it, decided once at master-data creation and rarely touched again.

T077K.lore: Vendor account group definitions, keyed by the group alone. T077D and T077Y sit alongside it in the same account-group family — the classification that everything else about a vendor master hangs from.

T077S.lore: G/L account group definitions per chart of accounts, keyed by chart of accounts and account group. T077Z names them, SKA1 references them for both group and number range — this is what constrains which numbers an account may take.

T077X.desc: Names/descriptions for customer account groups defined in table T077D.
T077X.lore: Language-dependent names for customer account groups, keyed by language and account group. The groups themselves are defined in T077D; this only supplies the wording.

T077Y.desc: Names/descriptions for vendor account groups defined in table T077K.
T077Y.lore: Language-dependent names for vendor account groups, keyed by language and account group. T077K defines the group; this supplies only the wording.

T077Z.desc: Names/descriptions for G/L account groups defined in table T077S.
T077Z.lore: Names for the G/L account groups defined in T077S, one row per chart of accounts, group and language. Descriptions only.

T078S.desc: Screen selection settings by action type for G/L account master data.
T078S.lore: Screen selection for G/L account master data by activity type — which fields appear depending on what is being done. One row per activity type makes it a compact table with disproportionately wide consequences.

T079V.desc: Screen selection settings for customer master data by sales organization.
T079V.lore: Screen selection for customer master data, keyed by sales organization (TVKO). Config that changes what a user is shown depending on which sales organization they happen to be working in.

T100C.desc: User-level control settings for system messages.
T100C.lore: Per-user control over system messages, keyed by message class, message number and user name, with USR02 as the user. Lets one person's error be another person's warning.

T130F_C.desc: The new Customizing table that copy-control-relevant entries from T130F must be copied into.

T134H.desc: Business area determination settings by organizational unit in Materials Management.
T134H.lore: Business area determination in Materials Management, keyed by valuation area and division, resolving to a business area validated by TGSB. A small derivation table between MM organizational units and FI reporting.
