T134M.desc: Control settings for quantity and value updates in Inventory Management.
T134M.lore: Whether quantity and value updates happen for a given valuation area and material type. A small table with large consequences: it is what decides whether a material type carries value in that area.

T147B.desc: Text descriptions for main criteria used in vendor evaluation.
T147B.lore: Descriptions for the main criteria used in vendor evaluation, by language. T147G holds the criterion; this is what appears on screen.

T147D.desc: Text descriptions for sub-criteria used in vendor evaluation.
T147D.lore: Language texts for vendor evaluation sub-criteria, keyed beneath the main criterion they belong to. T147G defines the main criterion and T147I the sub-criterion; the evaluation hierarchy is visible in the key itself.

T147F.desc: Descriptions of weighting keys used in vendor evaluation.
T147F.lore: Descriptions of the weighting keys used in vendor evaluation, defined in T147J. A text table at the outer edge of the evaluation configuration, per language.

T147G.desc: Definitions of main criteria used in vendor evaluation.
T147G.lore: Main criteria (HKRIT) for vendor evaluation — the top-level scoring categories, things like price, quality, delivery, service. Everything under vendor evaluation's sub-criteria and scores (ELBM, ELBP) eventually rolls up into one of these.

T147I.desc: Definitions of sub-criteria assigned to a main criterion in vendor evaluation.
T147I.lore: Sub-criteria assigned to a main criterion in vendor evaluation, keyed by main criterion and sub-criterion. T147G defines the main criteria and T147D the sub-criteria; this is the assignment between the two levels.

T147J.desc: Definitions of weighting keys used in vendor evaluation.
T147J.lore: Definitions of the weighting keys used in vendor evaluation, one row per key. Referenced from the evaluation record via ELBK and from the criteria configuration in T147F.

T156F.desc: Field definitions within a quantity string used in Inventory Management.
T156F.lore: Field definitions within a quantity string, keyed by segment and a counter. Structural metadata describing how the string is assembled, not stock data.

T156HT.desc: Main text descriptions for goods movement types.
T156HT.lore: Main text descriptions for goods movement types, one row per movement type and language. T156 is the movement type; this is the part anyone actually reads.

T156M.desc: Quantity posting string definitions for goods movements.
T156M.lore: Quantity posting strings for goods movements — which quantity fields get updated, keyed by posting string, status and a counter. Notably there is no client field in the key, making this cross-client movement logic.

T156S.desc: Quantity and value posting settings for movement types, used prior to Release 4.5B.
T156S.lore: Quantity and value update settings per movement type, described as being used prior to Release 4.5B. The key spells out the whole combination — value and quantity update, special stock, movement indicators — with T156 defining the movement type.

T156SC.desc: Customer-specific quantity/value posting settings for movement types, effective from Release 4.6A.
T156SC.lore: Customer-specific quantity and value updating settings for movement types, with an unusually wide key covering movement type, the update indicators, special stock, movement and consumption indicators. T156 owns the movement type; this refines how it posts.

T156SY.desc: System-defined quantity/value update settings for movement types, effective Release 4.6A.
T156SY.lore: System-delivered quantity and value update rules for movement types, keyed by transaction/event, the update flags themselves, special stock indicator and the movement indicators. Note the missing client field: this is system-wide, not customer configuration.

T156T.desc: Text descriptions for goods movement types.
T156T.lore: Descriptions of goods movement types, keyed by language, movement type and the qualifying indicators for special stock, movement, receipt and consumption. T156 holds the movement type's actual control settings.

T156W.desc: Value definitions for posting strings used in goods movements.
T156W.lore: Values for the posting strings used in goods movements, keyed by posting string and a counter. Configuration lying deep under movement type determination — not somewhere to browse casually.

T156X.lore: Rules deriving transaction and event keys from a movement type together with its value and quantity update flags, special stock, consumption and movement indicators. The long key is necessary: account determination branches on all of it. T156 supplies the movement type.

T158G.desc: Valid code definitions for BAPI goods movement postings in Inventory Management.
T158G.lore: The list of valid codes for BAPI goods movement postings — a single-field, client-independent table. Small, but it decides which posting flavours an interface is even permitted to request.

T159A.desc: Test data used for batch input in Inventory Management.
T159A.lore: Test data for Inventory Management batch input, keyed by report name and a counter. Development scaffolding rather than business data.

T159C.desc: Value definitions for counting cycles used in cycle counting.
T159C.lore: Value definitions for the counting cycles used in cycle counting, keyed by plant and the counting cycle indicator (ABCIN). Referenced from MARC and ISEG via that same field, with the plant validated by T001W.

T159F.desc: Error message definitions resulting from blocked objects in Inventory Management.
T159F.lore: Error message definitions for blocked objects, keyed by message class and number. A message catalogue for those situations rather than a record of any particular block.

T159I.desc: Test data used for batch input in physical inventory processing.
T159I.lore: Test data for batch input in physical inventory processing, keyed by report and counter. A development and testing aid that happens to live in the same database as everything else.

T159L.desc: Default value settings for inventory management and physical inventory.
T159L.lore: Default settings for inventory management and physical inventory, one row per plant. T001W supplies the plant and T064A a movement-related default — this is where a plant gets to differ from everyone else.

T159X.desc: Number range determination settings for Inventory Management.
T159X.lore: Number range determination for Inventory Management, keyed by plant (T001W), storage location (T001L) and movement type (T156). T159Z supplies the number range mode the determination points at.

T159Z.desc: Number range definitions for Inventory Management.
T159Z.lore: Number range assignments for Inventory Management, keyed by a number range mode. T159X defines the modes, making this the small table that decides which range gets used.

T160M.desc: Message control settings for system messages in Purchasing.
T160M.lore: Message control for Purchasing, keyed by message version plus message class and number — the configuration that decides how each system message is treated when it fires.

T161.lore: Purchasing document types (BSART), with BSTYP marking the broader document category — standard PO, contract, scheduling agreement, RFQ. Eight other tables (T161P/T/W, the T166 family, TVKO) hang further configuration off whichever type is defined here.

T161M.desc: Fine-tuned control settings for purchasing message types.
T161M.lore: Fine-tuned output control for purchasing message types, keyed by usage, application, print operation and message type. T685 defines the message type; DRUVO in the key is what separates the different print operations.

T161P.desc: Validation settings linking document type, document category, and item category in purchasing.
T161P.lore: Which combinations of purchasing document type, category and item category are permitted. A validation table whose purpose is to say no. T161 defines the document type, T163 the item category.

T161T.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing document types.
T161T.lore: Language texts for purchasing document types, keyed by type and document category. T161 holds the types themselves and the behaviour behind them; this side carries only the wording that appears on screen.

T161W.desc: Definitions of order types used for stock transfers.
T161W.lore: Order type definitions for stock transfers, keyed by supplying plant, receiving plant and document category. T161 holds the order type itself; this decides which one applies to a given pair of plants.

T163.desc: Definitions of item categories used in purchasing documents.
T163.lore: Item categories (PSTYP) for purchasing documents — the flag that decides how a line behaves under the hood. Referenced across RSEG (invoice line items) and QALS (quality inspection lots) among others, so it's a switch multiple downstream processes check.

T163B.desc: Definitions of purchase order history categories.
T163B.lore: The categories that classify each line in a purchase order's history — BEWTP is the category code. EKBE, the PO history table itself, depends on it to say what kind of event each history line represents.

T163C.desc: Text descriptions for purchase order history categories.
T163C.lore: Language-dependent texts for purchase order history categories. T163B defines the categories that classify each history record; this names them.

T163D.desc: Mapping between internal and external confirmation categories in purchasing.
T163D.lore: Maps internal confirmation categories to external ones in purchasing, keyed by the internal category. The external side validates against T163E; a two-column translation, essentially.

T163E.desc: Definitions of confirmation categories used in purchasing.
T163E.lore: Confirmation categories (EBTYP) in purchasing — the type tag on a vendor confirmation, distinguishing an order acknowledgment from a shipping notice or a delivery date change. Referenced by EKES and PEKES, the confirmation tables that hang off a PO line.

T163F.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing confirmation categories.
T163F.lore: Language-dependent names for purchasing confirmation categories, keyed by language and category. T163E holds the category's control settings.

T163G.desc: Control settings for confirmations in purchasing documents.
T163G.lore: Control settings for confirmations on purchasing documents, keyed by confirmation control key and a sequence number. T163L defines the key, T163E the confirmation categories in the sequence.

T163I.desc: Text descriptions for account assignment categories.
T163I.lore: Language texts for account assignment categories, keyed by language and category. T163K holds the definitions — this is the half of the pair that ends up in dropdowns, printed output and anything a person actually reads.

T163K.desc: Definitions of account assignment categories used in purchasing documents.
T163K.lore: Account assignment categories for purchasing documents (KNTTP) — the setting that decides whether a PO line is charged to stock, a cost center, an order, or a project. Referenced by RESB and RSEG, it's the switch between 'this becomes inventory' and 'this is a direct expense.'

T163KS.desc: Mapping of tax indicators to account assignment categories.
T163KS.lore: Maps tax indicators to account assignment categories per country. T163K defines the account assignment category and TMKK1 the tax indicator — this is the join that makes account assignment tax-aware.

T163L.desc: Definitions of confirmation control keys used in purchasing.
T163L.lore: Confirmation control keys (BSTAE), set on the vendor (LFM1/LFM2) to say which confirmation categories are expected, and in what sequence, for that vendor's purchase orders. This is the setup; T163E's categories are what get plugged into it.

T163M.desc: Text descriptions for confirmation control keys.
T163M.lore: Texts for confirmation control keys, keyed by language and key. T163L defines the control behaviour — what confirmations are expected before a goods receipt — and this describes it in words.

T163P.desc: Definitions of release creation profiles used in purchasing release procedures.
T163P.lore: Definitions of release creation profiles used in purchasing release procedures, keyed by plant and profile. Vendor purchasing data references it via LFM2, plant via T001W, with further profile detail in T163S.

T163S.desc: Text descriptions for release creation profiles.
T163S.lore: Descriptions for release creation profiles, keyed by plant, profile and language. T163P holds the profile itself and T001W the plant — this is only the text.

T163X.lore: No description is recorded, but the key gives it away: language plus item category makes this a text table for purchasing item categories, and nothing more can be said from the input.

T163Y.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing item categories.
T163Y.lore: Descriptions for purchasing item categories, one row per language and category. T163 carries the definitions and the control behaviour behind them; this table supplies only the wording that accompanies the code.

T166A.desc: Supplementary text assignments for purchasing document printouts.
T166A.lore: Supplementary text assignments for purchasing document printouts, keyed by print operation, document category, document type (T161) and the text object and ID. Output configuration rather than anything about the order itself.

T166C.desc: Document change records relevant for reprinting purchasing documents.
T166C.lore: Records which document field changes are relevant for reprinting a purchasing document, keyed by table name and field name. Field-level configuration deciding when a change is worth sending paper again.

T166K.desc: Header text assignments for purchasing document printouts.
T166K.lore: Assigns header texts to purchasing document printouts, keyed by print operation, document category and type plus the text object and ID. Document type via T161 — output configuration, not the text content itself.

T166P.desc: Item-level text assignments for purchasing document printouts.
T166P.lore: Which texts print on purchasing document items, keyed by print operation, document category, document type, item category and the text object and ID. T161 supplies the document type, T163 the item category.

T169D.desc: Amount tolerance check settings used in Invoice Verification.
T169D.lore: Amount tolerance settings for Invoice Verification, keyed by company code, item category and the goods receipt indicator. It defines how much variance is acceptable before anyone has to look.

T169DC.desc: Settings for automatic settlement of planned delivery costs.
T169DC.lore: Settings for automatically settling planned delivery costs, keyed by company code, purchasing organisation and vendor. Because the vendor is in the key, the behaviour can be enabled selectively rather than across the board.

T169H.desc: Definitions of entry profiles used in Logistics Invoice Verification.
T169H.lore: Entry profile definitions for Logistics Invoice Verification, keyed by profile with descriptions in T169HT. The definition is deliberately minimal — the profile is a handle, and the behaviour hangs off it elsewhere.

T169HT.desc: Text descriptions for entry profiles used in Logistics Invoice Verification.
T169HT.lore: Texts for entry profiles in Logistics Invoice Verification, keyed by language and profile. T169H holds the profiles that shape the invoice entry screen; this names them.

T169L.desc: Vendor-specific parameter settings for Invoice Verification.
T169L.lore: Vendor-specific parameter settings for Invoice Verification, keyed by company code and TOGRR, which links through to vendor company code data in LFB1. Company code via T001.

T169P.desc: Configuration parameters for Invoice Verification, one row per company code.
T169P.lore: Invoice Verification parameters, one row per company code. Anything about how invoice posting behaves in that company code that did not merit its own table ends up here.

T169R.desc: Validity period settings for invoice document reorganization.
T169R.lore: Validity period settings for invoice document reorganization, one row per company code. Housekeeping configuration — dull until the day it matters.

T169TC.desc: Definitions of item list display variants for Logistics Invoice Verification.
T169TC.lore: Display variant definitions for the item list in Logistics Invoice Verification, keyed by variant name alone. No client field in the key, so a variant defined here is shared across the whole system rather than per client.

T169TCDATA.desc: Configuration data for item list display variants in Logistics Invoice Verification.
T169TCDATA.lore: Layout configuration for invoice verification item lists, keyed by variant and screen position. Client-independent, and concerned with screen furniture rather than with any invoice.

T169WF01.desc: Release amount thresholds for the invoice parking workflow in Invoice Verification.
T169WF01.lore: Amount thresholds for the invoice parking release workflow, keyed by company code, release group, debit/credit indicator and local currency amount. VBWF08 defines the release group — the table that decides who has to approve which size of invoice.

T169Y.desc: Default aggregation settings for screen variants in Invoice Verification.
T169Y.lore: Default aggregation settings per screen variant in Invoice Verification, keyed by the variant name alone — and with no client field, so the setting applies system-wide.

T16CA.desc: Control settings for change management (DCM) in Purchasing.
T16CA.lore: Control settings for document change management in Purchasing, keyed by document category, document type and purchasing organisation. T161 defines the document type, T024E the purchasing organisation.

T16CE.desc: Configuration for how change documents are displayed in purchasing output control.
T16CE.lore: Configuration for how change documents appear in purchasing output control, keyed by document type, table name and field name. It governs presentation of changes, not the changes themselves.

T16CT.desc: Text descriptions for document change management reason-for-change codes in purchasing.
T16CT.lore: Language texts for the reason-for-change codes used by document change management in purchasing. No table is declared to hold the codes themselves, which makes this the readable face of configuration kept somewhere else.

T16FB.desc: Release indicators used to track the approval status of purchasing documents.
T16FB.lore: Release indicators — the approval states a purchasing document can be in. T16FE links them into the release strategy; this is the small vocabulary the whole release mechanism is built on top of.

T16FC.desc: Release codes used in purchasing document release (approval) strategies.
T16FC.lore: Release codes for purchasing approval strategies — the individual sign-off steps (FRGCO) within a release group (FRGGR). Points to T16FG, the release strategy itself, so this is one level down: the actual codes an approver enters.

T16FD.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing release codes.
T16FD.lore: Texts for purchasing release codes, keyed by language, release group and release code. T16FG defines the group and T16FC the code; this supplies the wording that appears against each approval step.

T16FE.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing document release indicators.
T16FE.lore: Language-dependent texts for purchasing document release indicators, keyed by language and indicator. The indicators themselves are defined in T16FB; this simply makes them readable.

T16FG.desc: Release groups used to categorize purchasing documents subject to a release strategy.
T16FG.lore: Release groups (FRGGR) — the first fork in purchasing's release-strategy tree, before release codes and strategies get involved. Referenced by six T16F* tables, it's config plumbing behind who has to approve a PO before it goes out.

T16FH.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing release groups.
T16FH.lore: Language-dependent names for purchasing release groups, keyed by language and group. T16FG defines the group that the release configuration hangs from.

T16FK.desc: Release statuses used within the purchasing document release strategy.
T16FK.lore: The release statuses within a purchasing release strategy, keyed by release group, strategy and eight release indicator positions. The combinatorial heart of release strategy configuration, and the reason it is hard to read.

T16FS.desc: Release (approval) strategies defined for purchasing documents.
T16FS.lore: Release strategies (FRGSX) within a release group (FRGGR) — the actual approval workflow for a purchasing document, spelling out which codes must sign off and in what combination. Referenced straight from EKKO, it's the rule the system checks before letting a PO go out.

T16FT.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing release strategies.
T16FT.lore: Language texts for purchasing release strategies, keyed by release group and strategy. T16FG defines the groups and T16FS the strategies; this table names the combination the two of them form.

T16FV.desc: Prerequisite conditions between release codes in a purchasing release strategy.
T16FV.lore: Prerequisites between release codes in a purchasing release strategy: which code must already have signed before another may. Pulls together release group (T16FG), strategy (T16FS) and code (T16FC).

T16FW.desc: Assignment of approver roles to purchasing release codes.
T16FW.lore: Assigns approver roles to purchasing release codes per plant, keyed by release group, release code and plant. Turns the abstract release code defined in T16FC into a person or role who actually has to click.

T16LA.desc: Text descriptions for purchase requisition processing statuses.
T16LA.lore: Texts for purchase requisition processing statuses, keyed by language and the update indicator UPDKZ. Descriptions only, with no check table recorded alongside them.

T16LB.desc: Definitions of list scopes used when displaying purchase requisitions.
T16LB.lore: Definitions of list scopes for displaying purchase requisitions, keyed by the scope LSTUB. T16LI and T16LL hold the detail under the same key, making this the header of that display configuration.

T16LD.desc: Routines controlling the structure of purchase requisition lists.
T16LD.lore: Routines controlling how purchase requisition lists are structured, keyed by list. T16LL defines the list; this says how it gets built.

T16LI.desc: Data retrieval settings tied to purchase requisition list scopes.
T16LI.lore: Data retrieval settings tied to purchase requisition list scopes, keyed by scope and a sequence number. T16LB defines the scopes themselves — this is what says, per scope, which data actually gets fetched.

T16LL.desc: Routines controlling purchase requisition list scopes.
T16LL.lore: Routines controlling the scope of purchase requisition lists, keyed by scope-of-list and a sequence number. T16LB defines the scope and T16LD the list; this sequences what actually gets executed.

T170.desc: Copy control settings for texts used in purchasing documents.
T170.lore: Copy control for texts in purchasing documents, keyed by target object and identifier, source object and identifier, and text name. Determines which text travels from which predecessor document into which successor.

T179T.desc: Text descriptions for material product hierarchy nodes.
T179T.lore: Language-dependent texts for material product hierarchy nodes, keyed by language and the hierarchy code. The wording behind a hierarchy level, and nothing more.

T500L.desc: Country grouping assignments used in personnel administration.
T500L.lore: Just the list of country groupings (MOLGA) — the code SAP HR uses to localize payroll and personnel rules per country. Referenced from org management (HRP1005, HRP1501...), personnel areas, even travel management (PTRV_HEAD). Tiny table, wide reach.

T500P.desc: Master data for personnel areas within the HR organizational structure.
T500P.lore: Personnel areas (PERSA) — the HR-specific division of a company code, sitting one level below it in the org structure. Points back to T001 (company code) and T500L (country grouping), so it's the join between legal entity and HR administration.

T502T.desc: Text descriptions for marital status codes used in personnel data.
T502T.lore: The language-dependent text for marital status codes (SPRSL plus FAMST) — just the labels, not the codes themselves. Referenced from PA0106 and PA0394, the personnel infotypes that actually store an employee's status.

T503.desc: Combinations of employee group and employee subgroup used in personnel administration.

T508A.desc: Work schedule rules used in personnel time management.

T510.desc: Pay scale groups used for employee compensation.

T510A.desc: Pay scale types used in payroll compensation structures.
T510A.lore: Pay scale types (TRFAR), defined per country grouping (MOLGA) — referenced by compensation infotypes like PA0008 and by organizational management tables (HRP1005, HRP1501). Part of the config chain behind what an employee is paid, one level up from the actual pay numbers.

T510F.desc: Assignment of currencies to pay scale types.

T510W.desc: Assignment of period parameters to pay scale types.

T511.desc: Master data for wage types used in payroll.

T512W.desc: Valuation rules applied to wage types during payroll processing.
T512W.lore: Valuation rules for wage types — how a given LGART actually gets computed during payroll, per country grouping (MOLGA). Sits right next to T512Z, which controls whether a wage type is allowed at all; this one governs what happens once it is.

T512Z.desc: Rules defining which wage types are permitted for each infotype.
T512Z.lore: The rulebook for which wage types (LGART) are permitted on which infotype (INFTY), per country grouping (MOLGA). It's config, not data — but if a wage type isn't listed here, payroll simply won't let anyone enter it.

T527X.desc: Master data for organizational units in HR organizational management.

T528T.desc: Text descriptions for HR organizational positions.

T529T.desc: Text descriptions for personnel action types.

T529U.desc: Status values used together with personnel actions.

T530.lore: The reason codes behind every personnel action — why someone was hired, transferred, or let go, not just that it happened. PA0000, the core Actions infotype, points straight at it, along with PA0044 and PA0302.

T530T.desc: Text descriptions for personnel action reason codes.

T549Q.desc: Payroll period definitions used in payroll processing.

T550A.desc: Daily work schedule definitions used in personnel time management.
T550A.lore: Daily work schedule rules for time management — a deep key (program, variant, sequence, end date) because schedules can vary and change over time. PA2003 and two other T550/T552 tables all build on it.

T550S.desc: Text descriptions for daily work schedules.

T551S.desc: Text descriptions for period work schedules.

T552A.desc: Monthly work schedule definitions used in personnel time management.

T554C.desc: Valuation rules for absence types in time management.

T554S.desc: Master data for attendance and absence types.
T554S.lore: The catalog of attendance and absence types (MOABW). Referenced by the time infotypes PA2001/PA2002 and by CATSHR (cross-application time sheets), so every logged absence or attendance record points back to a type defined here.

T554T.desc: Text descriptions for attendance and absence types.

T558B.desc: Payroll periods used for payroll account transfer processing.

T569V.desc: Control records used within HR to track processing status.

T5K8C.desc: Staging data for transferring external payroll results into Canada-specific payroll tables.

T605U.desc: Foreign trade rules converting export and import business transaction types between countries, one row per country pair and transaction type.
T605U.lore: Conversion rules for foreign trade business transaction types between an export and an import country, one row per country pair and type pair. It exists because the two sides of a border classify the same movement differently.

T605Z.desc: Foreign trade default proposals for the business transaction type in export, one row per country, sales organization, distribution channel, and item category.
T605Z.lore: Foreign trade default proposals for the export business transaction type, per country, sales organization, distribution channel and item category. Defaulting configuration — it proposes, the document disposes.

T616U.desc: Foreign trade rules converting import and export procedures between countries, one row per country pair and procedure.
T616U.lore: Conversion rules mapping an export procedure in one country to the corresponding import procedure in another, one row per country pair and procedure. A pure translation table for cross-border document flows, with no declared neighbours.

T616Z.desc: Foreign trade default proposals for the procedure used in export, one row per country, sales organization, distribution channel, division, and item category.
T616Z.lore: Default proposals for the export procedure in foreign trade, cut by country, sales area and item category. Determination config: it answers which procedure applies before anyone has to choose one manually.

T687.desc: Account keys used for automatic account determination in accounting.
T687.lore: Account keys used in automatic account determination, keyed by application and account key. T687T names them and KONV carries them on condition lines — the token that connects a pricing condition to an account.

T687T.desc: Text descriptions for account determination account keys.
T687T.lore: Names for account determination account keys, keyed by language, application and key. The keys themselves live in T687; this is the readable layer over them.

T691A.desc: Risk categories used in customer credit management.
T691A.lore: Risk categories for customer credit management, scored within a credit control area (KKBER). Referenced by KNKK (the credit master) and sales documents like VBAK, so this label is ultimately what decides how much rope a customer gets before an order gets blocked.

T691B.desc: Credit management groups used to group customers for credit control.
T691B.lore: Credit management groups used to bunch customers together for credit control, keyed by group and credit control area. KNKK references the group from the customer side; T014 defines the credit control area.

T691C.desc: Text descriptions for credit management groups.
T691C.lore: Text descriptions for credit management groups, per credit control area, group and language. T691B supplies the credit control area context.

T691F.desc: Control settings for automatic customer credit checks.
T691F.lore: Control settings for automatic customer credit checks, keyed by credit control area, check rule and a credit manager grouping. T014 supplies the credit control area and T691A the rule — this decides how the check actually behaves.

T691H.desc: Document value classes used to group document amounts in credit management.
T691H.lore: Document value classes for credit management — the groupings used to bucket document amounts within a credit control area (T014). Config that shapes how exposure is reported rather than how it is calculated.

T691K.desc: Forms of payment guarantee used in credit management, such as letters of credit.
T691K.lore: Forms of payment security (ABSFO) — letters of credit, bank guarantees, and similar — used in credit management on higher-risk sales. Referenced on sales document lines (VBAP, VBRP), it's what a risky order gets backed by instead of open credit.

T691L.desc: Text descriptions for payment guarantee forms.
T691L.lore: Texts for payment guarantee forms, keyed by language and form. T691K defines the forms; this is the language layer over them.

T691M.desc: Procedures defining how payment guarantees are handled.
T691M.lore: Payment guarantee procedures (ABSSC) — the processing logic layered on top of T691K's guarantee forms. Sits on the delivery (LIKP) and the sales document (VBKD), governing how a secured order actually gets handled downstream.

T691N.desc: Text descriptions for payment guarantee procedures.
T691N.lore: Language-dependent texts for payment guarantee procedures, keyed by language and procedure. The procedure itself is defined in T691M, so this contributes wording rather than control.

T691O.desc: Control settings for payment guarantee procedures.
T691O.lore: Control settings for payment guarantee procedures, keyed by guarantee category and a sequence. T691M defines the category and T691K the form reached via ABSFO.

T691P.desc: Assignment of payment guarantee procedures to customers.
T691P.lore: Payment guarantee procedure assigned per customer — KABSS is the procedure code sitting on KNVV, the customer's sales data. This is credit-management config: how a customer's payment gets secured before goods ship.

T691Q.desc: Text descriptions for customer payment guarantee procedures.
T691Q.lore: Text descriptions for customer payment guarantee procedures, one row per procedure and language. T691P holds the procedure being named.

T691R.desc: Assignment of payment guarantee procedures to documents.
T691R.lore: The document-level counterpart to T691P — same payment guarantee procedure concept, but assigned per document (BABSS) rather than per customer. TVAK, the sales document type table, references it.

T691S.desc: Text descriptions for document payment guarantee procedures.
T691S.lore: Language texts for document payment guarantee procedures, one row per language and procedure. T691R holds the procedures themselves; this is the descriptive half of a very conventional configuration pair.

T691T.desc: Text descriptions for credit management risk classes.
T691T.lore: Descriptions of credit management risk classes, per language and credit control area (T691A). A text table, but scoped by control area, so the same risk class code can be labelled differently in different areas.

T691U.desc: Determination rules for selecting a payment guarantee procedure.
T691U.lore: Determination rules for picking a payment guarantee procedure, keyed by a customer-side and a document-side determination key drawn from T691P and T691R, resolving to a procedure in T691M. Classic two-axis determination.

T6WP4.desc: Check table listing valid label shapes for inventory management.
T6WP4.lore: A check table listing valid label shapes for inventory management, keyed by the shape code. Materials reference it via ETIFO in MARA — domain-style configuration with a table behind it.

T702O.desc: Assignment of trip countries to trip regions and country groups in travel management.
T702O.lore: Maps each country to its trip region and country group inside Travel Management — the geography layer that lets travel rules apply per-region instead of country by country. PTRV_HEAD, the travel header table, relies on it.

T706B1.desc: Master data for travel expense types.
T706B1.lore: Master data for travel expense types, keyed by trip provision variant, expense type and a validity end date — so expense types are time-dependent by design. T007A supplies the tax code applied to them.

T706F.desc: Flat-rate amounts used for travel cost provisions.
T706F.lore: Flat-rate amounts for travel cost provisions, with a key spanning trip provision variant, trip type, country, region, area, vehicle class and a validity end date. Rate configuration where nearly every dimension is part of the key.

T706K.desc: Initial account assignment defaults for posting travel expenses to FI.
T706K.lore: Initial account assignment defaults for posting travel expenses to FI, keyed by trip provision variant, wage type, user group and an end date. The end date in the key means these defaults are versioned over time rather than replaced.

T706V.desc: Meal per-diem and maximum reimbursement amounts based on trip duration in hours.
T706V.lore: Meal per-diem and maximum reimbursement amounts by trip duration, carrying one of the widest keys in Travel Management: trip provision variant, several indicators, country group, region (T702O), area, reason codes, days, hours and an end date.

T74FA.desc: Master data for benefits insurance plans.

T74FC.desc: Coverage rules for benefits insurance plans.

T7EHS00_ANSWTYP.desc: Unique identifiers for answer types used in EHS questionnaires.
T7EHS00_ANSWTYP.lore: Answer type codes for EHS questionnaires — the format a response takes (QANSW_TYPE), whatever those turn out to be: free text, yes/no, or otherwise. Referenced from the question catalog and generation tables, it defines what kind of response a given question expects.

T7EHS00_ANSWTYPT.desc: Language-dependent text descriptions for EHS answer types.

T7EHS00_CATALOG.desc: Question catalog definitions used in EHS questionnaires.
T7EHS00_CATALOG.lore: The question catalogs themselves in EHS questionnaires — the named sets that questions and groups get organized into. Points to QRELTYP, tying a catalog back to what kind of object it's meant to be used against.

T7EHS00_CATALOGT.desc: Language-specific text descriptions for EHS question catalogs.

T7EHS00_FKATALOG.desc: Administrative data for EHS question catalogs.

T7EHS00_FORMTYP.desc: Form types used in EHS questionnaires.
T7EHS00_FORMTYP.lore: The catalog of form types available to EHS questionnaires — the shape a questionnaire can take before anyone starts asking safety questions. Four questionnaire-related tables, including the form and category tables, hang off it.

T7EHS00_FORMTYPT.desc: Text descriptions for EHS form types.

T7EHS00_QAGEN.desc: General answer types used in EHS questionnaires.

T7EHS00_QAGENT.desc: Text descriptions for general answer types used in EHS questionnaires.

T7EHS00_QASPC.desc: Assignment of EHS answer types to application components.

T7EHS00_QASPCT.desc: Text descriptions for the application components assigned to EHS answer types.

T7EHS00_QCATFORM.desc: Assignment of forms and programs to EHS question catalogs.

T7EHS00_QCRIT.desc: Language-specific criterion text for EHS question catalogs.

T7EHS00_QCUSREX.desc: Registered user-exit function modules for EHS question catalogs.

T7EHS00_QEDITOR.desc: Language-specific question text for EHS question catalogs.

T7EHS00_QFORM.desc: Assignment of forms and programs to specific EHS questionnaires.

T7EHS00_QFORMSPC.desc: Assignment of forms and programs to general EHS questionnaires.

T7EHS00_QGROUP.desc: Unique identifiers for EHS question catalog groups.
T7EHS00_QGROUP.lore: Question groups within an EHS catalog — the folder layer between a catalog and its individual questions. A straightforward hierarchy: catalog contains groups, groups contain questions, both traceable back through the same QUEST_CATALOG key.

T7EHS00_QGROUPT.desc: Language-dependent text descriptions for EHS question catalog groups.

T7EHS00_QREL.desc: Assignment of EHS questionnaires to objects.

T7EHS00_QRELTYP.desc: Object types used when assigning EHS questionnaires.
T7EHS00_QRELTYP.lore: The object types an EHS questionnaire can be assigned to. Small config table, but eight other T7EHS00_* tables reference it — catalogs, forms, custom extensions — so it's the pivot point for how a questionnaire attaches to a real object.

T7EHS00_QRELTYPT.desc: Text descriptions for the object types used in EHS questionnaire assignments.

T7EHS00_QSCANP.desc: File path configuration for EHS scanner ASCII files.

T7EHS00_QTFIELDG.desc: Text field definitions used in the general EHS questionnaire.

T7EHS00_QTFIELDS.desc: Text field definitions used in a specific EHS questionnaire.

T7EHS00_QTOPIC.desc: Subject areas defined for general EHS questionnaires.

T7EHS00_QTOPICT.desc: Text descriptions for general EHS questionnaire subject areas.

T7EHS00_QTOP_REL.desc: Assignment of subject areas to EHS questionnaires.

T7EHS00_QTYPE.desc: Categories used to classify EHS question catalog entries.

T7EHS00_QTYPET.desc: Text descriptions for EHS question catalog entry categories.

T7EHS00_QUEST.lore: One row per EHS questionnaire — the definition, not the answers. It links out to a relation type and a status table, so a questionnaire here is a live object with state, not just a static form.

T7EHS00_QUESTQ.desc: Individual questions contained within an EHS questionnaire.

T7EHS00_QUESTT.desc: Language-dependent text descriptions for EHS questionnaire definitions.

T7EHS00_QUSREX.desc: Registered user-exit function modules for EHS questionnaires.

T7EHS00_STATUST.desc: Language-specific text descriptions for EHS status values.

T852.lore: Subgroup definitions for consolidation — how entities get bucketed below the main consolidation group. The FILC* tables (consolidation line items) reference it, and it links to TCURC since consolidation always has a currency dimension.

T853.desc: Status codes assigned to companies for consolidation processing.
T853.lore: Status of a company within consolidation processing, keyed by ledger, version, company, year and period. Tracks how far a given consolidation unit has got in a given period rather than holding any values.

T856.lore: Transaction type codes used in consolidation — a tag on a posting for what kind of consolidation-relevant event it represents. Referenced from BSEG up through the special-ledger totals tables (FAGLFLEXA, FAGLFLEXT, FILCA, FILCP, FILCT), so it threads from the raw line item into consolidated reporting.

T856T.desc: Text descriptions for consolidation transaction types.
T856T.lore: Names for consolidation transaction types, keyed by language and type. The text companion to the definitions in T856, and of no use on its own without them.

T881.desc: Master data defining a general ledger, one row per ledger.
T881.lore: Ledger master data, one row per ledger. A short table with a foundational role: anything posting to a specific ledger identifies it by the RLDNR defined here.

T882G.desc: Assignment of company codes to general ledgers, one row per company code and ledger.
T882G.lore: Which ledgers a company code posts to, one row per company code and ledger. A small table with large consequences — it decides where a posting is recorded at all.

T8G04.desc: Rules for deriving line item categories from general ledger accounts.
T8G04.lore: Rules deriving a line item category from the general ledger account. No key fields are recorded in the catalogue, so all that can honestly be said is what it derives and what it derives it from.

T8G05.desc: Rules for deriving allocation activities from a procedure and process combination.
T8G05.lore: Derivation rules that turn a procedure and process combination into an allocation activity. No key fields are carried in the source, so the description is effectively the whole story here.

T8G06.desc: Dimensions used for allocation, account balancing, and consolidation.
T8G06.lore: Dimensions used for allocation, account balancing and consolidation. No key fields are recorded here, so beyond that stated purpose there is nothing solid to add.

T8G08.desc: Assignment of document types to business processes.
T8G08.lore: Assigns document types to business processes. No key fields or relationships are recorded for it, so beyond that mapping role there is nothing further to say.

T8G30B.desc: Account determination settings for document allocation based on posting key.
T8G30B.lore: Account determination for document allocation, keyed by chart of accounts, line type, account determination key, account and a cash relevance flag. T004 gives the chart of accounts and SKA1 the account it resolves to.

T8G30C.desc: Account determination settings for document allocation based on account.
T8G30C.lore: Account determination for document allocation driven by the account. No key fields are recorded for it here, so its structure cannot be described from the input, only its purpose.

TA20BUILDING.desc: Master data for buildings used in travel management.
TA20BUILDING.lore: Building master data used by travel management, one row per building number. A small reference table with no declared relationships — it names buildings so that other travel data can point at them.

TA20C.lore: Enterprise-specific categories for travel services, keyed by user group and category. FTPT_ITEM points back at it, so the classification applied to a travel request item is defined right here.

TA20SWITCH.desc: Global control parameters used in travel management.
TA20SWITCH.lore: Global control parameters for Travel Management, keyed by an attribute name. A generic switch table: one row per setting, whatever the setting happens to be.

TA22B.desc: Assignment of trips to travel plan types.
TA22B.lore: Assigns trips to travel plan types, keyed by MOREI, the plan type and KZTKT. Small Travel Management configuration linking how a trip is classified to how it is planned.

TABKT.desc: Automatic G/L accounts used for year-end closing and opening postings (Italy).
TABKT.lore: Automatic G/L accounts for year-end closing and opening postings under Italian requirements, keyed by company code and account. T003 supplies the document type and T074U the special G/L indicator.

TABWA.desc: Mapping of asset transaction types to depreciation areas, one row per chart of depreciation, transaction type, and depreciation area.
TABWA.lore: Maps asset transaction types to depreciation areas, one row per chart of depreciation, transaction type and area. It decides which areas a given transaction type actually touches.

TABWV.desc: Default asset transaction types assigned to a business transaction, one row per business transaction.
TABWV.lore: The default asset transaction type assigned to a business transaction, one row per transaction. A defaulting table in the strictest sense: it decides what gets proposed, not what ultimately gets posted.

TAKOF.desc: Reconciliation accounts configured with exception handling rules.
TAKOF.lore: Reconciliation accounts configured with exception handling, keyed by company code and account. SKB1 holds the account itself; appearing in this list is what marks a particular account as special.

TASSIGN_MM_T001W.desc: Auxiliary table holding status information for an SD maintenance view, keyed by sales organization.
TASSIGN_MM_T001W.lore: An auxiliary status table for a maintenance view, keyed by valuation area with T001W as the plant behind it. Housekeeping around a maintenance view rather than data anyone posts to.

TBAER.desc: Rules governing which fields of a posted document can be changed.
TBAER.lore: Rules governing which fields of an already-posted document may still be changed, keyed by account type, UMSKS, field name and company code. Company code via T001 — the gatekeeper on document corrections.

TBERG.desc: Authorization check settings maintained in the ABAP Dictionary.
TBERG.lore: Authorization check settings maintained in the Dictionary, keyed by an authorization-relevant user key. Administrative configuration — it governs checking rather than taking part in any posting.

TBKOW.desc: Temporary storage for valuation differences arising in accounting.
TBKOW.lore: Temporary storage for valuation differences, keyed by company code, currency type, local currency type, reversal flag, account, business area, currency and trading partner. Working data for a valuation run rather than a ledger.

TBSG.desc: Reason codes for ordering used in purchasing documents.
TBSG.lore: Reason codes for ordering, used on purchasing documents and keyed by the code alone. TBSGT carries the language texts, which makes this the definition side of a very small two-table configuration pair.

TBSGT.desc: Text descriptions for purchasing order reason codes.
TBSGT.lore: Descriptions for the purchasing order reason codes defined in TBSG, per language. A small text table sitting behind a field people fill in far more often than they ever configure it.

TBSLT.desc: Text descriptions (names) for posting keys used in accounting documents.
TBSLT.lore: Names for posting keys, keyed by language, posting key and special G/L indicator. T074U supplies the special G/L side, which is why the indicator sits in the key alongside the posting key.

TBUVTX.desc: Settings for transfer-posting tax in cross-company-code transactions.
TBUVTX.lore: Settings for how tax is transfer-posted in cross-company-code transactions, keyed by the two company codes involved and a tax code. Narrow configuration for a narrow but reliably painful problem.

TCG01.desc: System table defining specification value assignment categories in EHS.

TCG02.desc: Text descriptions for EHS specification value assignment categories.

TCG31.desc: Specification types used in EHS product safety specifications.
TCG31.lore: Defines the types a product safety specification can be — the classification layer under EHS specs. It points to TCG51, the property tree, so type determines which properties even apply to a given specification.

TCG36.desc: Authorization objects used to control access to EHS specifications.
TCG36.lore: The authorization group that gates who can see or edit an EHS specification — AUTHGRP is what gets checked. ESTRH, a core specification table, carries it, so access control is baked into the specification structure itself.

TCG46.desc: Source Environment, Health and Safety - Product Safety.

TCG51.desc: Property tree structure used to organize EHS specification properties.
TCG51.lore: The tree that organizes every property an EHS specification can carry — the folder structure for chemical and product attributes. TCG31 and three other EHS tables reference it to know where their properties sit.

TCG52.desc: Language-dependent text descriptions for the EHS property tree.

TCG53.desc: Property Tree - Value Assignment Type Assignment.

TCG76.desc: Component categories used in EHS specifications.

TCG86.desc: Characteristic ratings used in EHS specification management.
TCG86.lore: The rating values assigned to characteristics inside EHS specification management. It's a small lookup table, but four other EHS tables depend on it to interpret what a characteristic's value actually means.

TCG96.desc: Usage profiles used in EHS specification management.
TCG96.lore: Usage profiles that control how an EHS specification can be used downstream. Referenced by /TDAG/CPC_SREG, so the Compliance for Products add-on relies on core EHS usage profiles rather than defining its own.

TCGA7.desc: Language- Dependent Description for Report Symbol.

TCGC3.desc: Exchange profiles used in EHS specification data exchange.

TCGL1.desc: Report types used in EHS reporting.

TCGL3.desc: Applications associated with EHS report types.

TCULIV.desc: Customizing settings for direct postings in logistics invoice verification.
TCULIV.lore: Customizing for direct postings in logistics invoice verification, keyed by client alone, so one row governs the whole client. Global switches, nothing per company code or document.

TCURD.desc: Maximum allowed exchange rate differences for postings made in foreign currency.
TCURD.lore: The maximum exchange rate difference tolerated per foreign/local currency pair. A guardrail table: it exists to catch a rate that has been entered wrong before the posting inherits it.

TDG101.desc: Status values for dangerous goods packaging.

TDG102.desc: Text descriptions for dangerous goods packaging statuses.

TDG41.desc: Dangerous goods indicator profiles assigned in the material master.

TDG85.desc: Assignment profiles for exceptions to dangerous goods regulations.
TDG85.lore: Exception profiles for dangerous goods regulations — named override sets that let specific shipments bypass standard DG rules. DGTEXCEPT, the exceptions table itself, is built on top of it.

TDG86.desc: Text descriptions for dangerous goods regulation exception assignment profiles.

TDG87.desc: Assignment of objects to dangerous goods regulation exception assignment profiles.

TDG95.desc: Definitions of dangerous goods packaging levels.
TDG95.lore: Packaging levels for dangerous goods — a hierarchy keyed by DG_PACKAGE_LEVEL. VEKP, the handling unit header, references it directly, so DG packaging rules attach right down to the physical handling unit.

TDG96.desc: Text descriptions for dangerous goods packaging levels.

TDGA1.desc: Settings for activating dangerous goods checks.

TDGC5.desc: Dangerous goods management profiles assigned to sales and distribution documents.
TDGC5.lore: The dangerous goods profile attached to a sales document — PROLI drives which DG checks and rules apply. Both VBAK (sales order header) and LIKP (delivery header) carry it, so the profile follows the document from order through delivery.

TDGC6.desc: Text descriptions for dangerous goods indicator profiles used in the delivery header.

TDGD1.desc: Definitions of dangerous goods regulation profiles.
TDGD1.lore: Dangerous goods regulation profiles (DGRPROF) — the named rule sets a shipment gets checked against. Referenced by delivery item tables like LIPSDG and LIPSDGAP, it's the profile assigned before a shipment can be evaluated for dangerous goods compliance.

TDGD2.desc: Text descriptions for dangerous goods regulation profiles.

TDGD5.desc: Definitions of dangerous goods indicator categories.

TDGD7.desc: Assignment of dangerous goods indicator categories to regulation profiles.

TEMPLATE_HEADER.desc: Header data for purchasing document templates.
TEMPLATE_HEADER.lore: Header for purchasing document templates, keyed by business object type, user and a GUID. The user in the key makes templates personal — they belong to whoever saved them rather than to the organisation.

TEURB.desc: Alternative local currency and exchange rate type defined for a company code.
TEURB.lore: Alternative local currency and exchange rate type for a company code, additionally keyed by program and country. The program in the key means the same company code can be treated differently by different reports.

TF123.desc: Additional rules controlling execution of the SAPF124 clearing program.
TF123.lore: Additional rules controlling how the SAPF124 clearing program behaves, keyed by chart of accounts, account type and account. Fine-grained overrides on top of the program's standard selection logic.

TFAGM.desc: Assignment of clients and account types to clearing rules.
TFAGM.lore: Assigns clients and account types to clearing rules, keyed by account type. Tiny configuration, and the client sitting in the key means the assignment can differ from client to client.

TFAV.desc: Work lists used in financial accounting.
TFAV.lore: Work lists used in Financial Accounting, keyed by object and identifier, with TFAVT holding their names. It defines a named set of objects to be worked through rather than the working itself.

TFAVA.desc: Detail data for automatically generated financial accounting work lists.
TFAVA.lore: Detail rows for automatically generated FI worklists, keyed by object, table name and field name. It describes what a worklist selects on rather than holding what the worklist found.

TFAVT.desc: Text descriptions (names) for financial accounting work lists.
TFAVT.lore: Names for financial accounting work lists, per language, keyed by object and identifier. TFAV holds the work lists themselves; this table exists so that an identifier can be presented as something meaningful.

TFAVW.desc: Values contained in financial accounting work lists.
TFAVW.lore: The values held inside FI work lists — object, identifier and the value itself all in the key. Generic storage, so what a row means depends entirely on what its object says it is.

TFBUF.desc: Buffer table used to temporarily store financial accounting data.
TFBUF.lore: A buffer for financial accounting data in flight, keyed by user name, application key and a sequence number. Keyed by who is working rather than by any business object, which tells you it is temporary by design.

TFE05.lore: No description is recorded. The key is company code plus account, which places it among FI settings held at account level, but the input offers nothing more specific than that.

TFE18.desc: Identification of offsetting accounts used in BDF reporting.
TFE18.lore: Identifies the offsetting accounts used in BDF reporting, keyed by company code and offsetting account. A small mapping consulted at reporting time.

TFE19.desc: Accounts that are subject to BDF reporting requirements.
TFE19.lore: Which G/L accounts are subject to BDF reporting, per company code and account. An inclusion list for a country-specific reporting obligation, and nothing more.

TGSB.desc: Master data for business areas used in financial accounting.
TGSB.lore: The master list of business areas (GSBER). Referenced by 48 tables — asset accounting (ANLZ), document line items (BSEG, BSID, BSIK, BSIS), CO postings (COEP) — so nearly every financial posting can carry one, even though the table itself is little more than a code.

TGSBG.desc: Cross-system business area definitions used across multiple SAP systems.
TGSBG.lore: Global business area definitions shared across multiple SAP systems, keyed by the global business area code. TGSB and TGSBH key on that same code, making this the cross-system anchor for what is otherwise a local concept.

TGSBH.desc: Text descriptions for cross-system business areas.
TGSBH.lore: Descriptions for the cross-system business areas defined in TGSBG, per language. A text table for the global counterpart of the ordinary business area.

TGSBK.lore: Business areas as used in consolidation, keyed by a consolidation business area that maps back to TGSB. A translation list between the operational business area and the consolidation view of it.

TGSBT.lore: Names for business areas, keyed by language and business area. The business area itself is defined in TGSB; this table exists purely to supply the wording behind the code.

THKON.desc: Permitted alternative general ledger accounts for posting.
THKON.lore: The alternative G/L accounts a posting is permitted to use, keyed by chart of accounts, account and the alternative account. SKA1 validates the accounts and T004 the chart — a permission list rather than a substitution instruction.

THM071.desc: Risk potential classifications used for dangerous goods.

THM072.desc: Text descriptions for dangerous goods risk potential classifications.

TIBAN_WO_ACCNO.desc: Activation settings for using an IBAN without a separate bank account number.
TIBAN_WO_ACCNO.lore: A single per-client switch activating the use of an IBAN without a separate bank account number. One row, one decision, keyed by client alone.

TINPA.desc: Index update settings for sales documents linked to a business partner.
TINPA.lore: Controls whether the sales document index is updated for a given transaction group and partner function. Configuration that decides what gets indexed rather than holding any index data of its own.

TINSB.desc: Alternative bank accounts used by program RFBITB01.
TINSB.lore: Alternative bank accounts used by the one program named in its description, keyed by company code and bank. SKA1 supplies the account and T001 the company code — narrow, program-scoped configuration.

TINSO.desc: Records of failed payment transactions and their associated charges.
TINSO.lore: Failed payment transactions and the charges they incurred, keyed by report, company code, document, year and an item counter. Customer via KNA1, charge account via SKA1, house bank via T012 — the returned-payment paper trail.

TKSF.desc: Value table for the statistics group used in sales activity processing at header level.
TKSF.lore: A value table for the statistics group used at header level in sales activity processing, keyed by the group code alone. Domain-fixing configuration with nothing hanging off it.

TLSEP.desc: Control settings for how lists are separated.
TLSEP.lore: Control settings for how lists are separated, keyed by domain and value. Presentation configuration expressed against Dictionary domains rather than against any business object.

TMBW1.desc: Key values used to identify sources of supply in purchasing.
TMBW1.lore: The source of supply key values used in purchasing, keyed by BWSCL — the same field that appears on the material master in MARA and MARC, which is why it needs a check table here.

TMED.desc: Various control parameters used in purchasing.
TMED.lore: A grab-bag of purchasing control parameters, keyed by a business transaction code. The description promises little and the key promises less: one row per transaction, carrying whatever that transaction happens to need.

TMKE.desc: Grouping of purchasing organizations for calculation schema determination.
TMKE.lore: Groups purchasing organizations for calculation schema determination, keyed by the schema group alone. TMKS shares the key, so this is the vocabulary that the determination itself is expressed in.

TMKK1.desc: Account assignment settings for taxes in purchasing.
TMKK1.lore: Tax account assignment settings in purchasing, keyed by country and a tax indicator key. T163KS supplies the indicator and TMKK1T its text; the country in the key makes this national tax handling configuration.

TMKK1T.desc: Text descriptions for the tax account assignment settings in purchasing.
TMKK1T.lore: Language-dependent texts for the tax account assignment settings in purchasing, keyed by language, country and the indicator. Country via T005, the setting itself in TMKK1.

TMKKT.desc: Text descriptions for vendor calculation schema groups.
TMKKT.lore: Language-dependent names for vendor calculation schema groups, keyed by language and group. Text companion to the schema grouping used on the vendor side.

TMKM1.desc: Tax settings for materials in purchasing.
TMKM1.lore: Tax settings for materials in purchasing, keyed by country and tax indicator. MLAN carries the same indicator on the material side, T005 validates the country.

TMKS.desc: Calculation schemas used in purchasing pricing determination.
TMKS.lore: Calculation schema determination for purchasing pricing, keyed by the schema group of the purchasing organisation and that of the vendor. T683 holds the schema it resolves to; TMKE the organisation-side group.

TMKW1.desc: Tax settings maintained at plant level for purchasing.
TMKW1.lore: Plant-level tax settings for purchasing, keyed by country (T005) and a tax indicator. One of the several small determination tables that between them decide how tax is handled on a purchase.

TMMPURCFOLDERS.desc: Configuration for cFolders collaboration folder integration in purchasing.
TMMPURCFOLDERS.lore: Configuration for cFolders collaboration integration in purchasing, keyed by a URL. When the key field is an address, the table's job is pointing somewhere else.

TMM_BWEXT.desc: Administrative data for Business Warehouse extractors in purchasing.
TMM_BWEXT.lore: Administrative data for Business Warehouse extractors in purchasing, mapping an application and table to an extract table. Note the absent client field — this is system-wide registration data.

TMPPF.desc: Control profile settings for managing manufacturer part numbers.
TMPPF.lore: The control profile for manufacturer part number handling, keyed by MPROF. MARA references the profile from the material master and TMPPFT holds its description, so this row is the behaviour a material points at.

TMPPFT.desc: Text descriptions for manufacturer part number control profiles.
TMPPFT.lore: Text descriptions for manufacturer part number control profiles, by language. TMPPF holds the profile; this table only names it.

TMRM007A.desc: Tax codes used in invoice verification and invoice entry for financial accounting.
TMRM007A.lore: Tax codes usable during invoice verification and FI invoice entry, keyed by calculation procedure, tax code and account type. A filtering list narrowing what may be entered, rather than the tax code definition itself.

TMSI1.desc: Sub-item categories used in purchasing documents.
TMSI1.lore: Sub-item categories for purchasing documents, keyed by sub-item type. TMSI2 groups them, and RBDRSEG carries the same type through into invoice verification — so the category outlives the purchase order.

TMSI2.desc: Processing keys used for purchasing sub-items.
TMSI2.lore: Processing keys for purchasing sub-items, keyed by a sub-item group. TMSI1 and TMSI3 both hang off the same group key, so this is one of three tables sharing a single control concept.

TMSI3.desc: Field copy rules from a main item to its sub-item in purchasing.
TMSI3.lore: Field copy rules from a main purchasing item to its sub-item, keyed by rule group plus the field names involved. The group is defined in TMSI2; this holds the field-by-field detail underneath it.

TMY05.desc: Directory of versions used for LIFO inventory valuation.
TMY05.lore: Version directory for LIFO valuation runs, sitting underneath T001Y's valuation levels. Effectively tracks which LIFO calculation pass a given value belongs to — plumbing for inventory valuation history, not something anyone browses directly.

TNAD9.desc: Printer assignment for output determination by storage location and user group.
TNAD9.lore: Printer determination for output, keyed by application, output type, plant, storage location and user group. Where the paper comes out, decided down to storage location; T685 defines the output type.

TPAKD.desc: Valid account groups per partner function for business partners.
TPAKD.lore: Which customer account groups are valid for which partner function. A permissibility table — it constrains what can be entered rather than storing anything itself. T077D defines the account groups.

TPOOL.desc: Master data for LIFO valuation pools.
TPOOL.lore: The pool a material belongs to for LIFO valuation — materials get grouped into pools so they're valued together rather than one at a time. MBEW, material valuation itself, points here, and it links out to T001Y for further configuration.

TPRIT.desc: Text descriptions for customer delivery priorities.
TPRIT.lore: Language texts for customer delivery priorities, keyed by language and priority code. Nothing is declared alongside it — the priority values are referenced from documents, and only the wording lives here.

TPTMT.desc: Text descriptions for material item category groups in the material master.
TPTMT.lore: Descriptions for the material item category groups held on the material master, per language. A text table with nothing modelled around it — the group itself lives on the material, not here.

TR01T.desc: Text descriptions related to base table reorganization.
TR01T.lore: Texts relating to base table reorganization, keyed by language, application and object. Descriptive wording for a housekeeping activity rather than anything financial.

TRAS.desc: Interval definitions for days in arrears on overdue items.
TRAS.lore: Definitions of the days-in-arrears intervals used to bucket overdue items, keyed by interval ID. Texts live in TRAST — analysis configuration rather than accounting data.

TRAST.lore: Names for days-in-arrears intervals, keyed by language and interval ID. TRAS defines the intervals themselves; this is the label shown against each band.

TRERI.desc: Additional days used when posting remaining credit risk.
TRERI.lore: Additional days applied when posting remaining credit risk, per company code and policy key. A small numeric setting with a very specific job in an otherwise unrelated process.

TREV.desc: Field status variant settings used for funds reservation.
TREV.lore: Field status variant settings used for funds reservation, one row per variant. It determines which fields a reservation presents and how they behave, not what is being reserved or for how much.

TRVOR.lore: No description is carried in the source. What the key tells you: it is scoped by report, company code and fiscal year, with the company code resolving through T001 — report-level control data of some kind inside FI-GL.

TSAB.desc: Standard department assignments for contact persons.
TSAB.lore: The department code assigned to a contact person — ABTNR. KNVK, the contact person master table, carries it, and it shows up again on LIPS and VBKD, so the same department tag flows into delivery and billing document items.

TSAKR.desc: Control settings for creating a G/L account with reference to an existing one.
TSAKR.lore: Controls creating a G/L account with reference to an existing one, keyed by company code and account number, with the reference company code resolving through T001. Copy-template settings for account creation.

TSTOR.desc: Accounting documents posted with a reversed (backdated) posting date.
TSTOR.lore: Accounting documents posted with a reversed, backdated posting date, keyed by company code, document and year. Company code via T001; effectively a list of documents someone will eventually want explained.

TTABS.desc: List of tables that require special maintenance procedures.
TTABS.lore: A list of tables that need special maintenance handling, keyed by table name and activity type. Metadata about maintenance behaviour — it points at other tables rather than holding data of its own.

TTXC.desc: Assignment of an external tax system to a logical RFC destination.
TTXC.lore: Points an external tax system at a logical RFC destination, per system and event. The wiring between systems, with none of the tax logic in it.

TTXD.desc: Text describing how tax jurisdiction codes are structured.
TTXD.lore: How a tax jurisdiction code is structured, keyed by calculation procedure. TTXJ holds the jurisdiction codes themselves and TTXDT the text, both hanging off the same procedure — this defines the shape they take.

TTXDT.desc: Text names for tax jurisdiction code structures.
TTXDT.lore: Names for the tax jurisdiction code structures defined in TTXD, per language. A text table sitting on top of one of the more consequential pieces of tax configuration.

TTXF1.desc: Linking of word-processing texts to document types.
TTXF1.lore: Links word-processing texts to document types, keyed by text object, text ID and document type. Says which standard text belongs with which kind of accounting document.

TTXI.desc: Index file used by the external tax interface.
TTXI.lore: The index file behind the external tax interface, keyed by the reference object triple AWTYP, AWREF and AWORG. Links interface activity back to the originating document and nothing else.

TTXJ.desc: Check table listing valid tax jurisdiction codes.
TTXJ.lore: The valid tax jurisdiction codes. It's a check table, so it exists to stop anyone typing a jurisdiction that doesn't exist. Mostly invisible until someone loads master data and it rejects half the rows.

TTXJT.desc: Text descriptions for tax jurisdiction codes.
TTXJT.lore: Descriptions of tax jurisdiction codes, keyed by language, tax calculation procedure and jurisdiction code. TTXJ holds the jurisdictions themselves.

TTXP.desc: Product codes used by the external tax interface.
TTXP.lore: Product codes used by the external tax interface, keyed by external system and code. TTXPT joins on the same external system key.

TTXPT.desc: Text descriptions for product codes used by the external tax interface.
TTXPT.lore: Language texts for the product codes used by the external tax interface, keyed by interface and code. TTXP defines the interface side; this supplies wording for codes that mostly exist to travel to an external system.

TTXU.desc: Update information for the external tax interface audit file.
TTXU.lore: Update information for the external tax interface audit file, keyed by company code, document number and year. Interface bookkeeping: it tracks what was handed over, not what was calculated.

TTXY.desc: Tax information exchanged via the external tax interface.
TTXY.lore: Tax data exchanged over the external tax interface, keyed by reference object type, reference and organisation, then item, tax code and ship-to. Interface residue: what was sent out and what came back, per line.

TTYPV.desc: Financial accounting summarization settings dependent on object type.
TTYPV.lore: Financial accounting summarization settings that depend on object type, keyed by AWTYP plus a table and field name. Because the key reaches field level, the settings are per field, per table, per object type.

TVC5.desc: Allowed statuses for sales activities, grouped by status group.
TVC5.lore: Which statuses a sales activity is allowed to take, organised by status group. TVC6 defines the group; this enumerates the permitted values inside it.

TVC6.desc: Status groups used to categorize sales activity statuses.
TVC6.lore: Status groups for sales activities — the grouping layer sitting above the individual activity statuses in TVC5. Config, one level up from the thing being configured.

TVCPF.desc: Copy control settings for billing documents.
TVCPF.lore: Copy control for billing documents, keyed by the target billing type together with the source order, delivery and billing types plus item category. The key is the rule: whether a copy is permitted at all is decided by that combination.

TVFKD.desc: Billing dates assigned by number range for billing documents.
TVFKD.lore: Billing dates assigned by number range for billing documents, keyed by the number range interval alone. A tiny config table with no relationships modelled around it.

TVFS.desc: Reason codes for blocking billing documents.
TVFS.lore: Reason codes for blocking a billing document (FAKSP), referenced from the sales order header (VBAK) down to the delivery item (LIPS). When an invoice won't generate, this is often the code sitting in the way.

TVFST.desc: Text descriptions for billing block reason codes.
TVFST.lore: Language-dependent texts for billing block reasons. TVFS defines the blocks; this is the reason someone reads on screen when the invoice will not go out.

TVKNT.desc: Text descriptions for transportation connection points on a route.
TVKNT.lore: Language-dependent texts for transportation connection points on a route, keyed by language and node. Names only; the network the nodes belong to is defined elsewhere.

TVKT.desc: Account assignment groups assigned to customers, used in billing.
TVKT.lore: The account assignment group sitting on a customer's sales record — it's what tells billing which G/L accounts a sale should hit. KNVV (customer sales data) and VBRK (billing header) both point back to it.

TVKTT.desc: Text descriptions for customer account determination groups.
TVKTT.lore: Language-dependent names for customer account determination groups, keyed by language and group. TVKT holds the group itself.

TVPT.desc: Item categories used in sales documents.
TVPT.lore: Item categories for sales documents (PSTYV) — the SD counterpart to purchasing's item category tables. Referenced from the sales order item (VBAP) down to handling unit tables (VEKP, VEPO), it decides how a line flows through pricing and delivery.

TVSMT.desc: Text descriptions for material statistics groups.
TVSMT.lore: Language-dependent names for material statistics groups. Text only, with no related tables recorded, so the group it names is defined somewhere outside this entry.

TWLAD.desc: Rules for determining an address from plant and storage location.
TWLAD.lore: Rules for determining an address from plant and storage location, with a sequence number so that more than one can apply. T001W and T001L supply the two organisational halves of the key it works from.

TWPR.desc: Profile settings for value-based inventory management.
TWPR.lore: Profile settings for value-based inventory management, keyed by the profile alone. It defines a mode of working with stock in values rather than quantities, and little else lives here.

UKPF.desc: Header data for retail inventory revaluation documents.
UKPF.lore: Header of a retail inventory revaluation document, keyed by document number and year. The document identity, with the revalued items sitting at a level below.

UKSD.desc: Control totals maintained by users during online processing.
UKSD.lore: Control totals a user maintains during online processing, keyed by user name. Currency via TCURC — a personal running check rather than a system-wide balance.

UKSV.desc: Control totals maintained by users during update (batch) processing.
UKSV.lore: Control totals kept per user during update processing, keyed by user name. A reconciliation aid: what a user recorded as expected, to be checked against what the update run actually did.

UMSV.desc: Tax balances broken down by company code, tax code, and processing key.
UMSV.lore: Tax balances accumulated by company code, country, tax code and processing key for a given run identifier, split by debit/credit indicator. Run-scoped totals for tax reporting rather than a permanent balance.

UMSVZ.desc: Advance return data for sales/purchase tax by period.
UMSVZ.lore: Advance return data for sales and purchase tax, keyed by run date and run identification — the same run-key pattern the periodic FI programs use. Output of a periodic run rather than transactional data in its own right.

USEG.desc: Segment data for retail inventory revaluation documents.
USEG.lore: Segments of a retail inventory revaluation document, keyed by revaluation document, year and line. Its related tables supply the full valuation context: material, valuation type via MBEW, company code, plant, storage location and currency.

USER_ADDR.desc: Address and contact details for system users, such as name, phone extension, department, and room, one row per user.
USER_ADDR.lore: User address and contact details — name, extension, department, room, building, cost centre — one row per user name, with matchcode fields alongside for searching. Personnel-adjacent data about system users rather than about employees.

VAKPA.desc: Sales document index of orders organized by partner function, one row per customer, partner function, and sales order.
VAKPA.lore: A secondary index over sales documents organized by partner function, so a customer's orders can be reached in whichever role they hold. Very wide key: org levels, dates, order type and reference all sit ahead of the document number.

VAPMA.desc: Sales document index of order items organized by material, one row per material, sales order, and item.
VAPMA.lore: Sales order items indexed by material, with organisational data, customer, purchase order number and creator all baked into the key ahead of the sales document item. A secondary access path, not a source of order data.

VBEPDG.desc: Appendix data table holding dangerous goods information.

VBKA.lore: The sales activity itself, one row per activity document number. TVKO supplies the sales organization, and VGBEL links back through VBUK to whatever preceded the activity.

VBKPF.desc: Header data for preliminary (parked) posting documents.
VBKPF.lore: The header for a parked posting document — a draft that hasn't posted yet, one step before it becomes a real BKPF/BSEG pair. The four VBSEG* line-item tables hang off it, mirroring how BSEG hangs off BKPF.

VBOX.desc: Index linking billing documents to rebate condition records, one row per condition table, condition key, billing document, and item.
VBOX.lore: The index linking billing documents to rebate condition records: condition table, condition key, then the billing document and item, with a settlement-relevant date sitting in between. It exists so rebate processing can get from a condition to its documents.

VBRL.desc: Invoice list data for sales documents.
VBRL.lore: Invoice list data, keyed by document and item. KNA1 supplies the customer and VBUK the status of the underlying billing document — this is the layer that bundles billing documents together rather than a billing document itself.

VBSEC.desc: Document segment holding one-time customer or vendor data for preliminary postings.
