VBSEC.lore: The one-time account data segment for preliminary postings, keyed by source company code, document, year and item. Where name and address go when the customer or vendor does not warrant a master record.

VBSEGA.desc: Document segment for asset line items in preliminary postings.
VBSEGA.lore: The asset line item segment of preliminary postings, keyed by company code, document, year and line key. Its header is VBKPF; company code resolves via T001.

VBSEGD.desc: Document segment for customer line items in preliminary postings.
VBSEGD.lore: Customer line items of preliminary postings, keyed by document, year and line within the source company code AUSBK. VBKPF holds the preliminary document header and T053R supplies the reason code.

VBSEGK.desc: Document segment for vendor line items in preliminary postings.
VBSEGK.lore: Vendor line items of a preliminary (parked) document, keyed by company code, document, year and line key. VBKPF is its header; the structure mirrors a posted vendor line without being one.

VBSEGS.desc: Document segment for G/L account line items in preliminary postings.
VBSEGS.lore: The G/L account line items of a preliminary posting, keyed by the source company code, document, fiscal year and a line key. VBKPF carries the preliminary header, T053R the reason code, T001 the company code.

VBSET.desc: Document segment for tax line items in preliminary postings.
VBSET.lore: The tax line segment for preliminary FI postings, keyed by source company code, document, year and a line key. The preliminary counterpart to the tax segment of a document that has actually been posted.

VBWF01.desc: Release data dependent on company code, used in the release approval workflow.
VBWF01.lore: Release workflow variant data (WFVAR), scoped per company code — part of the document release approval chain alongside VBWF02 through VBWF16. If a document is stuck waiting on approval, this family of tables is where the workflow variant that governs it lives.

VBWF02.desc: Workflow procedures used for document release.
VBWF02.lore: Workflow procedures for document release, keyed by language and workflow variant. VBWF01 defines the variant; the language field in the key marks this as the descriptive side.

VBWF03.desc: Approval paths used in the document release workflow.
VBWF03.lore: Approval paths (FRWEG) for the document release workflow — the routing that decides which approver sees a document next. Part of the same VBWF* family as VBWF01, and referenced directly from the release header table VBKPF.

VBWF05.desc: Rules for determining the authorization path in the release workflow.
VBWF05.lore: Rules that determine the authorization path in the release workflow, keyed by workflow variant, document type and release group. Variant via VBWF01, path via VBWF03, group via VBWF08, document type via T003.

VBWF06.desc: Rules for determining subworkflows in the release process.
VBWF06.lore: Rules that pick a subworkflow in the release process, keyed by workflow variant, release path and the amount limit HWBIS. VBWF01 defines the variant and VBWF03 the path; this row says which subworkflow applies up to that amount.

VBWF07.desc: Approval groups used in the document release workflow.
VBWF07.lore: Approval groups used in the document release workflow, keyed by language and group — so this is the naming side, with VBWF08 holding the group itself.

VBWF08.desc: Assignment data for release approval groups used in the document release workflow.
VBWF08.lore: Release approval group assignments (FRGRP) in the document release workflow — who belongs to which approval group. Referenced from customer and vendor master data (KNB1, LFB1) as well as other VBWF* tables, tying people to the approval steps they're allowed to clear.

VBWF09.desc: People authorized to release preliminary postings.
VBWF09.lore: Who may release a preliminary posting, keyed by workflow variant, release path, level and an amount limit. Having the amount in the key is what makes release authority graduated rather than a simple yes or no.

VBWF10.desc: Text descriptions for release authorization paths.
VBWF10.lore: Descriptions for the release authorization paths defined in VBWF03, per language. A text table for a workflow-adjacent corner of FI configuration, carrying no meaning of its own beyond the label.

VBWF11.desc: Objects that require full release in the release workflow.
VBWF11.lore: Objects requiring full release within a release workflow, keyed by workflow variant and an object key resolving through VBWF12. Distinguishes what must be approved outright from what may pass with less.

VBWF12.desc: Text descriptions for objects requiring full release.
VBWF12.lore: Texts for objects that require full release, keyed by language, workflow variant and object. Variant via VBWF01, the object itself via VBWF11 — descriptions only.

VBWF15.desc: Additional assignment of people authorized to release preliminary postings.
VBWF15.lore: Additional assignment of people authorised to release preliminary postings, keyed by workflow variant, release path, release level and amount limit. The same variant and path skeleton as the rest of the release configuration, one level deeper.

VBWF16.desc: Document types relevant for the payment release process.
VBWF16.lore: Which document types are relevant for the payment release process, per workflow variant. T003 defines the document type, VBWF01 the variant that scopes the rule.

VEDG.desc: Supplementary dangerous goods data at the handling unit level.

VEKP_EHS_DG_APPEND.desc: Additional data for Class 7 (radioactive) dangerous goods.

VKAOF.lore: An index of sales activities still open, with the whole sales organisation hierarchy, activity type, customer, date, creator and document all sitting in the key. Everything worth filtering on is in the key, because that is all an index is.

VKAWV.desc: Index of sales activities organized by follow-up date.
VKAWV.lore: An index over sales activities ordered by follow-up date — the date sits at the front of the key, ahead of the whole sales-area, activity-type and customer breakdown. Built for what is due next, not for storing the activity itself.

VLKPA.desc: Sales and distribution index of deliveries organized by partner function, one row per customer, partner function, and delivery.
VLKPA.lore: A partner-function index over deliveries: customer, partner function, then shipping point, delivery date, sales organization, delivery type, the various customer roles and finally the delivery number. Built for finding deliveries by who was involved.

VLPMA.desc: Sales and distribution index of delivery items organized by material, one row per material, delivery, and item.
VLPMA.lore: A material-driven secondary index over delivery items: reach a delivery by what was on it rather than by document number. Wide key covering shipping point, delivery date, sales organization, delivery type and the customers involved.

VRKPA.desc: Index of billing documents organized by partner function.
VRKPA.lore: Billing documents indexed by partner function — given a partner and role, find the billing documents. Sales organisation, billing type, billing date and payer sit in the key alongside the document number.

VRPMA.desc: Index of billing document items organized by material.
VRPMA.lore: A billing document index led by material, then sales organization, billing date and onward to the billing document and item. Built for material-driven lookups rather than document-driven ones.

WBASSOC.desc: Global Trade Services table linking source and target documents across process steps, such as requirement and activity documents.
WBASSOC.lore: A generic association table linking a source document step to a target document step — requirement to activity and similar — with both sides carried at document, item and sub-item level. Process-flow wiring rather than business content.

WCOCOF.desc: Document flow linking condition contracts to their settlement documents, one row per condition item and step.
WCOCOF.lore: Document flow for condition contracts: which settlement documents came out of which condition item and step. Keyed by condition record, item, step and counter — a link table rather than a document in its own right.

WDFR1.desc: Requirements planning data for perishable goods.
WDFR1.lore: Requirements planning data for perishable goods, keyed only by client, with the actual dimensions arriving through its relations — location via KNA1, material via MARA, plant via T001W, purchasing group via T024. Short shelf life, wide reach.

WDFR_DISP.desc: Header data for perishables requirements planning.
WDFR_DISP.lore: Header data for perishables requirements planning, keyed by a single document number but well connected outward — customer via KNA1, vendor via LFA1, company code via T001, plant and storage location via T001W and T001L, purchasing org and group via T024E and T024.

WDFR_POS.desc: Item-level data for perishables requirements planning.
WDFR_POS.lore: Item level of perishables requirements planning, keyed by document, item and two further item qualifiers. Its links span customer location, vendor, material, company code, plant, country, tax code and purchasing document type: planning that has to know a lot at once.

WIND.desc: Index of condition records used for automatic document adjustment (mass pricing updates), one row per document type, condition record, and item.
WIND.lore: An index of condition records caught up in automatic document adjustment, keyed by document type, condition record number and condition item. It tells a mass pricing update which records to revisit.

WITH_CTNCL.desc: Numbering classes used for withholding tax certificate numbers.
WITH_CTNCL.lore: Numbering classes for withholding tax certificates — the scheme controlling how certificate numbers get assigned. Small config table, but the whole WITH_CTNCO/WITH_CTNUM numbering chain hangs off the class defined here.

WITH_CTNCLT.desc: Text descriptions for withholding tax certificate numbering classes.
WITH_CTNCLT.lore: Language texts for withholding tax certificate numbering classes. WITH_CTNCL defines the classes; this adds nothing but the description, which is the usual division of labour across this configuration area.

WITH_CTNCO1.desc: Configuration for withholding tax certificate numbering concept 1.
WITH_CTNCO1.lore: Configuration for one of the withholding tax certificate numbering concepts, keyed by company code. WITH_CTNCL supplies the numbering class; the numeral in the name is doing real work, since it identifies which concept this is.

WITH_CTNCO2.desc: Configuration for withholding tax certificate numbering concept 2.
WITH_CTNCO2.lore: Configuration for the second withholding tax certificate numbering concept, keyed by company code and withholding tax type, with the numbering class resolving through WITH_CTNCL. Country-driven certificate numbering, kept separate from the first concept.

WITH_CTNCO3.desc: Configuration for withholding tax certificate numbering concept 3.
WITH_CTNCO3.lore: Configuration for withholding tax certificate numbering under concept 3, keyed by company code, branch and withholding tax type. Number class via WITH_CTNCL, tax type via T059P.

WITH_CTNGR.desc: Numbering groups used for withholding tax certificate numbers.
WITH_CTNGR.lore: Numbering groups for withholding tax certificate numbers, keyed by CTNOGRP. WITH_CTNUM holds the numbers issued under the group and WITH_CTNGRT the description — this is just the bucket they hang from.

WITH_CTNGRT.desc: Text descriptions for withholding tax certificate numbering groups.
WITH_CTNGRT.lore: Text descriptions for withholding tax certificate numbering groups, by language. WITH_CTNGR holds the group; this supplies the wording.

WITH_CTNUM.desc: Assignment of numbering groups to numbering classes for withholding tax certificates.
WITH_CTNUM.lore: Assignment of a numbering group to a certificate numbering class per company code. It is the join that turns a class definition in WITH_CTNCL into an actual number source in WITH_CTNGR for a given company code.

WITH_ITEM.desc: Withholding tax data recorded per withholding tax type and FI document line item.
WITH_ITEM.lore: Withholding tax recorded against an individual FI document line, one row per line and withholding tax type. Type comes from T059P, code from T059Z, recipient type from T059C — the whole withholding configuration converges here.

WRF_PSCD_DLHD.desc: Header data for a purchasing date line.
WRF_PSCD_DLHD.lore: Header of a purchasing date line, keyed by a single date line identifier. WRF_PSCD_DLIT and its shadow counterpart both hang items from that identifier, making this the anchor for both.

WRF_PSCD_DLIT.desc: Individual dates within a purchasing date line.
WRF_PSCD_DLIT.lore: The individual dates within a purchasing date line, keyed by the line ID and a date ID. Its header is WRF_PSCD_DLHD; this is the item level and nothing beyond it.

WRF_PSCD_DLIT_SH.desc: Shadow (staging) table for dates within a purchasing date line.
WRF_PSCD_DLIT_SH.lore: Shadow copy of dates on a purchasing date line, keyed by date line and date plus a timestamp, user name and program. Those extra key fields are what make it a staging and audit layer rather than the live data; WRF_PSCD_DLHD holds the header.

WRF_STOCK_SITUAT.desc: Current stock situation data for inventory management.
WRF_STOCK_SITUAT.lore: Current stock situation per plant, material and a period flag. A prepared view of stock rather than the movement or valuation records that produced it.

WRMA_EVALPER.desc: Completed evaluation periods for returns material authorization (RMA) processing.
WRMA_EVALPER.lore: Completed evaluation periods for returns material authorisation, keyed by plant, fiscal year variant, period and inventory date. A record of which periods have already been evaluated, with T001W and T009 supplying the context.

WTAD.desc: Cumulative balance values for accounts receivable.
WTAD.lore: Cumulative balances per company code, customer, calendar year, withholding tax type and code. Accumulated by year rather than by document, with KNA1 supplying the customer and T001 the company code.

WTADP.desc: Cumulative accounts receivable values used in payment proposal processing.
WTADP.lore: Cumulative receivable values used during payment proposal processing, keyed by company code, customer, calendar year, withholding tax type and code, plus the payment run date and identifier. Running totals scoped to a specific proposal run.

WTADPN.desc: Supplementary cumulative accounts receivable values used in payment proposal processing.
WTADPN.lore: Supplementary cumulative accounts receivable values used while a payment proposal is being built, keyed by the run date and identifier, company code, customer, the proposal document and the withholding tax details.

WTAK.desc: Cumulative balance values for accounts payable.
WTAK.lore: Cumulative balance values in Accounts Payable, keyed by company code, vendor, calendar year, withholding tax type and withholding tax code. Totals per vendor and tax code rather than per document; LFA1 and T001 supply the master data.

WTAKP.desc: Cumulative accounts payable values used in payment proposal processing.
WTAKP.lore: Cumulative accounts payable values per vendor, company code, withholding tax type and code for a given payment run. An accumulator used while a payment proposal is being worked out.

WTAKPN.desc: Supplementary cumulative accounts payable values used in payment proposal processing.
WTAKPN.lore: Cumulative withholding-relevant payables figures used while a payment proposal is built, keyed by run date and identification, company code, vendor, payment document, calendar year, withholding tax type and code. Working data of a payment run.
